Oct. 20th, 2015 (Part III: Real stuff, Real Life)
Been drinking this recenly along with spring water sources: [https://drinkrealwater.com/real-science/](https://drinkrealwater.com/real-science/)
But more important, my 6-yo son loves that water and I've been having him drink it continually for the past 2 months now after he's switched away from bottled carbonated water. I've noticed remarkable improvements in his health with way better water, blue blocking in the evening, and all of the game mitigation I've done with RF-EMF hardwiring the controllers and magnetic EMF reduction like turning off the rumble in the controllers. The only thing left my son really needs is morning sunlight. What's amazing is that his school has 3 Recess breaks in the day, so he's getting a bunch and he knows the benefit of the sun too. I've been having him wear long-sleeve shirts though due to the fluorescent lights and the Wifi, and sent again another email to their principal showing the latest push to the Feds to get WiFi reduced in schools. They are in an 'ignoring state' with me for sure after my conversation last month. It will be an uphill battle it's appearing, but the war will be won someday.

### Scompy — 2015-10-21T23:10:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-180095>

Oct. 21st, 2015 (The trees have been talking to me)

**The Boxtheory of The Tree (by Scompy)**

_It's fall again. Most of those crazy walkers have forgotten why they named the season after me in the first place. It's what I do so well right now, drop my leaves--besides sticking to one place. Some of those silly _walkers_ think I eat the earth for energy too. They should know my leaves eat from the sky just like their skin does. Don't get me wrong, the soil is kind of like their food, but at the core, light is life.

A good summer means a good winter for me. This time of year that I really like to suck every leaf dry, wee hee hee! A few walkers understand how the rainbow works within... my leaves turn from green to yellow to orange to red. I have to admit, I slow down the light for my benefit. Eating redlight and tossing away the green is so great in the summer, and so tastey I might add.

Now I'm going to speak my nut. Those boxes are in control of the walkers, I'm sure of it! I think I've figured it out. They tell the walkers what to do, where to go and how to live. I'm suspecting the little boxes get the walkers to use bigger boxes for complete boxiphilia.

On a typical day, I usually see a walker wake up and feel compelled to talk on a little box, then jump into bigger box to move faster. While they are in the big box, they continaully get instructions from the little box that's in control. I haven't worked out why a lot of big boxes end up on the same path at the same time, resulting in moving slower overall compared to not being in the bigger box at all. Eventually, the walkers end up in cubical boxes for most of the day depriving themselves of the true Lifelight. I'm thinking the small boxes get them to work under phantom light to maintain box-control via exhaustion. Once the walkers have gotten tired enough, the little boxes command them to get back into a bigger box to go back to the huge box at the end of the day that they live in.

It's clear to me that the huge box acts as the housing for all of the smaller boxes. After sunset, the small box controllers tell them to turn on phantom lights to make them even more exhausted to retain their control. If the walkers don't comply by turning on the phantom lights, the small box assissts with its own phantom light. When a walker takes out the evening meal from cooling box, the walker often heats it up in another box and then eats in front of larger flat-box. Box-images on the flat-box appear to be entrancing.

Meanwhile, some walkers that are yet to be fully exhausted play on gameboxes. Popular gamebox activity I've called Boxcraft. The activity allows the walker to recreate our real-world into a series of boxes, using characters that are also represented by boxes. It's all quite ingeneous actually. The walkers end their day _going to sleep on top of rectangular boxes, after checking to see if any important boxes had previously arrived on the porch or in that small box just on the outside of the big box._

I've come to realize that if I were shaped like a box, I think they'd notice me again. And wooo-weee, so amazing to me they cannot see the energy from those boxes into the sky. They just are not aware how they are controlled. Rarely do they ever bother to look up anymore. _I have to admit I still prefer the walkers to get really close to me, like breathe on me whenever they can--at least, t_hat's how it used to be a few hundred years ago when I was younger. _It was more of a relationship they seem to have forgotten now.___ Strange that still a few of them look up at times. I actually miss the old days._

### Scompy — 2015-10-22T23:35:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-180194>

**Oct. 22nd, 2015**

**The Misinformed Willocks (An Aesop's Sort of Tale by Scompy)**
_<This is my version of an Aesop's Fable for the 21st Century>_

On a distant planet called Trelefles, a sentient form of life known as the Willocks were the dominant people of the lands. Very much like our trees of Earth and like Treants in earth legends, the Willocks had roots and prefered to soak up the sun in softer soil. The typical Willock lived a few thousand years. It was a good, long life. With new and wonderful advancements in technology, they developed the ability to _phase_ from one location to another for mobility!

It took a few hundred years to master _phasing_ to specific locations. But it was worth it... Willocks could move around freely from one location to the next and this started a great trade worldwide for exchanging commodities, especially unique types of soil. At first, some of the weaker Willocks realized just after any phasing event took place, great exhaustion would set-in and their leaves would fall off to make up for the lost energy--even before autumn! The technological specialists of the time told the Willocks that phasing into richer soil would help to prevent the energy loss.

Over the next 500 years, phasing became so intimately interwoven into society that there was an unspoken attitude of negativity work any Willock that preferred to stay in one place for any longer period of time. The main phrase sentiment was, "**_If you don't like to phase, you must be craze._**"

As the tade-industry grew, so did the buildings and skyscrapers. Harder dirt and stones were needed to secure these structures, so the worker-Willocks often found themselves phasing into places that were not as pleasant as they thought it should be. Under closer investigation and looking at census data, it was determined that the average lifespan of a Willock was now 900 years. It was just at this time the Herbologist Council was formed to help society with their growing ills.

But even with their shorter lives, the Willocks liked their lives and mobility. They even got used to the harder soils and windowed-buildings. And after a few more hundred years, Willock parents were giving birth to Willock children within these hubs. It became harder to seed and sprout their children in the trade hubs, but some of them were lucky enough, for whatever reason, to grow their little sprouts to maturity. The Herbologists once again suspected it was due to having access to better soils and getting in enough daily phasing.

In those concentrated zones of trade, the larger buildings blocked the light and work was hampered. Fortunately, technology saved the day once again and Suncubes were invented. Suncubes radiated a different kind of light that mimicked sunlight so the Willocks no longer needed to have small buildings with large windows. The construction trade boomed because of the Suncube and new designs were set into motion. Buildings could be made much taller and deeper, windows could be reduced in size and speciality soils could be phased into them to optimize efficiency in the workforce.

During the gradual implementation of the new Suncubes and buildings, the Herbologists continued to stay focused on finding and making excellent soils for the Willock society. Strangely enough, even those within the Willock Council came down ill with diseases as well, but realized that due to the quality and scarcity of the soils, they were being afflicted too. Nearly all Willocks suffered much shorter lifespans and came down with strange diseases never seen before. The Herbologists came to the rescue and realized that pouring more water into the hardened earth would help things out a great deal. But over time, the soils became more depleted of minerals, even with the added water.

Most Herbologists suggested manipulating the soil as much as possible--a new, elite group of Soil-Phasers were born. Their mission goal would be to phase to remote locations and find the best soils from around the world. Technologies advanced to the point of amazing soils that appeared to have slight benefits, even though they often caused side-effects. These so-called pharmisoils were kind of a hit-and-miss depending on who got them. Nonetheless, the Willock society used them and found themselves going to work to produce better products for growing commerce and making more soil-related products to benefit the masses. Evem though many Willock workers often complained about getting sick working underground or in buildings with Suncubes, the Herbologists voted and agreed that they needed to raise their efforts for better soils to the next level.

More spiffy and wondrous inventions soon arrived, including communication and telecom devices that increased the speed of commerce tenfold. Over this period of time, as more communications increased and everyone had access to phasing or communicating to any Willock at a moment's notice, some Willocks noticed that the natural sunlight looked quite dimmed than normal. A smart Herbologist contacted the Sunlookers to see if the sun itself was changing. The Sunlookers lived in a far, remote location that required several phasings to travel, as their observatories were far away from the trade hubs. The Sunlookers told the Herbologist that the sun appeared totally unchanged for more than 10,000 years of recordkeeping and is still the same.

When the Herbologist looked up into the sky, he too noticed there were no visual distortions in this location. When observing the Sunlookers, not only were extremely ancient and healthy with massive branches and roots, they seemed to have patterns of getting out into real sunlight and stayed rooted for longer times. The Herbologist scratched his head and asked, "I see you consuming the soil your roots are in right now, is that a special kind of soil that's only found in this part of the world?"

The Sunlookers responsed, "You've been barking up the wrong tree for so long that you've forgotten your main medicine--real sunlight. Here in this part of the world, your techology doesn't distort the skies and slows down the light. Here, we live with the sun not under a fake one. Soil is not even the second-most important thing for us. Water is. Soil is fine and all, but you only need a little for a great, long life. Connecting to the ground is just as important as the soil itself."

The Herbologist immediately dismissed the Sunlookers as **craze** because of their lifestyles. Something so simple as getting daily sunlight couldn't have been overlooked for so long. Technology was far more important anyway for mobility and communications, plus, everyone relied on the soil trade for commerce and health.

The Herbologist returned back to his life in the trade hub and developed a leaf-disease a few years later. But he remembered about what the Sunlookers told him about the light. Instead of asking for the normal pharmisoil treatments, he decided to take a risk and go against the grain of society. He didn't tell anyone else where he was going, but after leaving on a long vacation and staying with the Sunlookers for a few months and living as they lived, his disease vanished as if it were pure magic.

A year later, the Herbologist felt a moral obligation to return to the trade hub and tell everyone about why they were dying off earlier than they should be. But it didn't take long for that Herboligst to be labeled a **craze** for suggesting such things... to suggest changing the status-quo. Even though most dismissed his beliefs, it was his closest friends and relatives saw his disease cured--enough to plant the seed of doubt about the Herbologist Council and their mainstream practices to cure disease.

### Scompy — 2015-10-23T22:58:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-180305>

**Oct. 23rd, 2015 (EMF Testing)
**
Now I'm ultra-curious about: **"What happens to microwave and Radio-frequency inside of another electric or magnetic field?"**

I'm going to test myself with my meters at home this weekend. I'll have to get a more steady RF source because RF bounces around so much. I'm thinking my video game console output is perfect because about a foot away, I get pretty continual readings, and then put the console in a strong magnetic field next to my electric shavor while it's on to see the field falloff.

_**What I cannot test is specifically, "Is there an unforeseen compound or synergistic influence when a human is subjected to BOTH types of EMF at the same time?"**_

Here is why I'm wondering about synergy: I'll leave it at this...I know two people that work in a strong electrical field generated by a transistor right outside their window where they sit, and I've seen these two talking on cell phones to the ear in that electrical field. I notice Person-A gets sick all of the time and Person-B actively has cancer. This is obviously a double-whammy, but I'm wondering if 1+1 doesn't =2, but 1+1 =20.

### Scompy — 2015-10-25T04:23:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-180389>

**Oct. 24th, 2015 (EMF Testing Results)**
Mostly negative, but with one possible finding.

Using my cornet, I setup my Xbox360 to radiate around 0.11 to 0.13 milliWatts/Square meter in RF-EMF. I then turned on my razor that has a high magnetic EMF. I turned on/off the Razor at times at different locations around the meter, but I basically was only mitigating the RF-EMF from the Xbox because of the physical object in the way at different positions. There didn't seem to be an RF-EMF increase, though there was just more fluxuations in the RFs.

My next test was the reverse. I was looking for higher magnetic EMF from my razor when I subjected it to the RF-EMF from the Xbox360. My test was specifically plugging in the Xbox, having the razor on and stable, then unplugging the Xbox to see if the milliGauss on the meter decreased or increased. What was interesting there is that it appeared I may have gotten a slight increase overall when I had both the Xbox on and the razor on, but maybe too small to say for sure. The Wahl razor outputs up to 20 - 25 milliGauss when you hold it within an inch of the Cornet's sensor, so it's easy to put my Cornet beyond the yellow into the red. I basically got the same readings on the milliGauss, but at times, it looked like I might have gotten slight increases/decreases at times (like +/-0.0 to 1.0 milligauss), but it was more about the stability of the devices. This is one I'm curious about now because it might suggest that at the moment an RF-EMF strikes the magnetic EMF, there could be an interaction but it's probably wishful thinking I've found something. For now, I'm going to chalk it up to user-variance because of the wiggling of holding the Razor in my hand and not getting stable reading. Now I'm going to re-test this one later tonight or tomorrow with making sure the meter and razor are totally stable, and then unplug the Xbox on/off/on/off and see what I get. Kind of interesting nonetheless.

### Scompy — 2015-10-31T23:39:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-180858>

**Oct. 31st, 2015 Pt.2 (Grounding for All!)**

After reading a bit elsewhere on surfaces to ground on and if asphalt works, one guy said this...

\_\_\_
**The Best Surfaces for Grounding**
Clearly, the simplest way to ground is to walk barefoot outside when safe to do so. But what about urban or city dwellers who are surrounded by asphalt and concrete? Can you ground on those? And what about natural surfaces—which ones are the most effective? There are indeed significant differences between various surfaces.

The ideal location for walking barefoot is the beach, close to or in the water, as sea water is a great conductor. Your body also contains mostly water, so it creates a good connection.

A close second would be a grassy area, especially if it's covered with dew, which is what you'd find if you walk early in the morning. According to Dr. Oschman, concrete is a good conductor as long as it hasn't been sealed. Painted concrete does not allow electrons to pass through very well. Materials like **asphalt**, wood, and typical insulators like plastic or the soles of your shoes, will not allow electrons to pass through and are not suitable for barefoot grounding.
\_\_\_

Just was I suspected about asphalt. I've been designing a make-shift grounding anklet in my freetime. Will release my design here in text. I've bought some copper braiding. I want to loop this around so it can attach to the bottom of a shoe, but then come up the ankles and secure around it to touch the skin like an ankle cuff. So it can be a thing a person can easily take off and put back on a shoe or slipper that has rubber to get a grounding-influence when going out, but still wanting to wear whatever shoes are desired. I'm gonna prototype it real life in the coming days, so I'll post what I get. Maybe if it's really effective, I'll Kickerstarter it.

**November 2nd, 2015 (Sword Fencing Experience)**
In my undergraduate years at Oregon State, I was taught Sabre fencing by a Chinese Olympic master. That experience pushed me into being Treasurer during my Junior year and President of the club in my Senior. When we practiced, it would always be with normal equipment, but now I realized when we had competitions, I felt very wornout/drained faster than practices. At that time, I thought it was due to the added stress and excitement of the competitions. Now I'm thinking it may have been much more... nearly all competitions use circuits to judge points in competitions. Typical fencing salles have 8+ fencing strips all with electric circuits in close proximity to everyone. At some point, I'll get to a Salle again and take some EMF measurements. Really making me raise an eyebrow what type of exposure is going on.

[http://www.leonpaulusa.com/armoury/epee.html](http://www.leonpaulusa.com/armoury/epee.html)

And really, are the protons correct today? Geesh, 71.9 and 80.3 Proton density?! I rarely have ever seen it above 100. Thinking it's gonna hit that high today.

### Scompy — 2015-11-06T18:59:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-181275>

**November 1st, 2015 (Moral Obligation?)**

Yesterday walking through a parking lot to get my lunch, I saw two new mothers holding their newborns in front-slings. That was pleasant to see, until I realized both of them were on their cells talking to different people. So the mothers were holding their phones in their hands away from their heads--perfectly placing the cell phones a few inches from their babies' heads. I shook my head knowing the damage going on, only hoping that the mothers were acting more as an insulator for their babies. But what really made me fume was that after I got my lunch 15mins later, there they were again standing around talking on their cell phones.

So here's the question...Should I have interrupted their phone calls and given them a quick public service announcement by:

1\. Interrupting them and saying, "Hi, you have a beautiful child...You also might want to go online later and research what cell phone EMFs can do to the mylen in the brain to reduce autism and other risks. Have a nice day."
2\. Or just keep walking.

Do we have a moral obligation to share knowledge to the clueless when it doesn't present itself? I'm thinking no. But if the situation does present itself, I'm thinking yes. Then it becomes up to the individual to either dismiss what was heard or take it in as a possibility, and if that nugget is seeded, take-action. In this situations, I ended up walking by because they were so involved with their cell conversations. I was hoping one of them would see me and it would be an invitation so I could tell them what they were doing.

### Scompy — 2015-11-06T19:19:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-181278>

> [thisbirdhaswings said:](/goto/post?id=181276)
>
> The answer is no.

Thanks for the reply! It's why I passed on that situation. My further thinking is that because I have to live in this world with these clueless people, then how to we get them to wake up to the hazard in front of them? To me, a cell phone is a tool. Just like a knife or axe, you have to be careful to understand how to use it most effective so it won't damage you. This is what's going on. Most people are flailing about their 'cell-axes', holding them on the brain or babies and such because they don't have a clue about how RF works.

If we don't stay silent when a situation presents itself, we can take advantage of dispensing some knowledge in passing. Let's turn the tables. If your best friend had a newborn and you saw the same, would you feel a moral obligation to tell her? What makes a friend or relative any different than a stranger? It's the opportunity to share information. This is exactly why I didn't walk up to them and say anything... the opprotunity didn't arrive. But if it had, I would have mentioned something to them in a polite way.

My additional reasonsing would be this: Is it a moral obligation to tell someone drinking from a bottle that says 'poison' on it, but the person didn't see the label? Would you let that person keep on drinking, or do you let the person know that it's possible what is inside the bottle could be toxic? It's awareness to me. If they want to keep on drinking the bottle or talking on the cell, fine by me. I tried and then I'm moving on. I'm coming from a place where I want all of my neighbors to be healthy, not just my own son or myself. I do see the harm in sharing information from a snotty or bad disposition, or forcing the information on someone.

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Temperance\_(virtue)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Temperance_\(virtue\))

Jack just had a genius answer (once again) over on the Optimal Kids channel... I'm gonna take his advice to heart and carry around the website link to Jack's main website on a business card and hand it to people if the situation arises, and to 'say nothing'! Additionally, this teaches and builds Temperance in my...that I learn to craft my words verbally with great forethought, one virtue that I've been working on as a personal character trait.

I.e., Temperance has a great deal to do with keeping one's mouth shut. There are many benefits to this approach including misrepresentation of information, turning someone off to the information via conversation, and liability upon oneself to name a few. What's even more important about that approach is it promotes self-motivation in others...To be crude, each person has to own their shit. And it's human nature to not be told what is right or wrong... it's up to the individual to discover it and then own it. This weekend, I'm gonna photoshop some weblink cards because I'm finding I'm running into these situations at least a few times a month.

### Curves — 2015-11-08T02:50:47+0200

> [Scompy said:](/goto/post?id=181275)
>
> **November 1st, 2015 (Moral Obligation?)**
>
> Yesterday walking through a parking lot to get my lunch, I saw two new mothers holding their newborns in front-slings. That was pleasant to see, until I realized both of them were on their cells talking to different people. So the mothers were holding their phones in their hands away from their heads--perfectly placing the cell phones a few inches from their babies' heads. I shook my head knowing the damage going on, only hoping that the mothers were acting more as an insulator for their babies. But what really made me fume was that after I got my lunch 15mins later, there they were again standing around talking on their cell phones.
>
> So here's the question...Should I have interrupted their phone calls and given them a quick public service announcement by:
>
> 1\. Interrupting them and saying, "Hi, you have a beautiful child...You also might want to go online later and research what cell phone EMFs can do to the mylen in the brain to reduce autism and other risks. Have a nice day."
> 2\. Or just keep walking.
>
> Do we have a moral obligation to share knowledge to the clueless when it doesn't present itself? I'm thinking no. But if the situation does present itself, I'm thinking yes. Then it becomes up to the individual to either dismiss what was heard or take it in as a possibility, and if that nugget is seeded, take-action. In this situations, I ended up walking by because they were so involved with their cell conversations. I was hoping one of them would see me and it would be an invitation so I could tell them what they were doing.

I have agonized about this myself. Especially, when I was swimming in the ocean. Mothers and fathers out with their kids on the beach, having a special day with each other, cell phone in hand next to kids head. I never said anything because for me, it was an intellectual dilemma. My intuition always told me to not interfere. If, it every tells me to do otherwise, I'll be out there sharing information in a hot New York minute.

### Scompy — 2015-11-12T04:42:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-181728>

**Oct. 11th, 2015 (Looking out for other Biohackers and Biohackers that don't know they are Biohacking)**

Today I felt like I was part of an elite group--the Secret Society of Biohacking. Here is why. I work on the third floor and really it's the 4th floor adding the garage level. I've taken measurements at my workplace making sure the stair railings are grounded, and sure enough, the entire piece is connected metal straight into the floor into the garage. For this reason, when I take the stairs up to the 3rd floor, I always make a point to hold the railing as I go up. Every little bit of grounding when possible. I've been doing this for quite awhile now.

Very few people use the stairwell. More people use the elevator. When coming up the stairs today, I saw another employee at the top of the stairs holding the metal railing at the top. There's an additional 6-foot section of railing where another person can stand and not block people coming up the stairs. I saw he was working out in that space and holding onto the railing. But when he saw me come up the stairs, it was almost as if he felt embarrassed for working-out at that location and stopped his routine. When I got to the top, he was still holding onto the rail though not letting go, even though he had stopped his exercising.

So it makes me wonder a bit about others and how many people out in the wild are trying their own biohacks. Did he know he was grounding or was he just using the railing for balance of his exercises? I'm assuming he probably didn't have a clue about the benefit of holding onto that rail. Secondly, let's assume he didn't know he was grounding. Did he feel compelled by _**instinct**_ to hold onto that railing in knowing it had benefits?

Anyway, thought I'd post that bit for today.

### Scompy — 2015-11-15T06:16:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182004>

**Nov. 14th, 2015 (Autism Insights)**
I had a conversation today with an online friend that has an autistic son. She told me that she has terrible times with her autistic son due to tactile responses of food choices and trying to get him to eat better foods rather than the unfoods the autistic child eats--over-processed, high sugar foods. I was frank with her and told her it may also be behavior and his addiction to sugar that's hard to break--that she has enabled him for the past 10+ year to eat what he prefers, though I do know it's a touchy subject. I told her about sources of RF-EMF that could be problematic and she now has an understanding that she needs a meter to know specific sources of the environment around her and her son. I let her know about many EMF hacks I've implemented including turning off cell to airplane mode, unplugging the Wifi and hardwiring it, shutting down circuit breakers at night, etc. But sad to say, I believe they live in or near an EMF hellhole--Los Angeles. Plus, I get the impression she may also live close to freeways, and if you've read Jack's blogs, there are additional consequences.

One thing came out in the conversation though... the autistic child goes ballistic twice a year when he gets his head shaved. They have to strap him down, doesn't sound pleasant. I told her about the magnetic EMF intensity that comes off of those electric hair-cutting shavers pressed straight on the brain might have something to do with it outside of the louder sound a buzzer makes. But it made me think for a moment... might the autistic kid be _**sensing**_ when he's in stronger EMF and that's why he goes ballistic?

**Nov. 14th, 2015 (Scompy's Past Intermixed with Dry Humor)**
Really, I believe the heart of her problem is Los Angeles itself in my opinion. This resonates with me because I lived in Los Angeles for 14 years and know what it is like if she were trying to mitigate and biohack. I lived in Sherman Oaks, in the heart of Pasadena, and SouthPas. I also worked in Marina Del Rey, Santa Monica, Venice Beach and Pasadena. You really cannot escape cell towers and the like; good pockets are few and far between. Most of my migraines and calcium effluxing in that time of my life happened to and from work on freeways. I chalked it up to stress at that time, not having a clue about the EMF around me. I often had behaviors of playing video games until midnight to 3am and waking up around 9am to get to work by 10am. Did I ever roll down my window in the morning? Rarely because of smog and air quality. I additionally had problems I was unaware of being ungrounded continually working on the 12th floor and living off the ground too. The most progress I ever made was running early mornings around the Rose Bowl in shorts, and doing wind-sprints in the morning in a park. I chalked it all up to exercising at the time, like a cotton-headed-ninny-muggins (I thought I'd throw that reference here as December is almost upon us), instead of understanding the benefit of waking up with the sun, getting out into the sun and going to sleep a few hours after the sun had set.

But by about 2006, I knew it wasn't the exercise at all that was benefitting me... And I understood it wasn't really the food either. At work, I did some Instanty and P90X working out in a dark room on the 12 floor in the heart of EMF with other co-workers. So what happened to me 2-months of doing this? I gained 15+ pounds of fat, felt terrible, had horrible sleep, was really moody and then told my co-workers I was done because the joints in my body felt like they were about to snap. I also had a weird 'dried out' feeling throughout my body, and at night, I'd get weird tingles in places where the sun doesn't shine often enough. (For us mitigators of sunlight, we have it shine _**there**_ a lot more these days.)

I was a mess and didn't know why. So trial and error, I kept doing the things that worked and kept a mental list of big fails like this one. I found out that playing soccer, volleyball or running over lunchtime OUTSIDE was beneficial for me, but playing indoor soccer under fluorescent lights often resulted in heart palpatations an hour later after the workout. I am a stickler for attempting to draw connections and correlations that might point to a causation, but I'm skeptical enough to know never to jump to any conclusion without collecting enough data and even with the data, not necessarily making assumptions entirely.

So when looking at my past and adding this all up, I'm drawing a very strong correlation that my body does extremely well when I am outside under real sunlight, and even better in the mornings. Working out is maintenance and required, but not all it's cracked up to be for health. Food is the same way... (hold DHA because when I began slamming the DHA in my system, my entire 'dry feeling' inside my body is gone and was a cure for me). Having run a great deal in my life and a marathon in 2002, I now look as running and constant-motion exercises as **wasted time and junk**. Anaerobic has been by far the best for me...to maintain muscle mass, have physical definition and feel stronger all of the time and not worn-down. Plus, you don't need a gym membership to do any exercise whatsoever.

Ladies, listen up. The reason why many single men are going to the gym at 8-10pm is usually more motivated by running into women and sex-drive than personal health. Health might come in 2nd place at best. It's exactly the scene from the movie Grease when John Travolta tries out for sports to impress Olivia Newton John. Sex-drive often drives people into bad situations for environment... chatting on cell phones for hours on end, endless texting, staying up all night at a party, and going to the gym to workout under fluorescent lights on a stationary bike to crash melatonin and potentially feed depression.

### Scompy — 2015-11-16T01:12:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182047>

**Nov. 15th, 2015 (Controller Surgery Success)**

Controllers can live and function just fine without their rumblers. So today, I showed how to rip out rumble packs inside of the controllers to hack/mitigate the magnetic EMF when they vibrate. Vibration is excessive in most games. It's typicqal for many games have rumble going on every 5-10 seconds, and the rumble can be subtle-intense based on the action performed. There's some mitigation that a person can do in the settings of the hardware and per game, but this is ONLY for Player 1 in most games. Typically, Player 2, 3 or 4 is screwed because when Vibration is shut down in the options, it's common to still get vibration happening.

### Scompy — 2015-11-17T07:01:59+0200

****Nov.** 16th, 2015 (Seeing what we cannot see)**

It makes me wonder a lot...the push for Wifi everywhere. One needs to ask the question: does an EMF or Wifi camera exist, and if it does, who is using it, for what purposes and to what ends? The article above trivializes the use of a microwave camera as if there is no impact on our human body: that the camera can be setup anywhere in the 'name of safety' and on all of the time. Good safety = bad health and shorter lives as the result of such idiocracy.

### Scompy — 2015-11-19T07:32:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182310>

****Nov.** 18th, 2015 (More Biohacking)**

So I did some in-depth tests with my stetzerizers and found some issues. I've got 2 computer desks side by side in my office and one of the computers is close to a wall. On that wall, I had a stetzerizer because I measured a quite hot plug at 1000+ volts/sec. However, if the Stetz is on that wall pulling the v/m down to 100 vs. 1000, the wiring gets hotter when I measure with my Gauss meter and it pulls a stronger EMF into the room via the wiring inside the wall. So really, the best option would be to get better wiring in the wall itself and have it insulated for the field. My biohack eneded up being moving the entire desk 2-feet toward the center of the room. That puts anyone at the computer desk completely out of the field, and I'm minimizing my field by pulling Stetzerizers 'down the line' from where the energy comes in from the house. It's about location and knowing where the energy flows in the wires. I've effectively created a good sitting space at the computer. One thing to note about the stetzerizers. I'm not a fan of them because they draw a bit more power into the home overall. But they can help in the distribution. You have to know what you're doing with them in my opinion. No home is the same and if you only get a volts/meter plug analyzer, you're missing out on changes to the EMF.

### Scompy — 2015-11-21T20:47:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182484>

**Nov. 21st, 2015 (Morning Beginning and Philosophical Phinishing School)**

Today's sunrise. You can see how weak the sun is here in Seattle if you look at how fast the sun slides to the right in just 9 minutes. It's very low on the horizon and doesn't get that high in the sky  UVB potential = 0.01%.

I had many interesting insights about projection of truth today, as I am a truth-seeker. This line of thought below has to do with knowledge sets and the nature of truth. We all encounter this situation a lot in science and when truth-seeking. Here is my viewpoint.

The expression of truth-unfiltered (without tact) often results in the projection of arrogance to listeners. In contrast, the expression of truth-filtered by love, humor or positive virtue often results in the projection of confidence, especially in the face of threat, and especially when that threat is an immediate danger or need for survival. As an analog for what I just wrote, let's think of James Bond or The Saint character. Often the conveying of information under threat or dying shows stronger confidence in the person because of composure and keeping cool. At the root of truth-seeking, once the truth is known, the conveying of the truth to others becomes a high importance because we want our audience to embrace it. But truth itself, as we know, often cannot be handled well by an audience. The interpretation of truth by an audience usually requires a knowledge set to be acquired first before being able to come to a new conclusion about a new version of truth.

For these reasons, there's a caveat to the idea of marrying truth with a positive virtue: Sometimes, the expression of truth-filtered needs to be tempered to result in little or no words spoken, even when the truth-seeker wishes to project the truth on another individual. The reasoning being that the listener may not have a knowledge set to understand the finer set to details and subtleties required to grasp the truth. Moreover, the expression of truth needs to have added vetting and temperance until both the speaker and the listener have acquired similar enough knowledge sets for compatability.

Because perception plays greatly into the speaker understanding the knowledge set of the listener/audience, tact and temperance can be hard to balance against truthbombs. But because we desire to help others, we must understand when a person is receptive enough to the truth or not. Attempts to force the truth will usually yield the opposite desire because that person must first have the ability to own the truth, which is conditionalized by that person's knowledge set.

I also believe we have a huge issue in modern society concerning the expression of raw truth vs. filtered-truth. When I refer to filtered-truth, I'm not talking about altering the truth itself, but finding tactful methods to present the truth to create a receptive audience. I find it is common practice to accept the exposure of 'raw truth' and knee-jerk reactions rather than spending a moment to blend the truth with a positive spin, and if a positive spin cannot be found immediately, to temper thoughts until a good tact can be found to express the truth.

As a society, we've come to accept over-bluntness. Many of us have developed personal shields on our shoulders and thicker skin to compensate, but does it really? To make matters worse, there's not much teaching at all about virtues and tact, few Finnishing schools exist on how to craft words and express oneself to create a receptive ear. Some of us use great tact in our writing to edit and re-edit our texts and emails for our intended audience. But do we also do the same verbally with others?

The projection of truth in a good light can be quite hard to develop when having a very limited window to time to respond (verbally vs. written). Many people feel pressured verbally to get out words faster to project a perception of a higher intellect. However, the faster a person speaks often has nothing to do with intellect. Those that are more methodical and take time to think about a response often have a better ability to weave in benefits for the audience, create a more receptive audience and make the truth stick stronger.

### Scompy — 2015-11-22T06:53:14+0200

### Scompy — 2015-11-23T19:37:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182539>

**Nov. 23rd, 2015 (Epigenetics at Warp speed?)**

**Question:** Could our synapses be considered a warp-speed **epigenetic system** in our bodies? We get new information (from outside the body = environment) and then store it as a memory. Now, one more step: we have less dense synapses throughout the body, so would this also be a primary method to translate the enviornmental information (starting from mental input) and then propagate to other organs and tissues in our bodies for change?

**Suspicions in a new Topic:** I used to be around 300 pounds so I understand what it's like to \*attempt\* to walk through a door or get into a car at that weight. One thing I always remembered was how much I was sweating-out-light and how much I'd be out of breath. When in a sitting position, the amount of O2 transferred into lung tissue has got to be vastly reduced in comparison to a slim-person. Having good posture just translates into getting more O2 flowing in the body. It's why they tell us to get up from the desk every 30mins at minimum or not being a couch potato for too long. For this reason, having the extra fat in the modern life is resulting in a catch-22 to reduce O2 as an added complication.

**Video Game Mitochondria:** I'll share this bit of info for people that are reading. I made my shift from biology into video games professionally because of the game "Parasite Eve." It was a biological-thriller based on the main character and main nemesis being able to control and **quickly evolve the mitochondria for superpowers.** Here's the takeaway, "Aya confronts Melissa onstage, and Melissa says that Aya’s [mitochondria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mitochondria) need more time to develop. She flees backstage, with Aya giving chase. Backstage, Melissa then mutates into a beast and flees into the sewers, declaring that her name is now Eve... ...The next day, the three go to see Dr. Klamp again. After examining cell samples from that of Eve and Aya's, Maeda concludes that based on [selfish gene theory](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Selfish_Gene), Aya and Eve's mitochondria are in an evolutionary race for survival."

### Scompy — 2015-11-25T01:01:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182613>

**Nov. 24th, 2015 (What Scompy is Eating)**

I compiled a list of what Scompy's typical ketotic food sources look like on a weekly basis in the Winter:

**Meats:**
Salmon
Tuna
Oysters
Shrimp
Scallops
Fish (Cod, Halibut, etc.)
Pasture raised egg (yolk runny)
Chicken breast (morning/lunch)
Chicken thighs
Grassfed Beef (morning/lunch)
Beef hotdog meat (no buns)
Sausage, Chicken
Sausage, Pork

**Dairy:**
Feta Cheese (lots)
Goat Cheese
Real Motz Cheese (limited)

**Veges/Fruits:**
Avocado (1/day on average)
Garlic
Onion
Asparagus
Celery
Green/Red Bell Pepper
Spinach
Carrot (orange, purple & yellow varities)
Broccoli
Eggplant
Ginger
Lemons (unlimited to flavor drinks)
Limes (unlimited to flavor drinks)
Other Fruits (not eaten in winter, only in season)

**Nuts/Seeds/Beans:**
Pumpkin seeds
Brazil Nuts (limited 2x week max)
Macadamia (rare-limited 1/month)
Lentils (limited 1x week 1/2 cup)

**Herbs/Salts/Condements**
Celery salt
Celery Seed
Sea salt
Himalyan Salt
Pepper
Tumeric
Sage
Basil
Mixed spices
Habenero/Tapatio salsa
Real salsa (limited)
Mustard (rare)
Real Mayo Avocado oil (rare)

**Oils/Butters:**
Grassfed Butter
Coconut Oil
Olive Oil (only room-temp)
85% Dark choc (very limited 3x week after lunch)

**Other:**
Biohacked Redox/Spring Icecubes in lemon water
Buttered Iced Decaf Coffee w/coconut oil
100% pure Rye Crackers with butter (very limited - Finncrisp)

### Scompy — 2015-11-25T07:40:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182632>

> [lilreddgirl said:](/goto/post?id=182627)
>
> Yay thank you for that list! I am also using that list pretty much and I'm glad to see I'm on track... (I used it for a year and a half throughout all the seasons though so now can't wait for spring/summer to try to add carbs)

No prob...Yeah, I'm with you on that sentiment. I went into a ketotic diet in 2011, so it's also been a long time for me also. I've even gotten some nice aged wines for next summer and did a small cellar of sorts since I have a basement. I've got my own garlic and asparagus growing the backyard also, and now a ton of compost from this year for next year's crop. Garlic is already growing now.. it's super hearty and I can harvest it in May/June.

I find it insanely interesting that our culture has it exactly flipped.... from Halloween to Thanksgiving to Christmas to New Years to Valentine's Day to the Easter Bunny, marketing forces encourage us to eat high-carb. Then in the summer and early fall, there's a lull.

I want to show you something else quite fascinating. I took this snapshot on Google Trends just now below and looked up 'weight loss' and 'exercise' together. Look at what we see!

Because of New Year's resolutions, we see peaks in January compared to every other time of the year. Marketing forces feed on society norms to retain control over our personal, individualized decisions from November through December, the Months of Gluttony. They do everything in their power to suggest and encourage us to let loose of our self-control and disregard the virtue of temperance. They do this because they want us to let-loose our wallets to them. Then come January, they feed on our guilt for letting loose the previous months to make a buck again. Genius? No, but it does rhyme with insideous.

### Scompy — 2015-11-25T08:00:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182633>

**Nov. 24th, 2015 (Today's Stealth Biohack)**

I work in a fairly good-lighting situation. It's a general sentiment in the office that we all keep the overhead fluorescent lights OFF to make the entire workspace semi-dark with ambient natural lighting around the edges. Here and there, people have permission to use standing lights at their desks if they want. But yesterday, guess WTF happens... office worker decides to turn on a free-standing lamp adjacent to my cubical. And yes, it has a fluorescent bulb in it. I'm pissed off internally and say nothing. I'm not reallypissed off because of the fluorescent bulb itself, but because I was never asked if I cared or not to have a new light turned-on in my space. So today over lunch, Scompy lies in wait. The moment shows itself. Like an Indi-car tire-rotation in the pit, he switches out the fluorescent bulb with incandescent one in record time. Lighting looks about the same. Scompy wins the race...one small hack in a great hackfest of making everyone's life better.

Why don't you change all bulbs officially in that office?

### Scompy — 2015-11-26T09:17:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182692>

**Nov. 25th, 2015 (Pondering the Sun again)**

This type of article below got my brain going again. Does our sun create other elements over time and may need to purge those elements like we purge metals from our own bodies for optimum star-health? Thinking here about iron, gold and the beloved Factor-X IRIDIUM. There are very long spans of time of calm, then quick/sudden disruptions. It so makes me think about a vacuole or lysosome to bring 'elemental waste' to the surface and then discharge it. There's a ton of comets and debris brought into the sun all of the time, so it's bound to fill its tank so to speak.

Sure, comets and asteroids can cause major catastrophic events on earth too, but physical solar ejecta seems likely over a longer periods of time. Maybe I read too much Dragon Riders of Pern as a kid. What I want to know for sure is the distribution of the iridium PPM across the entire planet. One would expect the impact site to contain much more PPM of iridium closer to the impact site(s). Is that what the data shows? but more important, what happens to the comets and meteors that go into the sun? Do they change elemental form or need to be eliminated at some point?

[http://www.sunisthefuture.net/tag/iron/](http://www.sunisthefuture.net/tag/iron/)

[http://www.bibliotecapleyades.net/ciencia/ciencia\_sol01.htm](http://www.bibliotecapleyades.net/ciencia/ciencia_sol01.htm)

### Scompy — 2015-11-28T18:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182777>

**Nov. 28th, 2015 (Einstein's Mother)**

In looking at a bunch of DHA articles today on Sciencedaily, it pointed me to Einstein's mother. There's a notion that Einstein was breastfed until the age of 6. If this is true, I'd like to know what foods his mother may have been eating to replenish DHA in her own body. There are more than a few copycat biohackers breastfeeding their children past the age of 2.

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2013/08/130813101927.htm](http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2013/08/130813101927.htm)

I was not breastfed. I know my mother told me I used to open the fridge as a 2-year old and eat blocks of butter straight. It all makes sense now.

### Scompy — 2015-11-30T19:30:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182885>

**Monday, Nov. 30th, 2015 (Did I just figure out seizures/epilepsy/migraines?)**

Because of the Japanese Seizure event ([https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dennō\_Senshi\_Porygon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon)), we know:

1\. Purple light + Red light PULSED at the same time (about once/second) messes up the translation of the information in the brain and can induce a seizure.

Because of Dr. K, we know:

2\. The body can leak red light.

Premise... **The eye itself can leak red light inside of it.** So what if it's just additive? Environment adds purple light + pulsing. The body creates the red-light component. The combination triggers a seizure.

Initial thoughts...Maybe negative changes in the RPE allows more leaking of redlight in the retina, such as cases with meningitis?

I bring this specific example up because when I was in my late 20s, I went on a date with a woman and she told me she had a 'condition.' After picking her up, she told me she suffered from seizures and had meningitis in the past. However, she had many of the clues figured out. I took her to a restaurant in Los Angeles, it was a very hot day and they were running fans overhead. She said she had to get out of there immediately, and I asked why. She told me she got seizures when fans and lights are going at the same time. When an overhead fan is on 'low speed', it usually causes a strobe effect, but of course, going up to medium or fast is analagous to increasing frame-rate or screen refresh rates beyond the point our brain can detect the individual pulses.

I'm glad strobe-lights **_faded out_** in society (ba-ba-boom). And I'll bet my bottom wallet that a lot of dance-club-puking in the 70s and 80s had nothing to do with alcohol, but more with being in a darker room with flickering lights at 2am when the melatonin is shattered. I'm glad the fluorescent-flickering is mostly gone now, as I remember going into grocery stores in the past and being under fluor-flickering for 20mins and getting stressed out. My mother admitted the same thing to me saying she 'rushed' through the store because it was so bad at times, and she felt like she needed to 'escape.'

Driving in a car would be another source to trigger a seizure when there are lots of trees, so there's a lot of shadow-to-light-to-shadow flickering.

### fitness@home — 2015-11-30T20:37:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182889>

> Driving in a car would be another source to trigger a seizure when there are lots of trees, so there's a lot of shadow-to-light-to-shadow flickering.

I believe that the strobe effect can also induce nausea. Another strobe-like thing that affects me (nausea) is when at a railroad crossing at night. If there are cars with headlights on the other side it can make me queasy.

Interesting thoughts in your post above. My DH has had a series of seizures over the past 18-24 months.

### Scompy — 2015-11-30T20:51:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-182892>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=182889)
>
> I believe that the strobe effect can also induce nausea. Another strobe-like thing that affects me (nausea) is when at a railroad crossing at night. If there are cars with headlights on the other side it can make me queasy.
>
> Interesting thoughts in your post above. My DH has had a series of seizures over the past 18-24 months.

I specifically work in a field that deals with Frames/Second. Often, most games suffer frame-rate hits at times vs. what we see on TV that pegs the frame/rate the same. I've seen similar seizure behavior manifest over the years in people when frame-rate drops are bad in games and even my own son reacting negatively due to certain light flickering. Hitting right at 25 Frames/Sec is probably a horrible flicker point, as it upcycles to 50 frames/second, which looks to be problematic for our eyes. It's always desired to get games locked at 60 frames/second and try to peg that frame rate there all of the time.

Obvsiouly, fixing the leaking of red-light is huge and the underlying issue. I used to get migraines too back in my 20s when my retinal capillaries were bursting, so this subject really hits home with me personally. I'd probably be blind right now if I hadn't made the decision to run a marathon by prepping for a year running in morning-sunlight 2-3 hours at a time, 3-4 days a week. Funny enough, the aerobic exercise was pretty much junk for me, and it was the morning sunlight exposure around the Rose Bowl that saved me. I didn't realize this until a few years ago. I also had reduced the glutamates, figuring out the end-result calcium effluxing at that time, but not knowing why until a few years ago (thanks for Dr. K of course).

(As a side note, I've got my son to be wary of flashing lights and he knows it can be bad for him. It's awesome to see that when something happens onscreen for him to look away in knowing it's not healthy).

### Scompy — 2015-12-02T20:06:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-183076>

**Dec. 2nd, 2015 (Past Glutamate Overload)**

I once had a REAL SUPERPOWER that few understand or know about. I know a hella-lot on the subject of Glutamate and Glutamic Acid (FYI if you're not aware, I've got a Bio degree and did my homework). My past superpower was that I was a **walking glutamate detector when I had poor redox, poorly hydrated and didn't get daily sun in a EMF loaded environment.** I want to say that again in another way. I had the ability to detect glutamate consumption through my front vision because my visual disturbances (capillary swelling/bursting) were 100% correlated to what I had previously eaten 30 minutes in the past. Typically the swelling and issues I got in my vision lasted longer with a higher glutamate load, or shorter with a smaller glutamate load. My instrumentation for about 2 years was highly tuned and I knew exactly what foods, and specifically, what glutamate-concentration-in-foods would trigger a tiny, small, medium, large or extreme response in my vision. Tiny responses often were flashed and only lasted a minute or two, medium tyipcally lasted 30mins, and extreme responses caused blood-leakage and spot could last for days, weeks or months.

It's the direct ingestion of glutamate is the problem I've found. Glutamic acid in that form isn't a problem to me if we're actually consuming it that way... THEREIN IS THE PROBLEM. Let's talk about glutamic acid (the amino acid) for a moment. It's highly abundant in the food supply, right? However, in my case in the past, glutamic acid load didn't seem to be too much of a problem in unprocessed foods. Look at cheese for a moment. Parmasean was previously heated when you put in on the table in a shaker. That stuff is literally pure glutamate. But if you have a lower-protein cheese, you get lower glutamic acid overall because glutamic acid is part of that protein content on the label.

Most bread BY DEFAULT is grinded up and dried out. Why is this bad? Because 40% of the GLUTEN molecule by dry weight is glutamic acid and drying it/heating it then yields far more glutamate. In other words, bread is the absolute perfect combination of glucose chains and higher glutamate content to make it addicting. How many people do you know that can say they're able to remove all bread from their diets? It's for this synergy of glutamate and sugars that promote the biochemical addiction.

Another great example would be the protein powders muscle builders are getting at the stores. On the label, it says some Milligrams of glutamic acid, but it's been powderized, dried out or over-heated at some point; that label is untruthful because some % of that 'glutamic acid' is 'glutamate.' Dried fish and dried meats (beef jerky) are also ultra-loaded in pure glutamate content for this reason. Aspartame is obviously a huge issue. I couldn't even do certain bone broths in the past until I learned not to overheat them. Food prep was half of the battle in my past to learn ways to reduce glutamates as a bandaid I understood redox and contributors to my bad environment... the quantum mechanisms to cure my issues outright.

Overheating glutamic acid --> glutamate is kind of an analog to overheating a fat or oil to create transfat. It's also my opinion that the body creates glutamate when it needs it and can handle tiny amounts that uptake/filtered into the bloodstream via the small intestines at a rate similar to glucose uptake. (This is why I said before the direct injection of glutamate via vaccines into muscle tissues has got to be problematic to say the least). The controls over glutamate if I remember correctly are much tighter in comparison to glucose as well in how much exists in the blood at any time.

When you let the body operate well with a higher redox, consumption of real glutamic acid or even smaller amounts of straight glutamate isn't going to be as problematic because the body will be making glutamate when it needs it and regulating it easier. The lower the redox you go, the body has a harder time with larger/higher quantities of glutamate via the gut. But if I'm preparing my foods by heating or drying it out, I've got a big problem. Again, if liver function is stifled, glutamine regulation is also compromised.

One last thing that's an important takeaway from my years of research on this issue. Glutamate is a suspected suppressor of taurine in the liver: the very molecule that can also compete at the NMDA receptor for potential receptor regulation. Like I said before, an insulin secretor because glutamate turns on/activates many mechanisms. Fixing organ function means fixing quantum biology. So it's my strong hypothesis, because I've literally seen it in my own life, that when a person eats ketotic and also concentrates on reduction of glutamate, more things fall into place, more weight is shed, less addictive cycles occur, etc. That glucose-glutamate synergy when it hits the bloodstream at the same moment in time from the gut is what I personally believe was a major problem for me in the past. It's EXACTLY the glucose-glutamate synergy we see in every piece of processed junk food on planet earth.

But..., the elimination of the sugar and glutamate was just one piece of puzzle and only a bandaid to the real underlying problems. Solving the redox requires a great deal of effort, and one's personal environment is EVERYTHING in this equation. This is why the overfocus and fixing glutamate is only a temp-hack because it's a much more complex quantum issue when other mechanisms are not optimal. Solving the quantum solves it outright so the impact of glutamate in my system now in 2015 compared to 2005 is night and day. Between 1996 - 2000, migraines and blind spots in my vision was my normal. There was a 6-month period when a large dried blood on the inside of the gel in my left eye blocked most of front vision and I couldn't read with both eyes at the same time. So you see to me, this is all about truth and my past to prevent blindness. It was a powerful motivator.

### Scompy — 2015-12-03T08:15:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-183155>

**Dec. 2nd, 2015 (A 10pm EMF experiment)**

It's easy to sort and click on current data to show cell phone coverage maps. I did the area I live just north of Seattle to see what a 2G+3G cell network looks like, then a comparison with only 4G active. The maps speak for themselves: it's easy to see the additional spread of EMF.

> Most bread BY DEFAULT is grinded up and dried out. Why is this bad? Because 40% of the GLUTEN molecule by dry weight is glutamic acid and drying it/heating it then yields far more glutamate. In other words, bread is the absolute perfect combination of glucose chains and higher glutamate content to make it addicting. How many people do you know that can say they're able to remove all bread from their diets? It's for this synergy of glutamate and sugars that promote the biochemical addiction.
>
> Another great example would be the protein powders muscle builders are getting at the stores. On the label, it says some Milligrams of glutamic acid, but it's been powderized, dried out or over-heated at some point; that label is untruthful because some % of that 'glutamic acid' is 'glutamate.' Dried fish and dried meats (beef jerky) are also ultra-loaded in pure glutamate content for this reason. Aspartame is obviously a huge issue. I couldn't even do certain bone broths in the past until I learned not to overheat them. Food prep was half of the battle in my past to learn ways to reduce glutamates as a bandaid I understood redox and contributors to my bad environment... the quantum mechanisms to cure my issues outright.
>
> Overheating glutamic acid --> glutamate is kind of an analog to overheating a fat or oil to create transfat. It's also my opinion that the body creates glutamate when it needs it and can handle tiny amounts that uptake/filtered into the bloodstream via the small intestines at a rate similar to glucose uptake. (This is why I said before the direct injection of glutamate via vaccines into muscle tissues has got to be problematic to say the least). The controls over glutamate if I remember correctly are much tighter in comparison to glucose as well in how much exists in the blood at any time.
>
> When you let the body operate well with a higher redox, consumption of real glutamic acid or even smaller amounts of straight glutamate isn't going to be as problematic because the body will be making glutamate when it needs it and regulating it easier. The lower the redox you go, the body has a harder time with larger/higher quantities of glutamate via the gut. But if I'm preparing my foods by heating or drying it out, I've got a big problem. Again, if liver function is stifled, glutamine regulation is also compromised.
>
> One last thing that's an important takeaway from my years of research on this issue. Glutamate is a suspected suppressor of taurine in the liver: the very molecule that can also compete at the NMDA receptor for potential receptor regulation. Like I said before, an insulin secretor because glutamate turns on/activates many mechanisms. Fixing organ function means fixing quantum biology. So it's my strong hypothesis, because I've literally seen it in my own life, that when a person eats ketotic and also concentrates on reduction of glutamate, more things fall into place, more weight is shed, less addictive cycles occur, etc. That glucose-glutamate synergy when it hits the bloodstream at the same moment in time from the gut is what I personally believe was a major problem for me in the past. It's EXACTLY the glucose-glutamate synergy we see in every piece of processed junk food on planet earth.
>
> But..., the elimination of the sugar and glutamate was just one piece of puzzle and only a bandaid to the real underlying problems. Solving the redox requires a great deal of effort, and one's personal environment is EVERYTHING in this equation. This is why the overfocus and fixing glutamate is only a temp-hack because it's a much more complex quantum issue when other mechanisms are not optimal. Solving the quantum solves it outright so the impact of glutamate in my system now in 2015 compared to 2005 is night and day. Between 1996 - 2000, migraines and blind spots in my vision was my normal. There was a 6-month period when a large dried blood on the inside of the gel in my left eye blocked most of front vision and I couldn't read with both eyes at the same time. So you see to me, this is all about truth and my past to prevent blindness. It was a powerful motivator.

Scompy,

I was a walking aspartame detector when I was expecting my first child! And a 70s fluorescent light detector - school sports teacher used to ask me why I played so well in (daytime ) practice but not in evening (flickering light) matches. As a student I worked in the window seat of an old historic library rather than the sparkling new building because I could concentrate better there.

I love your thoughts and the insights we can collect from hindsight. Having given up bread, sugar, processed anything, all at once, I swear toast is more addictive than bread.
When I first developed gut problems closely followed by skin problems I'm sure it was acute blue light poisoning as I had recently started doing computer work at night once the family were in bed in a year that was also stressful because of family health problems/bereavements (and I owned my first mobile phone). Very early on I was told I could eat what I liked but perhaps it might be wise to avoid Indian spices. I already knew I couldn't go near Chinese food but Indian was fine (even when I would normally avoid some of the ingredients). With terrible redox, I once offered to eat a Mars Bar in the doctor's office so he could watch my skin change over the next few hours because he didn't believe me. Sadly he didn't take up my offer. Now I am still careful, epi-paleo ( winter light deficient) but mistakes don't bite me very often. Very glad to read your positive experience and thoughts behind it.

### Scompy — 2015-12-05T23:49:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-183432>

**Dec. 5th, 2015 (Thank you 8th Grade Teacher)**

Many years ago, like in 1983, something Incredible happened that I'm going to share here in my journal.

^^^^ I have a lot of Incredible gear like this (picture taken today), not just because I'm a game designer, but also because of a subtle reference most people won't know. I went to middle school in Corvallis, Oregon at what was called "Western View" at that time. Today, it doesn't exist because it was torn down by the city because they feared it wasn't earthquake-proof enough. Anyway, Western View was the home of someone incredible... his name being Brad Bird. He is responsible for the movie at Pixar. If you look at the movie, you'll actually see reference to Western View in the movie itself. I'm only a handful of people that picked up on this fact. Brad Bird had gone to Western View 10 years prior to me. Now as another weird thing, my future would hold some relation to Brad Bird, 'cause I was intimately involved with the WALL-E game.

Anyway, that was a semi-side tangent. Interesting enough, my 8th Grade English teacher had profound influence on my life and has huge implications here on the forums. Her name was a Mrs. Keltner. That year in 8th grade prepared me for straight As in high school. Without Mrs. Keltner, I probably would have been an average student. Why? Here's the deal. Even though I dreaded going into her class at the beginning of the year, by the end of the year I was pulling Bs in her class. She had just come out of college in 1982 teaching college English, but now was teaching us 8th graders. That wasn't the shock. The shock was that we were all subjected to her crazy rules in her class. For one, she kept the room temperature at 62 degrees. This was not only a major complaint from the students, but I remember parents complaining too in the winter session. Many students ended up wearing coats in class. She didn't budge on the lower classroom temp and presented them with information about how lowering the temp in classrooms was shown to improve the memory in children.

Second, she NEVER used the fluorescent lights in the room. It was always semi-dark and the window blinds were the only source of light in the class. This made a memorable experience where we often had to give presentations in the front of the classroom. I vividly remember sometimes looking at students speaking almost in the shadows at times when there was heavy cloud-cover outside.

I had a very hard time in her classroom with grades. But it was still my favorite and most memorable class because she challenged us on so many levels. I was barely pulling Cs and by the end of the year, I finally got a B and felt proud of it. I learned higher grammar for my age group and didn't know the impact that class would have on my high school years. Mrs. Keltner at that time in 1983 was probably in her early 60s, and I doubt she's still around now, but thank you where ever you may be. Your insights into CT and artifical lighting was profound for you generation of students.

### Penny — 2015-12-06T03:10:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-183470>

Yeah, that's what started us on this party - constipation - that's when I learned about GABA - your gastro guy looks at you differently when you tell him your kid needs GABA... not Miralax - I swear they would have killed him - I think that is why P5P is the number 1 supplement with the autistic kids - it converts glutamate into GABA and so you can also make dopamine and serotonin with it - we used GABA Calm a sublingual and it was so calming - of course "it doesn't cross the blood brain barrier" was what the gastro guy said - now I think you can use GABA Calm as an environmental/redox test because years later we noticed "it doesn't work as well as it used to"... which is actually good now that we've calmed the blue light down or at least use flux/blue blockers at night - now I know my kid cannot handle being in an environment that is 6 straight hours of wifi - my other kid I give mag and k2 and hope for the best, but like me, he is always in the sun - rhodiola was a life saver for my kid as were epsom salt foot soaks - nausea, a 30 day migraine for which we got called "truant" - and to make it even more fun, the kid can't process pharmaceuticals - but that didn't stop them from trying to give them to him - it seemed like the neurologist was even deadlier than the gastro guy... I had no idea that if calcium didn't efflux, glutamate wouldn't be so much of a problem... it's replaced by potassium (which is also low in people) which is a single bond instead of the double bond of calcium - frankly, I can't believe how many processed foods I was feeding my kids - now I've totally changed the food and the light so naturally the kid is starting to cheat - I've gotten so good at mitigating every reaction with a supplement because of their crappy lifestyles that I wonder what they are going to do when they go out into the world and encounter all the maladies that most people face because they haven't mitigated anything in their environment - you just hope they remember something you did or said and maybe they'll start doing some of these lifestyle changes on their own when they start feeling like scata...

### Scompy — 2015-12-06T23:50:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-183548>

**Dec. 6th, 2015 (The Seed)**

Humans are like seeds. Environment means everything.

\--Put a seed on a rock, then watch the seed have a harder time growing. It's getting light and grouding, but has an impaired issue with its roots to take in water.
\--Put a seed under a canopy of other trees, and it has a harder time getting light even when there's a good water source.
\--Put a seed in earth that can retain water and under good sunlight and you get a good plant. A plant with a much better potential.

Humanity has been living under a blanket ofg growing nnEMF. Does this help us grow? In some environments, it is an invisible canopy that also sucks away our water source at the same time. We're like that seed that wants the sun and water to thrive. Are we getting it daily?

### Scompy — 2015-12-08T08:28:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-183681>

**Dec. 7th, 2015 (The Quantum Life Game)**

Tonight, I started to create a game, because it's what I do best. So who is in with me? Anyone else with fun ideas? Below is my initial premise with some modern-day humor woven into it based on everything we all know here. It's basically a reflection about the stupid-crap people are doing to themselves based on their environments. I see each Player growing over the course of the game, continually being damaged by what we all know...

_**The Quantum Life.**_

Everyone starts out at 100 MindHealth and 100 BodyHealth. These two values are tracked over the course of the game. When a Health value drops below 50, you must draw a Minor Disease card. When a Health value drops below 25, you must draw a Major Disease card. There are ways to fight-off the diseases, but it will cost you Tokens or $$ to do so. The Player that survives the longest wins the game.

**Resources:** These are available resources that you can attain through the game and maybe trade with Players bartering-style; at times, you gain them as a bonus or you can convert your $$ into Tokens as well. Each Token probably costs $10,000.

· CT Tokens: You may use 1 token to recover 1d6 Health when you see the (c) symbol.

· Sun Tokens : You may use 1 token to recover 1d6 Health when you see the (s) symbol.

· DHA Tokens: You may use 1 token to recover 1d6 Health when you see the (d) symbol.

· Water Tokens: You may use 1 token to recover 1d6 Health when you see the (w) symbol.

· Biohack Tokens: You may use 1 token to recover 1d6 Health when you see the (b) symbol.

**Stage 1: The Baby Shower (Roll 1d6)**

1\. 8 Tokens, $3000

2\. 7 Tokens, $4000

3\. 6 Tokens, $5000

4\. 5 Tokens, $6000

5\. 4 Tokens, $7000

6\. 3 Tokens, $8000

**Stage 2: The Birth (Roll 1d6)**

1\. Standard hospital practice includes an RF-EMF Foot tag monitor and accelerated vaccine schedule; reduce MindHealth by 5d6 (c or d) and pay $1000 in medical bills.

2\. Anti-vax parents spend money on a high-end Baby monitor and use it nightly, while talking all day long on their cell phones for continual RF-EMF; reduce MindHealth by 5d6+6 (c) and pay $500 for the monitor.

3\. Parents slap sunscreen on you daily--chemical toxins added & sun benefits reduces; Pay $200 in sunscreen products and remove BodyHealth by 2d6+12 (s)

4\. Born in Winter, little sun exposure and parents don't know how to biohack; reduce BodyHealth by 6d6 (s)

5\. Parents elect to use formula and heat it in a microwave because it is too painful to breastfeed; remove 2d6 Body Health(d), 2d6 MindHealth(d) and pay $2000 for formula.

6\. Given fluoridated and distilled water for a few months to save money for the family; remove 4d6 BodyHealth(w or b) and 2d6 MindHealth(w).

**Stage 3: The Toddler (Roll 1d6)**

1\. Mother decides to go to the park daily; you receive +3 Sun Tokens.

2\. Father gets a bonus for working overtime and buys a magnetic EMF-generating vehicle; remove +1d6 MindHealth and +1d6 BodyHealth, but gain $5000.

3\. Parents get a bit of information how to biohack on social media; you receive +3 Biohack Tokens

4\. Family moves to northern Montana and begin eating fish; you must remove BodyHealth by 2d6 (s) but you gain +1 DHA Token, +1 CT Token and +1 Water Token.

5\. Your parents realize you don't like the expensive toys they bought you and instead, like to raid the fridge to eat grassfed butter; you receive +2 DHA Tokens and $3000 for the toys.

6\. Mother gives you ice whenever you want and colder baths in the summer; you receive +3 CT Tokens.

Etc., etc., etc....

Items:

· Hardwired Computer Components

· Blue Blockers

· nnEMF Detector

Etc., Etc., Etc.

That's about as far as I got, but you see where I'm going with it. Could be a board-game too instead of cards landing on squares...

It occurred to me today that when DNA unravels, it probably looks like spiral galaxies. So after 34 seconds of searching, I get this:
[http://www.goldennumber.net/dna/](http://www.goldennumber.net/dna/)

Because the sequence is always additive taking the last two digits, it makes a lot of sense that one can attribute 'growth' to the Fibonacci Sequence. For this reason, the sequence is a result of the laws of volume. And if volume is altered and curved (Einstein), then one would also expect the Fibonacci Sequence to do the same resulting in different values. I'm now pondering where the Fibonacci Sequence exists in the mitochondria. Thinking has got to be there for optimal performance.
