Thanks for sharing Scompy. Sounds not too unlike an encounter I had with the Entergy guy that lives in my neighborhood. I'm interested to hear about your setup as we bought a piece of property and are going to build. The more I say I want the more my husband gives me the side-eye. But he's like do the leg work and we'll try to find someone who can understand what you want. I was thinking a lot about John ott's book and windows too. Especially for my son...

### Scompy — 2016-11-23T07:57:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-204233>

**Nov. 22, 2016 (Jack Kruse Tidbits are Boombits)**
So I'm not sure where I found this, but when I find information that is profound, I usually mine the info for later to lock it up into my brain. This one I read and reread, but then it hit me what's probably happening here between Cytochrome Complex 2 and 1 and I could finally visualize it fully. Dang, this shows how important it is to read, go look up terms and get familiar, then read again, and then take out something at a later time and read again for it to sink in. Now I see exactly why and how to laugh at Food Gurus:

_"So the more charge a mitochondria has the more electric field lines exist to control charged particles. This is why the amount of DC current a cell has is correlated with health. It offers more control to atoms in a tissue lattice. This is why electric fields control protons in the matrix and in enzymatic reations. When the cellular charge drops so does our control of protons. This is what we call inflammation in medicine. It can be thought of chaos or a lowering of the pH. A lowered pH leads to a lowered EZ in a cell. A lowered EZ means less light can be contained by the cell.

Since electric fields allow electrons to move away from them, you should begin to understand why we use oxygen to breath. Oxygen is a strong reducing agent at the end of ECT so it draws the current of electrons of food to it. The voltage or the charge in the mitochindria just correlates to the number of electric fields in mitochondria. These fields are always working opposite ECT flow when the charges on the inner mitochondrial membrane are high. This is seen when the inner mitochondiral membrane is oscillating at 100 Hz and fat burning and the fingerprint of the effect is seen in the alignment of the cristae around a lipid droplet in mitochondria.

Each cytochrome generates its own electric fields. Those fields are related to the charge they hold. So this means we can have instances in mtochondria where we see reversal of electron flow from cytochrome 2 to one. This often happens in diabetes where no superoxide pulses are made. This is not an optimized finding. When ECT flow goes to two to one it slows the electron tunneling at cytochrome one because electrons are flowing to cytochrome 1 from both directions and they want to repel the charge. That changes the force there and alters tunneling speeds and changes the amount of NAD+ and the amount of oxygen at the ATPase causing low NAD+ and pseudohypoxia. This is what we see in aging and disease generation.

Since the positive charged protons are spit out of the cytochrome mouths from the matrix you can beign to see why positive charge point away from the electric fields in mitochondria and why electric fields point towards electrons. This explains why electric field lines go out of positive particles like protons and into electrons. Negative charges cancel out positive ones, but it has no affect on their mass. Since electrons have 1/1836 the mass of protons this creates an energy imbalance and it is why protons are always associated with inflammation. Contrast this with magnetic field lines. They are always zero when considering an object or subatomic particle. This is the basis of the second law. Magnetic fields lines need motion to exist. This is why the ATPase generates them. When magnetic fields do exist their closeness or density is related to their strength. This is why when things condense magnetism grows. This is why ice is less magnetic than heated water. The direction of the motion determines the direction of the field lines and hence the magnetic field.

Cytochromes are like loops and the ECT is like a charged wire passing through the loops. This is why the 3rd law shows that the more charge on the ECT the larger or stronger the magnetic field in mitochondria become. This is why ketosis helps in disease reversals. It is also why carbohydrates do not. They lower voltage and current on the inner mitochondrial membranes. This is why 1 mole of carbs make 36 ATP and why 1 mole of palmitate makes 147 ATP. The charge of particles flowing through the cytochromes changes the rotation of the magnetic field. Their reversal of flow does not.

But magnetic fields all exert forces on moving charged particles so this is why the ATPase exerts a force on ECT. The magnetic field also affects oxygen distal to the ATPase because it is paramagnetic. It also exerts a force on Ca2+ and Mg2+ for the same reason. Magnetic flux lines generated by the ATPase are interesting because of how the cristae are aligned. The ATPase close to others will have magnetic fields that increase magnetic pull toward one another but the folded cristae means that fields opposite ATPase will repel one another even though ECT flow is in a giant loop. The loop is not a circle and this induces a varying magnetic force field to control protons inside the matrix. These magnetic forces might be critical to the generation of a magnetic monopole in the matrix because opposite magnetic poles attract. This would create a geometry deep inside the matrix away from the cristae where this effect could occur based upon Maxwell's laws. The charge through the loops is the key to understanding the size and strength of the magnetic field."_

### Scompy — 2016-11-27T19:07:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-204373>

**Nov. 27, 2016**
This paper on liver mitochondria has a lot of great nuggets for those first exposed to the science.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3942818/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3942818/)

**Mitochondrial function**
Mitochondria are the power producer of the cell which plays a central role in the generation of energy from nutrient oxidation. Hepatocytes are rich in mitochondria; each hepatocyte contains about 800 mitochondria occupying about 18% of the entire cell volume. Mitochondria have a unique role in the liver compared to other organs as they participate in glucose, lipids and protein metabolism. Mitochondria play an essential role in the cell as they provide the majority of cellular energy in the form of ATP; generate and regulate ROS; buffer cytosolic Ca2+ and regulate apoptosis. Carbohydrates and fats are oxidized in the mitochondria to produce energy. Glucose is converted into pyruvate within the cytosol, transported to the mitochondria where it is converted to acetyl-CoA. Acetyl-CoA undergoes TCA cycle resulting in CO2, water and energy. FAs in the liver originate from the diet, adipose tissue lipolysis, or hydrolysis of intracellular stores or _de novo_ lipogenesis. These free FAs (FFAs) are metabolized through oxidation, ketogenesis or esterification into triglyceride. Together with the muscle, the liver is the main site for mitochondrial fatty acid oxidation. The oxidation of FFAs occurs mainly in the mitochondria of hepatocytes where FFAs are converted by carnitine palmitoyltransferase-1 (CPT1) into acyl-carnitine which is transported into the mitochondrial matrix. FFAs oxidation into acetyl-coenzyme A (acetyl-CoA) and its subsequent oxidation by the TCA cycle generates reduced NADH and reduced flavine-adenin dinucleotide (FADH2). NADH and FADH2 are the electron donor and transfer the hydrogen/electron to an oxygen molecule, _via_ a variety of redox components in complex I through IV in the mitochondrial respiratory chain located in the inner mitochondrial membrane\[[46](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3942818/#B46)\]. The flow of electron in the respiratory chain is coupled with pumping of protons from the mitochondrial matrix into the inter-membrane space, thus creating an electrochemical gradient across the membrane\[[46](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3942818/#B46)\]. When ATP is low, protons re-enter the matrix trough ATP synthase and the energy released initiates ATP synthesis\[[46](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3942818/#B46)\]. Although most of the electrons end up in water, the insulation of complexes I and III of the respiratory chain from oxygen in not perfect\[[46](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3942818/#B46)\]. Excessive electron flow to the respiratory chain results in accumulation and leakage of electrons at these two sites which react with oxygen to produce ROS\[[47](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3942818/#B47)\]. About 1%-2% of mitochondrial oxygen consumption results in ROS production under normal condition\[[48](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3942818/#B48)\], 90% of cellular ROS are produced in the mitochondria\[[49](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3942818/#B49)\]. Mitochondria play an important role in ROS homeostasis as they are the main site for ROS generation but can also be a target for excessive ROS exposure. The ROS production occurs through reduction of oxygen to superoxide (O2-) by complex I and III of the electron transfer chain (ETC). Under normal conditions Mn-SOD located in the matrix of mitochondria converts the O2- to H2O2 which is subsequently reduced to H2O. ROS production in mitochondria is upregulated in conditions where increased NADH and increased membrane potential is not coupled with an increase in ATP production. Excessive production of ROS exceeding the cell’s antioxidant defenses can damage components of the cells such as lipids, proteins and nucleic acids (particularly mtDNA) leading to oxidative stress and ultimately apoptosis. This can be observed in conditions of increased oxidation of FFAs such as in NASH\[[50](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3942818/#B50)\] and when levels of NADH are augmented due to alcohol metabolism in alcoholic steatohepatitis\[[45](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3942818/#B45)\].

> **Fun Friday, September 16th, 2016**

> **Analyze the lyrics quantumly! I always had wondered about the lyrics of this song, they never made any sense to me until now. I'm thinking Neil Peart in 1980 had a strong clue about what was up, especially considering the "Invisible Airwaves" passage.**
>
> **Music: [Geddy Lee](http://www.rush.com/band/geddy-lee/) and [Alex Lifeson](http://www.rush.com/band/alex-lifeson/)
> Lyrics: [Neil Peart](http://www.rush.com/band/neil-peart/)**
>
> Begin the day
> With a friendly voice
> A companion, unobtrusive
> Plays the song that’s so elusive
> And the magic music makes your morning mood
>
> Off on your way
> Hit the open road
> There is magic at your fingers
> For the Spirit ever lingers
> Undemanding contact
> In your happy solitude.
>
> Invisible airwaves
> Crackle with life
> Bright antennae bristle
> With the energy
> Emotional feedback
> On a timeless wavelength
> Bearing a gift beyond price –
> Almost free…
>
> All this machinery
> Making modern music
> Can still be open-hearted
> Not so coldly charted
> It’s really just a question
> Of your honesty
>
> One likes to believe
> In the freedom of music
> But glittering prizes
> And endless compromises
> Shatter the illusion
> Of integrity
>
> “For the words of the profits,
> Are written on the studio wall,
> Concert hall –
> Echoes with the sounds…
> Of salesmen.”

### Scompy — 2016-12-08T20:39:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-205009>

**Dec. 8th, 2016. (Posting here as well...I wrote in QH:L,W,M)**

Hi grade-school teachers and administrators. Thanks for letting me come into your school today and talk about some "fun-da-mentally fun fizz-icks!" I'd like you all to consider this question: "Are you able to control the physics in your classrooms?" Seriously, I'm not trying to be edgy here, but from my perspective, you may have a harder time doing it... and without your knowledge! Yeah, I know you think you all are quite 'grounded' in science. Good to know.

The last school I was at, I had to keep things high-level for them. Strangely though, it appears you may have been missing some super-simple physics from your own school days...I'm talking about stuff that is basic and fundamental to the universe. Gravity? No. The elements? Good guess, but sorry, no. I'd like to introduce a particle to you called the photon and a way we describe them called the Electromagnetc Spectrum. The EM spectrum tells us the wavelengths of light. A Wavelength? Wow, okay, let me make this simple. Each color (or wavelength of light) has a different wave distance. So if you take red light, it has a longer distance compared to, let's say, blue light that has a shorter wavelength. You can have fun with rainbows this way, right?

But there is something to point out...The shorter the wavelength, the more power it packs. This is why when you listen to AM radio it is not as clear and crisp compared to FM radio. There's this new tech called LiFi. It's a form of WiFi, but it packs a greater punch because it uses a shorter wavelength compared to WiFi that uses a longer one. For this innate reason of how light works, LiFi will be able to send and transmit data faster than WiFi. Have you seen those large radio transmitters up the hill from where you live? Those guys are pretty tall, huh. They output a wave of light that may be hundreds of feet long. Yes I said light. I know I'm talking about radio. Oh boy, let's just move on.

So now you're asking again about the lengths of these light waves? Well, let's start with colors again. Our eyes can see colors, if you recall each second of your existence. Therefore, the color entering your eyeballs tells you something about the size of the wavelength. If your eyes were built bigger, like a lion's eyes, you could probably see longer wavelengths... you know, colors that are a little bigger than red called infrared. But now imagine you are a bug with tiny weenie eyes. Is it possible you can see the colors of the rainbow beyond violet that also pack more of a punch? Actually, we know some bugs can do this and see what an uber-mega violet. Some people like to call that Ultra-violet. I'm sorry if this is coming of sounding condescending or that I could be one of those know-it-all types that are a total turnoff. I understand that you may not have been taught this before.

This is where it may get more complicated. That ultraviolet wavelength (remember this is a smaller distance compared to colors) comes in three varieties that your 1st graders can probably understand also: A, B and C. I know, hold tight. At dawn, the A-version of ultraviolet color that we cannot see (remember some bugs can see this because they have tiny weenie eyes) appears with the colors that we can see. This light is called UVA. Can someone tell me what that stands for? Technically yes, the University of Virginia is U.Va, but we're talking about light still.

As the sun... what is that? Yes I am aware that the Sun is the nearest Star to earth. Anyway, as the sun starts moving overhead higher into the sky, more B-version ultraviolet light typically shows up. The B-version has even a smaller wavelength than the A-version of Ultraviolet, the UVA light. No I am not planning on testing you with a Scantron machine. As you would have guessed, the B-version of Ultraviolet light is appropriately named UVB. It's that UVB stuff that traditionally your doctors have been telling you to block out with sunscreen because, they too, unfortunately had not had grade-school training in light physics. That's fine...it's one reason why I came to your WiFi powered school.

The C-ultraviolet light you asked about? Can it fry us like an ion blaster? Yes, that's true, UVC light is a large part of the reason why astronauts wear space suits, but I have no idea why you're talking about blasters at this time. Luckily, our atmosphere blocks out UVC in most cases... Basically, there's little need to worry about the UVC light from the sun. We can actually make UVC light on earth, like using an Arc welder or when they want to blast Ebola to shreds. Why did I say blast,.. Oh well, yes, Xrays, Gamma Rays and Cosmic rays are even smaller wavelengths than UVC! No, no, no, I'm not talking about Ray guns. Yes, I have seen those old 1950 movies with the Gamma Ray guns, what's your point. Ion cannons vs. Ion blasters? Really, you just went there? No Chewbacca is a Wookie, not an Ewok...I know, but let's take up the L.A.S.E.R. Crossbow some other time.

The original conversation was about how you all are not able to control the physics in your classrooms. It has come to my attention that your administrators have recently made life 'perceptively' easier so you can connect your electronic workbooks, tablets, computers and other devices to your WiFi routers. From this previous presentation, can anyone tell me what you think WiFi is? Anyone? Bueller? Yes that is correct, WiFi is that thing that allows you to surf the internets and check your email. As it turns out, the wavelength of WiFi is about a foot in length, so because our eyeballs are not a foot in diameter, we cannot see that light even though it is there. Dinosaur eyeballs? Interesting, well at least you're thinking...about this. Yes I will say it again so it sinks in...WiFi is light, but it's a form of light our eyes cannot see. Radio sounds you ask again? It is probably going to be too much of a tangent to talk about Modulation. Yes, surfing online might get you want you are looking for, doubtfully.

So I need to ask, do any of you have control over the WiFi in your classrooms? In other words, are you able to shut down your WiFi whenever you want? What? What? Calm down, one at a time... I can't hear over all of this shouting. You first in the blue shirt. What was that again? You don't think shutting down the internet is a good idea? Well, that's a great point. I am just wondering why your administrators decided to choose WiFi over a wired connection. Yes, a wired connection is faster and more reliable, and poses far less of a health risk. There's no reason to giggle. Yes, I did say a health risk. Because of your tech devices need a WiFi connection. Yes, I see.

Next, can you control those overhead lights in your classrooms? That's correct, the light switch! Okay, shhhh, shhhhh. Yes, one at a time, you with the cell phone in hand, what is your question? No I'm not implying to shut down the lights, but that might be an interesting experiment. Let me ask it this way... Do you have the ability to choose the type of bulbs in the lights above your head in school? You don't, why not...Because your administrators saved the school money with cost effective decisions...? I see.

Now I need to ask, have you noticed any behavioral differences since installing the WiFi last year? Or under the fluorescent lights for hours on end? Hmm, that's intriguing you thought I was talking about your behaviors, but I was implying the behavioral differences in your students... Have you seen more aches, ADD, ADHD, asthma, autistic or erratic behaviors appear later in the day compared to earlier in the day for instance? Wow, I didn't expect you all to agree with me. What's this about your school nurses and the timing of meds? Biochemical issues in most of the students? I see.

Now that we've moved into the 21st century and those those thick rectangles called books have become outdated, I'm going to leave you this website as a link in case you change your minds about the biochemical influences influencing your students. What was that again? You are knocking down the library to put in computer center for the students? I see... so all students will have their homework and lessons on WiFi tablets to work on during school and to take home after school.

I think I'm about done here now. I am going to leave you this website link about WiFi in schools you will all appreciate. Get back to me if you have any questions. Thanks for letting me come in and speak today.

[http://www.wifiinschools.com/index.html](http://www.wifiinschools.com/index.html)

### Scompy — 2016-12-09T21:21:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-205063>

**Dec. 9, 2016 (Rolling the Bones about the Future)**

Interviewer: So let's take a road trip back into the year 2024. What on earth made you think of inventing Bone Armor at that time?

Ray Oflite: How should I put this... with all of the advances in Radio-Frequency technologies, their proliferation after 2020 and the density of our power systems already in-place, most cities had reached an upper limit of saturation, so to speak. A few people were starting to catch onto the fact that their health issues might be connected to the biophysics they allow into their lives and where they live. But back then, but most people simply didn't think it was as large of a problem at that time as we know it is today.

Interviewer: True, it seems like history repeats itself with these types of issues of toxins that the general public doesn't perceive or can readily observe. You know, like the Mad Hatter mercury poisoning from the 19th century, smoking and asbestos, lead wall paint in houses and so forth. (Shakes head). I wasn't even aware of the Radio-Frequency issue until about 2026 after India, Finland, South Africa and Russia had pressured the WHO that eventually established the lowered limits we have worldwide today.

Ray Oflite: It's quite interesting how it required some major studies to show how manmade EMFs are influencing mitochondrial function directly as an observable response. The pioneering efforts of quantum observations helped a lot.... You know, to see what was going on inside of organelles directly. Once the mitochondria, golgi bodies and ribosomes didn't seem so 'woo' to the general public, a lot of things changed around the public's perception of the processes involved. The Scientific Standards adopted in 2022 also helped establish a more unified experimental approach with studies.

Interviewer: Are you referring to controlling for Light physics in most of the scientific studies? (Scratches head)

Ray Oflite: In part yes. We cannot forget the water physics with the light physics because of the dependencies tied up within the processes. Before the Scientific Standards, most institutes, research labs and universities rarely tested for and controlled for these most fundamental and basic aspects of light frequencies, photon interactions and intersections with water and life. Plus, by 2020, most scientific tools had been constructed with RF devices and other EMFs that negatively impacted scientific outcomes.

Interviewer: (Facepalm) For sure, like throwing a monkey wrench into the equation without realizing the monkey wrench was there at all. It's perplexing that the scientific community hadn't figured out these controls earlier in their experiments ... that something like manmade light could be far different than natural sunlight we have been under for all of eternity. It almost smells like there could have been deeper reasons involved, even political.

Ray Oflite: Actually many researchers and scientist had figured it out about century ago, but their information was buried in part. For instance, back in the mid-20th century, John Ott had shown the omission and addition of various frequencies upon various plants. (Pauses to drink his RO water). Many mitochondriacs in the early 21st century also promoted a similar emphasis in Quantum Health groups, but because most of those sources were on social media, the public generally took their outlook with a grain of salt.

Interviewer: I remember those days for sure (laughs). I totally recall that time after Pollack and Wallace received the Nobel prizes and the KMA -- Kruse's Mitochondriac Army -- became more popular to get the message out to the general public about how to cure with nature rather than bandages with the biochemistry. I remember when I first heard about the KMA and thought it meant they were the "Kiss My Ass" group, can you believe that?

Ray Oflite: Fun times. Yes, the establishment of the biophysics and identifying successful and failed biohacks came together in those days. If you think about the medical and pharmaceutical companies of that era, it makes more sense as to why biohacking became a thing. If you remember, mainstream scientific community didn't really understand the importance of UV light. As for my Bone Armor line, as long as humanity is better off, I can suffer with less mula knowing future generations won't have to develop the neolithic diseases that were rampant by 2020. If you didn't know, many people are still buying the Bone Armor to this day as a novelty item to this very day! (LOL's violently). It's all good....

Interviewer: Yeah, let's get back to the Armor if you don't mind. Did you ever think your line of armor would end up so trendy that it would spinoff into "The Bonesters?"

Ray Oflite: I didn't think my Armor line would have been taken to that level, that's for sure! (Shock and Awe expression) Tattoos had become sort of the norm by the early 2000s. Remember those lower back wings? The "tattoo was no longer taboo" by 2016 or thereabouts. Of course, today most of us know to shy away from back-blocking sunlight to help our biophysical processes work better with the atmosphere we've got now.

Interviewer: Hey, take a look at this... (Shows Ray his tiny arm tattoo) From our previous interview last year, we know that your Bone Armor line is made from a form of synthetic calcium composite to help buffer the impact of RF EMFs--both reflective and absorptive. But what is still not clear, what were your original reasons for making it?

Ray Oflite: Wow, so I still have the original set that I made for myself. I keep it in a case at the office... sort of like my First Dollar. My motivation to make it was sort of a biohack to see if it would help my tinnitus, ADHD and capillary bleeding to help combat the increasing Radio-Frequency exposures. Like a lot of us, I discovered I was becoming electrohypersensitive decade of EMF expansion. Believe it or not, I first marketed a crude Armor line to the outdoor LARPers...the Live Action Role-Players that hit each other with fake clubs, swords and axes.

Interviewer: (Interrupting) I doubt most people would know about that outdoor gaming club. It was pretty obscure even in its own day.

Ray Oflite: It turns out that many LARPers had many gaming behaviors outside of being outside. Most gamers in general consumed tech-gadgets daily as a norm, aside from their phones, devices and SmartStuff at home. But nerdiness can pay off. Some gamers also dug deeper and learned the quantum biology. A few LARPers came to me looking for ways to mitigate the damage while outside playing their techy games, and were fine with wearing the armor indoors too. That's where I came in with the Bone Armor to make them look all medival-like. Just a few years later if you recall, a few athletes adopted my armor into their implants. First were the Bonester arm, leg and even face implants to look rough and tough. The rest is history.

Interviewer: Some of the teeth-on-chin implants were really over top... I'm glad that didn't become a mainstream trend.

Ray Oflite: Yeah, what can you do? Because the implants became popular and so easy to install or remove among high school and college students, enough people 'felt' a benefit wearing the armor or getting a new implant. I think that was the main driving force as to why it spread. If you think about it, the implants are a bit like an exoskeleton to help reflect and mitigate the radio-frequencies. I didn't realize until enough people tried it that my calcium composite mixture ended up as a great RF EMF back-reflector. I just didn't know how much they would become in-demand because so many people figured out it was something about the power-systems and tech causing their problems.

Interviewer: I think I get it... sort of like surgically putting on a newborn's cranial-cap instantly on Day 1 to protect against the Radio-Frequency EMFs?

Ray Oflite: Good analogy.... Like putting a mobile phone or WiFi router near the brain to damage neurons without having a cranial skull cap formed. I also consider the cranial density and thickness into that line of thought.

Interviewer. Yes, what about the standard EMFs?

Ray Oflite: Manmade magnetic and electrical fields can be just as bad or even worse because they penetrate matter directly.

Interviewer: Like when I run my Washer/Drier unit and there is like a 6 foot field around it?

Ray Oflite: Yes. If you think about babies and kids with less biomass overall, they just don't have the mitigation and calcium reserves we have as adults. I'm really glad the standard have been lowered because it takes decades before enough myelin forms around the neurons to protect them...assuming calcium isn't continually being shoved into cells to feed underpowered mitochondria. Yes, in some ways, I wish we could turn back the clock and prevent the world's unnecessary suffering from neurological conditions, cancer and other diseases as Wallace identified for his Nobel Prize.

Interviewer: (Interrupting again) On that sentiment of turning back time, you reminded me of the Superbowl 54 Event.

Ray Oflite: Oh yes. I still don't remember how many players were injured...

Interviewer: Five players had bones broken and another four players snapped their ACLs all in the very same tackle. I remember it because it was 5+4 for Superbowl 54. That one play changed the outcome of that game and probably got some people very rich in Vegas too.

Ray Oflite: (Giggles) Yeah, that was some smack-up and one for the record-books. The intensity of the EMF at that superbowl was off the charts. Many people had measurements... something to Smoogle for sure. Bottom line though, it also had a lot to do with personal behaviors with their latest tech gadgets and the long-term exposures for many decades in the works... since mobile phones really took off. As the Mitochondriacs from the KMA had known a decade before that event, lowered redox and calcium effluxing were at the root.

Interviewer: That one event really got the public to start asking deeper questions about the biophysics, instead of keeping focused on the biochemistry.

Ray Oflite: Personally, I think that was just the start of it. It still took several years for the public to really grasp what was at work around them... in personal physics spaces. I can attest for it with my Bone Armor line because those few years following Superbowl 54 was when my profit margins really tanked.

Interviewer: Really? You were still in the spotlight and everything appeared fine.

Ray Oflite: No, not at all. Our marketing campaign painted that illusion to the public. Narrative is simple. I look back on it and wished I had come clean about those losses in the height of my fame.

Interviewer: So are you implying that the plate implants cut deep into your profit margins?

Ray Oflite: (Laughs). No, The Bonesters ended up being some of my largest customers! It was still common for 20 year-old's to have calcium leeched from their bodies without them knowing why, so it was still useful and a cheaper option than surgery.

Interviewer: You know, I can safely say that you're no bonehead after this interview series.

Ray Oflite: Thanks! It was fun, let's do this again.

Cimate and in things within that sphere no? And the things within the sphere that live on the surface have cavities don't they?

Would you be concerned that these cavities conditions and the conditions of the climate within that sphere might be affected by the microwaves in ways few others do? Might climate deniers be the cover for geo-engineering?

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electron\_transport\_chain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electron_transport_chain)

Outer **(mitochondrial)** membrane.
Inner **(mitochondrial)** membrane.

**Still looking for a place that give details of membranes,
cellular, mitochondrial, and other.
how they go awry, and how to restore them to health.
So far I got impression that mitochondrial membrane is just there,
not taking part in what Complexes (I-IV and Q) are doing.
That likely is not the case, parts of membrane's content is likely being exchanged in the process and requires renewal and replacement.**

The electron transport chain in the mitochondrion is the site of [oxidative phosphorylation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oxidative_phosphorylation) in [eukaryotes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eukaryote). The NADH and succinate generated in the [citric acid cycle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Citric_acid_cycle) are oxidized, providing energy to power [ATP synthase](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ATP_synthase).

Interior 1960s glass @ 10am: 13 mW/Sq m (almost UVB1)
Interior 1960s glass @ noon: 7 mW/Sq m (UVB 0)

I had clouds come in at noon vs. 10am with blue-sky. Still, getting UVB through the glass inside the home is AMAZINGSAUCE, considering I have a LOT of glass and vertical glass skylight panels in every room.

up to 55% of a stool sample is bacterial biomass
the mucosal surface contains a microbiota that is significantly different to that found in a stool sample

in the majority of cases humans are born sterile
and immediately upon exit from the mother start to be colonized by microbes
\================================
The current census of the inhabitants of the distal gut: the early years of the distal gut

The adult and ageing distal gut microbiota

A bacterial-centric view that needs to encompass all the microbiota

The luminal microbiota versus the mucosal microbiota

Is there a core microbiome?

The mobile microbiome or mobilome

The distal gut microbiome as a driver of health and disease
Some of the most commonly seen bacterial metabolites in the human gut are the SCFA,
**butyrate,
acetate,
lactate and
propionate**
{Saric, 2008 #15051}.
The two bacterial groups that are mainly responsible for producing butyrate are the
_F. prauznitzii_ and
_Eubacterium rectale_/_Roseburia_ groups
{Louis, 2010 #17389; Louis, 2009 #15805}. This metabolite has been implicated in large array of effects in the intestine that include controlling apoptosis, cytokine production, energy for colonocytes and mucus synthesis {Guilloteau, 2010 #17009}. Hence any changes in these groups would potentially have an impact on this function and host physiology.

### Scompy — 2016-12-29T18:23:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-205885>

Quantum Qaos (imagine an echoing sound effect)

Objective: You must get through the obstacles of modern life. If you survive to the age of 30 without obtaining a disease, you win!

Sounds easy, right? Not so fast. Each Stage is divided up into different timespans. Retry as many times as you wish to see how long you can survive. As you make a CHOICE, try not to cheat. Just read the answer you deem logical at the time, or just randomly guess. \[If you like this game and want more, I will expand it to a higher age\] For the parental inclined, I'd consider this rated PG17, with a hint of R on top. (Look for the "=-=-=" between each section).

\=-=-= SECTION A BELOW

STAGE A: NEWBIE (Newborn) After birth, your parents surf online constantly. Dad placed the router behind your bedroom wall where your crib sits next to an electrified wall. As new parents, they are pretty fearful because you get sick a lot more often than other babies. Parents have been influenced by a vegan aunt that has raised several children to get the kid on soy formula. To ease fears and lower redox, doctors has you on an accelerated vaccine schedule.

Somewhat alarmed by any noise you make at night, Parents have just bought the latest radio-frequency baby monitors that are placed next to a shelf close to the Smart Meter that sits just outside your room, aside from the mobile phone tower down the block. Mom keeps the windows closed and curtains drawn to prevent the car fumes from getting in the home. However, she does get you outside to the park every few days with a hat, sunglasses and sunscreen to provide about 1.73% light exposure daily. With the little myelin you have and the skull-cap you have yet to form, you have some available options to you.

CHOICES available for STAGE NEWBIE:
1\. SCREAM
2\. SLEEP
3\. VOMIT
4\. 1AM
5\. CRY

1\. SCREAM: You've done a great job to increase your parents cortisol and stress levels gradually over time. Mom instinctively knows something isn't quite right, so she has been freaking out about not breastfeeding. She finally gives in, sees a lactate specialist and starts up th sn-2 DHA for some seriously protective yum. For this stage, you've barely warded off a neurological condition. You can move onto the next stage. However, you're having serious problems creating any new myelin at this point. Lucky for you, your skull cap has fully formed a bit earlier than normal and that extra calcium layer has prevented you from a longer period of neurological damage.

2\. SLEEP: With all of the latest and greatest tech going on in the home and the bright nightlight in the room, you're having a hard time sleeping. So why not try to sleep a lot more and decrease the parent's stress a bit? Unfortunately , this strategy leaves you more hungry and less time to eat overall. Plus, the parents think it's abnormal you are sleeping so much and take you into the doctors more frequenly. You must retry this stage because the continual bathing of non-native electromagnetic and fluorescent lighting on weekly trips to the hospital and lack of protective fats shuts you down and starts up autism.

3\. VOMIT: Continually heaving does give your mom the strong impression that the soy formula is not to your liking. However, they switch formula again to a highly processed formula with added glutamate and preservatives. Thinking vomiting might be a problem, you are smart to invoke diarrhea also. Unfortunately, mom decides to start heating milk in the microwave. Now that both parents are freaked out and nothing is staying down for several weeks, you realize you've also become excitotoxic and dehydrated to the point of disease formation. With the next vaccine shots and blue-light exposures, you realize it ain't going too well and you decide to acquire Asthma and Epilepsy. Now you must retry the challenge from scratch. Kind of sad your parents also used distilled water to mix in the formula.

4\. 1AM: You are a smart kid in the making. You STOP CRYING and decide to giggle at 1am exactly 1 minute after dad typically shuts down the computer back on the back-side of your crib. You do this for several weeks. Dad finally sees a pattern. He stays up until 2:20am, but at 1am you don't giggle. Dad comes in to see why you didn't laugh. At 2:20pm when the computer shuts down and you giggle, he finally gets it through his thick skull that you might be giggling at the computer shutting down rather than seeing him. So dad tries and experiment and shuts down the computer at 11pm the next night and finds out you are happy with some of the electronics off at night that cause magnetic and electrical fields into your crib. The laughing also got mom to be so happy that she takes a stab at breastfeeding and it sticks. Congrats, you have prevented an autoimmune disease for now. You can move onto the next Stage. I forgot to mention that Dad, being hyper-protective, steps it up a notch and turns off the router within 2 feet of your crib. You laugh for minutes to let him know he's a genius. You forget to thank him 20 years later though.

5\. CRY: You know what babies do best. Cry. Because of all of the manmade electromagnetics around you, it's so obvious to cry as much as you can to let your dimwitted parents know you don't feel great. Mom feels a bit guilty for not breastfeeding because of her aunt's input, but thinks you just may need more love and snuggling. She kicks Dad out of bed and has you sleep next to her, because for some reason, she notices you stop crying when you sleep in her bed after formula feeding her there. You know the truth though. That formula sucks pretty bad, but at least you are not continually in artificial electromagnetic fields on a 24/7 basis and can sleep better with the light off. Although dad is a little peeved and is getting less loving 24/7, you've pushed off a disease for now, but you still get sick due to higher inflammation. A few weeks later, Dad is quite irate to have been sleeping on the couch, and mom feel guilty. You are placed back in your crib and come down with daily migraines and sleep disturbances. Although your parents generally think you are okay, you are too far down the road toward a future disease. You must retry for a better choice.

### Scompy — 2016-12-29T18:24:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-205886>

\=-=-= SECTION B BELOW (START IN SECTION A)

STAGE B: TODDLER (1 year old) You have managed to side-skirt some non-native EMFs at this point and sucked down some sn2-DNA, but since your teeth have come in, your parents think your health is entirely based on the food you get now. You've gone a full year with very little sunlight and when you get outside, they like to slather on some type of weird poisonous lotion to lower redox a bit and block out all Vitamin D production. For that reason now that winter is approaching, you feel a bit under the weather. Additionally, they've got you a foot in front of the Television twice a day to watch educational videos and mom constantly chats on the cell phone allowing you to babytalk to all of her friends. This stimulation has made you want to drool and eat her phone, but mom just lets you play games on it instead. What to do?

CHOICES available for STAGE TODDLER:
1\. TOY
2\. ACROBATICS
3\. COLORS
4\. SUCK
5\. SWING

1\. TOY: You decide to figure out what toy you like best. Although you like the ball the best, parents think you could get hurt no it. Instead, they keep shoving this insense-flashy rectangle into your hands that electrifies you into submission. Dad says to mom, "This will be a great, continual distraction and education tool at the same time! It connects easily to WiFi anywhere, like on the plane trips we are planning. Plus, it will give us more time together...if you get my drift." Although you know this gadget is going to be a big problem, resistance is pointless and the play-pad is too bright to ignore. You have no choice but to become addicted to this tech toy to the point when mom or dad attempts to take it away, you shriek as if the world has fallen into oblivion. It doesn't take a few months before you obtain several new Psychiatric disorders and insulin resistance that starts making you overweight. You're too far gone to move to the next stage because of what's coming. Good job! You believe you will fit in perfectly as a standardized citizen of the future.

2\. ACROBATICS: When you are at the park, you develop your survival skills like you're a monkey in the trees. When your parents notice how acrobatic you are, they let you do go hog-wild apestuff on the grass in the sunlight. You figure out if you roll around a lot, that poisonous lotion gets rubbed off on the grass while you ground. Because you get a bit overheated, parents realize it's better you just wear a t-shirt instead. The pink-cheeks also let your parents know to hydrate you when they are finished playing with you at the park. Also, they bathe you in a metal bath that happens to have grounded plumming, unbeknownst to their brains. Sun, water and grounding for the win! You're still surviving and can move onto the next stage. Strange enough, parents feel a lot better also and they have no clue why.

3\. COLORS: Mom and dad notices that when you are thrown in front of a TV several times a day, you become mesmerized by blocky colors on kid's shows. The TV not only makes a great babysitter and gives more play-time for the parents to surf the internet randomly. Although you feel a bit neglected at times, the colors become your best friend. Typically, mom and dad leave the TV on later at night after work and when they start getting lethargic as well. Soon, your circadian rhythm crashes because you rarely get outside into the morning sunlight and watch so much TV after dusk. As you can guess, you're not making it beyond this stage. You first develop chronic fatigue and sleep issues, but that later manifests into a perpetual brain fog and the potential for some very bad. You fall behind in your learning progression and memorization abilities, ironically, as you watch educational TV shows.

4\. SUCK: Sucking your thumb has been a pastime for many, many months. Mom likes the sign of this because it means the breastfeeding is about to end. However, you are a smart kid, did I mention that yet? You figure out that your teeth are kid of abrasive to mom, and go more gently on her. Mom realizes you are always calm and happy after a meal, so she continues breastfeeding close to the age of 2. Woohoo, some win and win for some win-sauce! You can't believe that worked and you're able to move onto the next stage. But now you are a little bit of afraid what it's gonna be like in a year or two.

5\. SWING: When you are taken to the park, you laugh so much that your parents keep you on the swing for longer periods of time in the sunlight. For some strange reason, you notice that your parents use chemical-added disinfectant wipes to wipe down the sunlight-cleaned swing chairs. Oh well, at least you're enjoying the outdoors and your hat keeps flying off your head due to the wind. You get smart and play-up the crying when they take you out of the swing to make the time last even longer. Somehow you feel very grounded on this swing... oh wait, you are actually grounded for real because that metal chain is connected to the metal pole in the ground for extra electrons. Eventually you just make it through the next year with enough Vitamin D production to ward off a modern disease, for now at least. You also made friends with some other kids in swings adjacent to you, and formed a swing alliance to meet-up with the kids at the same time each day at the park.

\=-=-= SECTION C BELOW

STAGE C: TERRIBLE (2 years old) In this stage, you only have two options, because it's gonna be terrible if you choose wrongly. Your parents are at their wits end and have been chronically sleep-deprived for well over 2 years. They are also wondering how they survived, let alone you. Now it is all or nothing.

CHOICES available for STAGE TERRIBLE:
1\. ONESIES
2\. TWO'S

1\. ONESIES: Now that you have grown out of your onesies, your parents must get you an entirely new wardrobe. Mothers believes that because autumn is coming, you need to be kept extremely warm and protected from the cold. They get some extra thermal protection even though it rarely drops below 50F at night. They always keep you in long-sleeve shirts, long pants and typically coats. In addition, they put a lot of covers on you at night as well so you over heat. Now that breastfeeding is done and you're not get ablet to sip down lots of fluids, you become dehydrated for months on-end. You chose poorly and must choose the other option. As the sunlight wanes during winter, you become depressed, come down with alleriges to every food under the sunless-sky, and develop more Electro Hypersensitivity to non-native electromagnetic fields. More electrons steadily leave your body over this year until spring-time when you are diagnosed with a neurological condition.

2\. TWO'S: It's your terrible two's, or should be. But why do you have to make it so terrible for everyone? You know why many kids get so terrible. It's no wonder. You often visit other kids and see their tech toys, but for some reason, your parents haven't saturated you in that stuff...at least not yet. When you visit other kids, you go rampage on their tech toys and break several cell phones and pads. Although you broke-off relationships with the kids and your parents have to pay for repairs, you are no longer exposed to their blue-light, manmade EMF-emitting devices. Mom and dad keep you at home for now. The new babysitter often opens the windows when she takes care of you and also gets you outside a lot. Somehow, you make it through your terrible twos without a disease...but still with some damage and inflammation.

### Scompy — 2016-12-29T18:25:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-205887>

\=-=-= SECTION D BELOW (START IN SECTION A)

STAGE D: KID (7 years old) Your parents have thought twice about putting you in 1st grade in the local school district and decided to move to a smaller town twenty miles away. Lucky for you, the nearest mobile tower is 7 miles away! However, mom and dad have you on the 7th floor of an apartment complex far away from grounding. Another problem...over 20 nearby neighbors use 24/7 connected mobile phones. Due to the long-distance away to the phone tower, more energy is required to make a connection, which translates into more mitochondrial destruction to your small body. How do you think you will prevent the next disease?

CHOICES available for STAGE KID:
1\. SOCCER
2\. GRANDPARENTS
3\. GAMES

1\. SOCCER: You show interest in playing soccer (that's futbol to the rest of the world), but your parents are afraid to get you outside into the sunlight. But this isn't the generation of playing sports in the cold and mud. They elect for an indoor soccer league at 7pm under fluorescent lighting on artificial turf that prevents grounding. It doesn't long to realize you are out of breath continually on the field and one of the worst players on your team due to the hypoxia. After a minor twist, you tear your ACL tendon and it requires surgery. Consider this choice a fail because the doctor also says you are already experiencing signs of bone loss as a six-year old.

2\. GRANDPARENTS: Mom and dad are pretty worn out keeping up with your strange ADD-like behaviors. They elect to send you for a summer to gramps in a remote town in northern Arizona. As old-timers, they don't understand all of the latest rage with tech. Instead, Papa teaches you how to play chess at his home. He gets you on his schedule waking up with the sun and taking a morning walk. For some reason, gramps likes to play outside on a metal table with lemonaid under the sun. Because games often take over an hour, you are continually grounded, get a ton of sunlight and drink EZ-charged lemonaid. You are able to move to the next stage.

3\. GAMES: Because you are emotinally moody, mom and dad agree to get you the latest game console to distract you more. The wireless controller is amazing at driving up your blood pressure, especially when the rumble kicks in with each game. After getting really competent with your thumb-eye coordination, parents are much happier witnessing your progress. Of course, they let you play well into the evening to crash your melatonin and wonder why it takes you an extra hour each night to unwind. Even though you wake up each morning with dark circles under your eyes, they give you high sugar foods to wake you up--typically some pancakes or waffles with agave nectar or a bowl of cereal. A year later, the doctor believes you have become a Type II Diabetic and starts you on insulin. Starting a disease at this age means you'll be lucky to make it to the age of 65. Consider yourself toast, with lots of honey and jam spread on top.

\=-=-= SECTION E BELOW

STAGE E: SCHOOLER (14 years old)
Your life doesn't look all roses walking to school, with some wilting inside of it. The latest school push was to install WiFi into the building for pulsing nnEMF into the brains of the staff members, faculty and students for optimal mitochondrial degregation. It looks like tech tablets for homework, routers by your teacher's desk, artificial lights overhead and shades usually pulled down in most classrooms at school. Because of the school district's policy to 'help?' students, they've limited recess to just once a day to minimize sun exposure. Now how is this going to work?

CHOICES available for STAGE SCHOOLER:
1\. nnEMFs
2\. CFLs
3\. LEDs
\[Really writer, you're giving me these choices?!\]

1\. nnEMFs: Okay, so now that you are a little worried about acquiring autism at this rate, but your strategy here is to bust out some EHS on the school's principal because the doctor said you had it a few years back. It took you a good month for them to read the literature on Electromagnetic Hypersensitivity Syndrome to realize it isn't an imaginary disease. Even though those pesky administrations won't wire their school for better connectivity and upload/download rates, they have moved your desk in the classrooms as far away from the routers as possible. Interesting enough, they usually put you by the windows. You also started wearing a thicker hoodie and using it all of the time for a little more radio-frequency EMF mitigation. Congrats to you. The next stage is available.

2\. CFLs: Not only are the overhead lights a problem in the schools, you also get them into the evening. Dad installed new ones. The city planners also put CFLs outside on the streets to disrupt your sleep. Psoriasis and then Fibromyalgia or Hashimoto's Disease are in your future. However, if you also omit the sun for a little longer, you could try for something like Multiple Sclerosis. Try again, you must.

3\. LEDs: To save money for the school, they decided to replace the fluorescent lighting with newer LEDs. Unfortunately, the blue-light spectrum is broader and more effective at spreading blue light at all times of the day. Several of your teachers are influenced by flickering LEDs at their desks as well. It's not just the crashing your melatonin levels for the day, you're getting some spontaneous seizures and continual headaches to help you flunk some of your classes. More bad on the horizon, so consider this option is no bueno.

### Scompy — 2016-12-29T18:25:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-205888>

\=-=-= SECTION F BELOW (START IN SECTION A)

STAGE F: POSTGRAD (22 years old)
You made it this far, why turn back now? Somehow you've dodged a childhood problems and are kicking iup your love life. Coming into adulthood has been a bit of a challenge for whatever reason. You're not sure it's the times you are living in, but everyone seems distant in an overly-connected world. How could this be? Where is the wooing these days?

CHOICES available for STAGE POSTGRAD:
1\. PANEL
2\. MOVIES
3\. SEX

1\. PANEL: After consulting with your doc and some vampirish bloodwork, it appears your hormone panel is more than crashed after seeing the test results. They want you to supplement with Vitamin D and get your hormones balanced by additional supplementation to improve iodine, magnesium, potassium and so forth. They are even considering starting you on a few powerful pharmaceuticals to help. Because you are not that knowledgeable on all of this junk your doctor is advising and it also seems to cost a pretty penny as a recent college grad, you decide to go on vacation to a tropical island and eat a lot of fish, crab and try their local food. In actuality, knowing you haven't hooked up with anyone in quite some time due to your crashed libido and excessive studying until 4am every night for the past 4 years. You can continue to the next Stage because this vacation has recharged your health back from beyond the brink.

2\. MOVIES: It's sad times when you find yourself going to the movies alone, taking selfies and sending text-messages to yourself when looking for a date. The pain \*feels\* real enough, but is it just an illusion? After all of that texting, your mitochondria are in a lot of real pain in a struggle to preserve lowered energy, but strangely, your interpretation is depression and wanting to sleep away the tears. Out of all of the fish in the sea, there is not one to eat. Suddenly, a potential hook comes right up to you and stares at you with a smile in the movie theater with phone in hand! Could this be the perfect match? It appears the stranger has just caught a "virtual videogame creature" hiding beneath your seat and cheers his win to you! You don't pass up the opportunity to introduce yourself. Within a week, you hook up, but within a month, you're broken up again due to OCD-like behaviors exhibited that you cannot stand. As you continue to go to the movies alone, you don't realize that being pulsed with over 100 radio-frequency phones inside the theater for 3 hours at a time could be a problem to your health making this a pattern. Consider yourself microwaved and vibrated in all of the wrong ways. Now you must pick a better option.

3\. SEX: You decided sex was gonna be a thing...how predictable. The writer is guessing this was also your first choice. Although it is true that the survival of humanity depends on this three-letter word, you could have considered the Panel option because of the mysterious word choice. I'm done listening to your justifications here...I'm failing you outright for picking sex. You should have known instantly this option would be a fail. The mind is also a powerful resource for survival. Choose a better option.

\=-=-= LAST STAGE: SECTION G BELOW

STAGE G: WTF (30 years old)
Now that you made it this far and met your true love on a tropical island, you wake up from the dream and realize the nightmare that you have to pay down more than a hundred thousand in college debt. You find the first job that comes along that pays well, move into the city and start feeling somewhat demyelinnated by it all. At least you have a good income, and who knows, maybe you'll find the love of your life at this new work environment that can help pay down your accrued debts. The plan is rock-solid. Here are your options.

CHOICES available for STAGE WTF:
1\. MONEY
2\. DRUGS
3\. ROCK

1\. MONEY: Always a good choice. Scratch that. Usually a good choice. But in this case, it's turns out to be altogether wrong. You must choose another option, but first you probably want to know why the money be a big, fat fail. As it turns out, you find an interesting, luring attraction to the Lottery and gambling machines at the local casino-boat. After seeing that fresh paycheck, you decide to take it to the next level baby: VEGAS! The flashing neons, the free buffets, old singers, what's not to like?! Well Craps, you find that something is driving you to play, play and play all the money away. Yay! Already 100,000 grand in debt from college, there's not much more to lose, right?! RIGHT?! There's no turning back now! What could go wrong. After a weekend in a daze and waking up completely naked (aside from a Fitness magazine to cover the privates) in an unknown apartment having lipstick written all over your body in odd locations with $$$ $ymbol$, you realize it sounds a lot like a bad movie plot. No matter, the blue-light addiction over time leads you dependencies, brain fog and early-onset Alzheimer's Disease with a chocolatey hint of vaporized Dementia. You are still wondering why "SEX" wasn't an option here...considering all of that nakedness and lipstick. After all, it would have been "Sex, Drugs and Rock". That would have been closer to something meaningful at least.

2\. DRUGS: Because the stress of your job is abnormally high and those previous labs came back hormonally awkward, you decide the best course of action is to supplement. Screw the drugs altogether. The doctor's advice is just gonna be a bandage. How could those all-natural supplements be anything but good. You realize you can work indoors all of the time, pop pills for your health and find a mate soon enough. But strangely, you end up gaining a lot of weight and need a new wardrobe. A co-worker suggests to sign up for the discounted company gym plan. You lose about 10 pounds at first and feel ultra-motivated to slim down for attracting your future spouse. However, the only time to workout is around 9pm after a hard day when the electric treadmills are available. The only drawback is the sweat people leave behind on them, but that's what a towel is for after all! But doing this for a year has gotten you obese. The irony! What's going on? If only you had learned about the chronobiology no one dare teach you in college. Oh well, you know the phrase, "No pain, no gain". Seems like that's flipped somehow..."In pain, lotta gain" of weight that is. After switching up breakfast to a jar of sugar with a tablespoon of concentrated coffee, you realize insulin resistance could be an issue. Congratulations on achieving early Type 2 Diabetes to the light physics you have misused by omitting the sun from your life and embracing artificial lighting thereby crashing your Vitamin D status for calcium channel havoc in your insulin mad world.

3\. ROCK: And Roll you say? Well no. This is the correct option... I know, you are flabbergasted? Here is why! On your lunch breaks at work, you found a little sushi restaurant just on the outskirts of the city. For some reason, you're strangely attracted to the spicy eel and a few other people that often show up and attend the joint. You follow one person, with chopsticks in hand, over to a bridge. What's weird is that the person you are attracted to doesn't sit on the bridge at all, but on an rock outcropping to get a good view of things. You are smart how you're gonna catch this fish. The next day, you decide to get your sushi earlier and take a seat on that same rock outcropping before your future lover sits there. In the direct sunlight over the noon hour, you produce a lot of Vitamin D lovin' while grounding all at once. But for some reason, your lover doesn't arrive. You rinse and repeat this pattern for a week, but still no lover, even though you really have come to like this rock. After several weeks, your lover finally shows up, but sees you sitting there. Your future spouse looks too shy and starts to walk away when you shout out, "Rock my world!" The fish is caught. You both eat sushi together on that rock and start a new friendship.

\=-=-= THE END, FIN, ALL DONE, OKAY?

### Scompy — 2016-12-30T20:20:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-205927>

> [Debbieinbend said:](/goto/post?id=205909)
>
> Scompy,
>
> Interesting thread. I have a question for you. I learned yesterday where I get sun at lunch is less than 10' from a smart meter. Do you know how far away I need to be to be safe from it?
>
> Thanks,

Ten feet away is too close because the intensities coming off of them are so strong. The falloffs here would be similar to a microwave when it is in use and the scattering of light from it. I'd say more like 20 to 25 feet could still be detected to a point, but the levels would be low enough (falling below 1 milliWatt per Square meter). I like to see environments below 0.05 milliWatts per Square meter. That is x100 compared to 0.0005 milliWatts per Square meter when RF is pretty well bottomed out.

### Penny — 2016-12-30T21:00:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-205931>

Wow Scompy, I just read that whole thing - thank God we found a day care lady who had the kids in the sun all day... then the Montessori school continued the tradition - I took them to the park all the time after school - I breastfed my youngest until almost 2 years old - he would see me after work and say "boob?"... it was hilarious - he was never sick - when all the other kids at day care were sick, he either caught a mild version of it or didn't get it at all - once I stopped however, kill health goodbye... we were so proud of our clever first born too - at 2 he could load whatever program he wanted to on Windows... so many "educational" games - he was allergic to milk so like an idiot I put him on soy formula - he was hyper from birth - I was a lot calmer though - I attributed it to the exhaustion of having a baby at 38... what I believe now is I transferred my incredible mercury load to him...

My youngest was so sick at school - plus he had a wireless keyboard/mouse - my oldest figured out in elementary school that if he drank 1/2 gallon of water while at school, he could mitigate the headaches - my youngest would pour the bottled water onto the ground so I would think he drank it...

I finally ended up home schooling the youngest one but sadly, it is an online school and he is on the computer 14 hours per day - the oldest one is constantly playing video games while he is home - they are no longer running flux on their computers either - the oldest went to jury duty recently - I asked him to get hand scanned and he laughed at me - but when he went there, unbelievably, he asked to be hand scanned and even more unbelievably they did it without an act of congress... so I continue to tell them in the fond hopes that when they get whatever disease they get, they remember my constant blathering about sunlight, my love fest with nature and seafood...

The hell of it is they both want to be software engineers - can't wait for that - years ago I took a trip with them - the youngest one felt so much better in nature - once we arrived back to civilization, it all went to shit again - I never in a million years would have figured it was the electricity - Jack Kruse for the win

We are so lucky to live in Ventura California where yesterday it was 82 degrees - we are surrounded by lemon and avocado orchards and weirdly, strawberries popped up 2 weeks ago... yum I can't imagine the state of our health had we not lived in a sunny climate in a small town by the ocean -

Funny story - and maybe an idea for some of you - for Christmas I got my son's girlfriend a picnic backpack so they could go on picnics together - I find that once kids realize how fun it is to go outside they go outside - they just need a little nudge sometimes - because dopamine is at an all time low...

I got this one:
[https://www.amazon.com/VonShef-Back...id=1483124381&sr=8-3&keywords=picnic+backpack](https://www.amazon.com/VonShef-Backpack-Compartment-Detachable-Flatware/dp/B00L55DL2I/ref=sr_1_3?ie=UTF8&qid=1483124381&sr=8-3&keywords=picnic+backpack)

Key Point: In the coherent phase, the water molecules oscillate between two electronic configurations in phase with a resonating EMF. You should make sure that EMF is sun light and not blue light or a nnEMF. Sunlight drives the idealized electronic configuaration life needs to work wirelessly with the sun. When the sun sets the temperatures drop and water cools and its density changes. This works better with the magnetic flux increase that occurs at night in miotchondria.So drink your water un-fluoridated and COLD! You might be beginning to understand how the quilt is built now. Limiting fluoride increases the EZ in water by lowering the dielectric constant. Water that is cooled at night is capable of carrying more oxygen and electrons in its hydrogen bonding network to enlarge the EZ in water. Mitochondria needs oxygen and electrons for their inner mitochondrial membrane. Water is a repository of electromagnetic radiations. Water is designed to work wirelessly with the sun, but its abilities can be usurped by man made EMF's. In a blue lit microwaved world EZ water is the ultimate Faraday cage for mitochondria in a cell.

Water allows the quantum dance to happen in all life, not just us. We are just beginning to unfold its mysteries and why it is vital to all life on this planet. Beta oxidation in mitochondria not only makes CO2 but it creates water!!! Water is the stage life is built upon.

Water is what makes all plants grow via photosynthesis. All animals need water to survive. When water falls from the sky as rain, it is distilled; at least it used to be before our atmosphere was polluted by modern chemicals and EMF's in the ionsophere.

If you use the photoelectric effect of the sun to enlarge the charge in cell water, and add grounding to Earth, your needs and want for water will begin to approach your aquatic ancestors and your brain will repair and re-grow faster. This is quite important in diseases like Autism and Alzheimer's disease. Signs of leptin sensitivity begin to appears and labs start to improve. ### Scompy — 2017-02-04T05:49:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-207916>

What is a poison? (By Scompy)

Many definitions say something like this, "a substance with an inherent property that tends to destroy life or impair health."

However, my definition is more like: "Something that can cause an imbalance in a specific process or system so it does not function well or function at all."

Notice, this could be anything that interferes with a process that if that process or mechanism is thrown out of balance, can cause problems.. some only a ripple influence, some minor, some major, or even death.

In thinking about the application of poisons and how they intersect us, this is why I could consider ANYTHING a poison if it can result in a negative influence considering both the frequency of application and the intensity of it. Sure many foods in the extreme like high-carbs in high volumes, etc. Sure nnEMFs. Sure artificial lighting and unexpected light-timing. But I also want to point out HOW we live (and trying to be healthier) can end up kicking us in the butt also. This includes too much sun at once to get burned and blistered, staring at the sun for too long, extreme over-hydration, staying in the cold for too long, exercising too hard or long without proper training, fasting for too long, etc.

I'm not trying to be controversial, but just realistic that each individual has different conditions in their worlds and in-play. Balancing individual systems can change with resistances and have dependencies on a whole host of other mechanisms. I view this all as a moving target to know the current state of poison resistance (when understanding a known poison).

It's like from the Princess Bride movie and the talk about illusion of Iocane Powder... The Dread Pirate Roberts builds up an immunity to the poison over very long periods of time (years or decades) via slowly building tolerance, just like a person needs to build a skin callus over the spring to not let summer sunlight burn the skin. This is an analog for hormesis and figuring out at what intensity and exposure something slightly poisonous actually can benefit us. Without resistance, we couldn't build muscle, so there's another analogy.

But when a person wishes to run a marathon and hasn't yet built up the lung-capacity... maybe the person can only run 3 miles at best... would you recommend it's fine for that person to run the marathon the following weekend? Yes we can build resistances to some types of poisons and overcome them by building our redox potential, lowering heteroplasmy and lowering our inflammation potential.

I like to keep in mind that in some individuals, there could be a cascade of underlying issues that makes the poison work better, because of other poisons synergizing together, causing like x9 cogs to go bad versus just that 1 cog in the whole system. The interplay and connections can be often far and deep, and the variables even among specific tissue types still need a lot of internal exploration to find out how certain poisons intersect with them.

Case in point with nnEMFs: I like to think about what we know and don't know about specific frequencies and how they interact in tissues. We see a ton of glioblastomas appearing with phone use, but maybe there's a connection not only with intensity and frequency of use, but the specific poison itself, like a specific frequency range, that may play an additional role to have the poison act faster as a dielectric blocker, or have a longer falloff after the exposure time.

For all of the variables in-play, I see the most reasonable course of action for any poison, whether it is cigarette smoke, alcohol, even a supplement dose, any dang thing that could offset a balance, and possibly offset the balance at the wrong time of day or even the wrong season to influence the systems we have built in us. I view everything through this lens now... nothing is \*immune\* to the concept that it could be a poison under certain conditions, durations or intensities--including the medicines we claim to be so.

But I want to stress this last part..., on the flipside of the equation, the omission of things can act to swing the balance the other direction, and arguably, it's more easy to accomplish... the lack of sun for so many diseases is the poison administered because it's a gate for many aromatic compounds, EZ water, etc. Omission of enough water (not hydrating enough) is now a huge thing, especially in modern environments that dehydrate us more frequently... wonderful synergies at work. nnEMFs are the hardest 'sell' to nearly everyone around us, because as a poison, they appear to be a slow-moving poison like cigarette smoke damage in many, and can act to be a fast-moving poison in others, such as those with EHS.

I'll end with my grandfather... he died at the age of 90 in the early 1990s. He fought as a soldier in World War I on the frontlines of France and got mustard gas poisoning. The mustard gas gave him many problems in the months after, but he recovered and lived a full life. However, around the age of 87, he started having lung issues. Guess what? The damage he received from World War I came back again and still had the influence to take his life. The poison was both a short-term one that he thought had passed... but it turned into a long-term problem due to its influence upon his cogs.

### Scompy — 2017-02-07T17:48:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-208131>

Here is the way I see it. In my N=1, this is where the I.F. (Intermittent Fast) is amazing and is profound. If a person gets enough nutrient density in one meal, and leaves it only to 1 or 2 meals a day, it will tap energy reserves 12+ hours into the fast. My IF days typically went 18 hours. On the days I pushed it closer to 1 meal (like a 20 hour day), the next morning I was in a very strong ketosis. Typically 2 or 3 pounds of fat would melt off me rinsing and repeating. For balance and a bit more nutrient density coming back into the body (mainly for the K+), I broke the fast once per week for a 12/12 day. In a person already extremely obese though, there are a lot of trapped poisons that have got to be liberated with the excess fuel release and blood ketones The purging of those poisons needs a lot more hydration also to make the dilution mixture lower to minimize damage to the kidneys, liver, etc.

Yes I agree... I think it takes time for adjustments and really testing one's N=1. Lotta people give up too early. I didn't find IF to be effective in the first 2 months until I was very disciplined in my behaviors and eating and being honest with what I was putting in my mouth.

For the women I've personally known, for some reason it appears taking a fast too long can be problematic for them for whatever reasons (probably many are underlying with thyroid, estrogen, lack of sun, etc.). One woman I know did best doing 14 hour fasts maximum because she was getting shaky. Regulation of blood glucose and insulin point to the lack of sun, but it's complex, right? See seemed to do better with a low-carb/keto approach without IF. However, I love one huge meal a day and then fasting for the rest of the day. Time saver also, and because I choose to eat a lot of seafood and higher potassium-rich foods, I don't get any issues anymore at all...

Jan I didn’t think the recipe section was part of the article. It just looks like a recipe page on a newsletter. But in any event I think leptin resistance is a separate issue, and in terms of IF or fasting, we have to look at the wider picture. My thinking has been influenced by
[https://www.jackkruse.com/intermittent-fasting-and-leptin/](https://www.jackkruse.com/intermittent-fasting-and-leptin/)

Also, hunger doesn't just pop up when someone stands next to an open refrigerator full of favourite food. And a scale saying I'm too fat to eat that day is a form of self-torture. I'm also a role model for my children, particularly DD, and my granddaughters. My mother role modelled getting on the scales, because she didn't know the consequences. I do. I would be horrified if I saw any of my family doing what you suggest, I wouldn't want them to think it was "normal" to do it, either in front of the refrigerator, or in the bathroom. I have a choice - Even if it’s taking me longer to reach my goals, I choose to explain to my family about leptin resistance and that sometimes it isn't cured overnight, or magically initiate fast weight loss, particularly as a post-menopausal woman in the environment we now face. It doesn't make me undisciplined, or stupid.

I will never again let the number on a scale dictate my mood or my actions for the next 24 hours until I get on it again, to rinse and repeat. That would be dumb enough, but knowing I would be role modelling an eating disorder to future generations of females in my family….? Not a chance.

### Scompy — 2017-02-24T07:33:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-209188>

**A little experiment:**
After taking this video, I actually went up to the woman that pulled up in her car, smiled and showed her my Cornet meter. I was getting about 40 milliWatts per square meter on average about 2 feet away from her. She was actively using her cellphone and acted quite distracted, but finally looked up. I decided to volunteer a little info to her and said, "Hi there... I am doing a fun biophysics lesson right now before I go into the school... this meter says I'm getting pretty high RF EMFs from your cell phone,,,the radio frequency radiation coming off of it...probably because we're so far away from a cell tower to make a stronger connection." She looked quite perplexed and said, "What is radio frequency?" I answered back, "that's what your cell phone is doing to communicate... you may want to look that up later because my meter is showing quite unhealthy levels..." I left it there and walked inside to get my kid. She didn't look concerned.
