### Scompy — 2017-12-09T07:44:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-229845>

It is often useful to study the simplest system. Therefore, hydrogen, the simplest nucleus, has been studied extensively. The isotopes of hydrogen show many of the effects found in more complicated nuclei. (The word isotope refers to a nucleus with the same _Z_ but different _A_).
There are three isotopes of the element hydrogen: hydrogen, deuterium, and tritium. How do we distinguish between them? They each have one single proton (_Z_ = 1), but differ in the number of their neutrons. Hydrogen has no neutron, deuterium has one, and tritium has two neutrons. The isotopes of hydrogen have, respectively, mass numbers of one, two, and three. Their nuclear symbols are therefore 1H, 2H, and 3H. The atoms of these isotopes have one electron to balance the charge of the one proton. Since chemistry depends on the interactions of protons with electrons, the chemical properties of the isotopes are nearly the same.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The curve of the average binding energy per nucleon.

Energy may be released as a packet of electromagnetic radiation, a photon. Photons created in nuclear processes are labeled gamma rays (denoted by the Greek letter gamma, g. For example, when a proton and neutron combine to form deuterium, the reaction can be written 1n + 1H Æ 2H + g. Energy must balance in this equation. Mass can be written in atomic mass units (u) or in the equivalent energy units of million electron-volts divided by the square of the speed of light (MeV)/c2. (From Einstein’s mass-energy equivalence equation, E = mc2, u = 931.5 MeV/c2.) The mass of the deuterium nucleus (2.01355 u) is less than the sum of the masses of the proton (1.00728 u) and the neutron (1.00866 u), which is 2.01594 u. Where has the missing mass (0.00239 u) gone? The answer is that the attractive nuclear force between the nucleons has created a negative nuclear potential energy–the binding energy _EB_–that is related to the missing mass, D m (the difference between the two masses). The photon released in forming deuterium has an energy of 2.225 MeV, equivalent to the 0.00239 u required to separate the proton and neutron back into unbound particles. The nuclear decay photons are, in general, higher in energy than photons created in atomic processes.

When tritium is formed by adding a neutron to deuterium, 1n + 2H Æ 3H+ g, a larger amount of energy is released–6.2504 MeV. The greater binding energy of tritium compared to deuterium shows that the nuclear potential energy does not grow in a simple way with the addition of nucleons (the total binding energy is roughly proportional to A). The binding energy per nucleon continues to grow as protons and neutrons are added to construct more massive nuclei until a maximum of about 8 MeV per nucleon is reached around A = 60, past which the average binding energy per nucleon slowly decreases up to the most massive nuclei, for which it is about 7 MeV.

How does a nucleus, which can have up to approximately 100 protons, hold itself together? Why does the electrical repulsion among all those positive charges not cause the nucleus to break up? There must be an attractive force strong enough to be capable of overcoming the repulsive Coulomb forces between protons. Experiment and theory have come to recognize an attractive nuclear interaction that acts between nucleons when they are close enough together (when the range is short enough). The balance between electromagnetic and nuclear forces sets the limit on how large a nucleus can grow.
[http://www2.lbl.gov/abc/wallchart/chapters/02/3.html](http://www2.lbl.gov/abc/wallchart/chapters/02/3.html)

Cell mutation?

Gamma rays are strongly penetrating ionizing radiation.
What that means is that they create charged radicals in any material they travel through.
In large doses it is enough to kill cells and cause radiation poisoning.

Gamma radiation is much more penetrating than other kinds of radiation.
Alpha and beta radiation can be stopped by air and skin.
Gamma radiation needs a lot more matter to stop it. Several inches of lead or several feet of concrete are typical shielding.

There needs to be new trespassing laws created and added to state ballots for any form of mass, energy or light that enters our property, or the airspace above it. I think this is spot on, Scott, and more than just correct tactically, but the correct epistemological/ethical/political approach as well. I have thought about this exact issue for a long time and agree completely.

But there are many many practical issues. First of all, the fundamental rights that have already been long recognized in America, especially with regards to property, are already being violated routinely both directly by and with the support of the government. So we are already on a very shaky foundation with rights.

"Rights" are subject to inflation just like money. "Bad rights" chase out good. Many entitlements exist today that are disguised as "rights," destroying the concept completely. (There's that pesky epistemology again.)

Rights properly understood are the morally-correct governmental recognition of one's individual autonomy, and are supposed to protect you from being injured or robbed by others. Rights cannot entitle you the to services of another because this requires theft which violates the rights of another by definition. Therefore healthcare cannot be a right, for example. Welfare cannot be a right. Education cannot be a right, etc, etc. The litmus test is the requirement of coercion or theft or the property of another citizen. If it requires that, you cannot have a right to it. No matter what the politicans babble.

If one is injured by electromagnetic energy created and released by another person, this is a form of assault and should be considered a violation of rights absolutely. But the government is too busy creating a welfare state to be concerned with the technical application of legitimate rights in this complicated area today.

In a saner society, this issue would be a technical legal issue to accurately define the risks of radiant EM energy, it's range and scope, and how to define "property rights" in this complicated technical context. That's what should happen. But if you look around at the climate and the people in power today...well, all I can say is...Good luck with that!

But I agree with you wholeheartedly in principle here. In fact, I think it's an enormously important philosophical point.

With help from CignatureHealth I am drinking DDW (about 105ppm), with great success. My known results are below. (note that the results are combined, indicate initial tests and first followup)(the top figures are ppm, the bottom are ATP-RPM)
Listening to videos and research that they make available,
my understanding is that their plan is to get on the breath condensate below 130ppm, stay there for a while, say 3 months, and then go for long term maintenance.
That maintenance, may looks like one month out of the year to drink 105ppm.

In the view of research that you provided @[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) this may not be the best way to approach this task.
Body (when it is able) expels excess deuterium in minutes not months.
(Not getting into food part, solar exposure, Circadiaan rhythm, nnEMF, etc., keeping that part as a constant of what I am doing now).
I think that better way to approach task of keeping my (78yo) body constantly at below 130ppm is to drink DDW all the time.
Just likely somewhat less deuterium depleted.
That is using DDW of a strength less than 105ppm and more than 150ppm.
150ppm is a tap water
105ppm is two parts of tap water and one part of DDW-25ppm ( or close to it).
(25+2\*150)/3=108.3ppm (**one part of 25ppm plus 2 parts of 150ppm**)
One approximation that I am planning for future is

(25+5\*150)/6=129.2ppm (**one part 25ppm and 5 parts of 150ppm**)

What is your opinion?
Obviously, I plan on testing my results,
I am just looking for a good guess.

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)

Do light physics matter much to our health? Here is a picture taken 8 hours ago along the side of my home. We had an inch of snow last night. You are looking east and the sun is to the south over the top of my home. The shadow you see are from the eves of my roof.

Does infrared radiation influence the water physics inside of our cells and our mitochondria that generate the energy to keep us alive and kick'in? If cells become dehydrated by nnEMFs and artificial light frequencies, could that be a problem for our health? Did you realize our cell membranes grow sheets of water with the applied light-physics of sunlight and the light output of the mitochondria to grow and maintain a proton:electron gradient known as Exclusion Zone water?

Natural EMFs are always working inside of us. Our electromagnetic energy system drives our regenerative processes and there are many natural frequencies that have many benefits in balance, like UV-B to liberate cholesterol for forming Vitamin D... a wonderful free radical scavenger and anti-cancer agent. But in our modern world, we have not only radically changed our behaviors compared to our ancestors, we are now saturated in non-native EMFs and artificial frequencies alien to our biology, providing abnormal signaling and influencing negative responses within cells including the generation of more free radicals.

What generation of humanity is more insane? Our ancestors, just 100 years ago, lived and breathed in nature each day, had deep relationships, relied on others for their welfare, spent time with our children and had engaged conversations with other human beings for hours on end? Or, the Modern Human that is encouraged by industry and altered culture to rely on technology, have relationships online, look at devices more than 300 times per day, babysit our children with tech-gadgets and look forward to the AI on the horizon to interact with over humans? Will people wake up in time?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-235652>

What is a good way to invoke mitochondrial efficiency and possibly some biogenesis to boot? Build a snowman. My method was pretty simple. I stopped eating at 1pm yesterday. About 18 hours into my fast, I took a pinch of salt and hydrated this morning before going outside and took things slow. In the first 15 minutes, I was very cold due to the wind and bundled up.

But after moving around and building large snowballs, I wanted to be in my t-shirt. When rolling snowballs, I put my knees on the ground on purpose for additional grounding. By 10am, the sunlight's IR was so strong that the snow melted perfectly to make it sticky (Dr. Gerald Pollack alert). I had my sleeves rolled up almost like a tank-top by that time. My neighbors passed by fully bundled with hats and sunglasses walking dogs and probably thought I was gonna 'catch a cold.'

My formula was Sunlight + Cold Thermogenesis + Fasting + Grounding...and some exercise to force my lungs to work harder. Plus I have "Olaf" looking inside my home for the next 2+ weeks if it stays under 35'F.

### Scompy — 2018-02-28T07:43:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-235921>

Tesla is haunting me now. What are you seeing below? My truck's alternator. The same technology brought you this picture also converting DC into AC in a camera. Yesterday, I got a new battery for my truck as it looked lower power than it should be. The low-battery was a symptom of a larger issue: a failing alternator. I suspected this could be the case and asked the mechanic to test it. He ultimately revealed a dying alternator.

What happens when there is more resistance produced when converting DC to AC because of the bearings not spinning as well as they should or the make of the rotor itself (old vs. new)? A larger magnetic field can be produced. When I look my truck in yesterday to switch out my alternator with a new one, I measured my seat around a 4 milliGauss magnetic field. After I got my new alternator installed, it's down to 1 milliGauss at my seat = less resistance produced just like having a normal switch vs. a dimmer switch on lights.

The lesson takeaway is that the mechanisms that drive electronics can change over time. It's good to know what is going on at T0, T1, T2, T3, T4, T5, etc. This is why I generally shy away from buying used electronics. I've tested bad monitors and other gadgets producing high nnEMFs when one would not have expected it. Also adding, we need to know what's going on at 0G, 1G, 2G, 3G, 4G and 5G as well.

### Scompy — 2018-03-02T08:24:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-236128>

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I see a terrible science-fiction plot. It is "screenplay fodder" for Hollywood writers, so listen up. It is a future time about 100 years from now when Humanity has forgotten about nnEMFs entirely. Society proceeds forward through the next 30 years without questioning the real, yet invisible physics around our environments. EMF Meters are ultimately confiscated by authorities. Hospitals become grand, golden palaces with people spend a lot of their time later in life with AI servants.

It looks like a mutated Land of Oz to me, but frequency-updated for technology rather than visible color-updated in Munchkinland. The Wizard ain't that friendly either, and the city isn't emerald. It's a time where technology is passed off as "magical" to the masses so they don't question why it has become fundamental in their lives. The artificial and the natural have merged so it's definitely "not cool" to question this future form of integrated-nature.

As life is harmed in this futuristic Shakespearean tragedy, the illusion must intensify; technology is hidden altogether and society believes it to be "magic." If the technologies behind the magic were discovered by a heroic Protagonist, it might create a powerful conflict for a meaningful story. So for now, we won't break the 4th wall. Humans typically preoccupy their days interacting with magical minions rather than other humans. There is a strong effort to remove "the old ways" by destroying older misinformation that the "ancient scientists" had observed and warned about that worked against their great grandparent's lives.

Speaking of lives, the life span had also increased greatly since ancient times. Nearly all of society believes it's normal to live to the age of about 30 to 40 and then pass away. Those that live to the age of 50 are considered elders and are very grateful for such long lives. Those that missed out on acquiring a "condition" in their prime (at the age of 15) are known to be of "great fortune." Strangely, many of the Leaders and Rulers seem to never die. Hmm. Instead, this "magic" has become a synonym with "longevity" and "power."

The Leaders strongly advocate the masses to "embrace the magic" when possible. Also, society has come to believe their Leaders are benevolent and wise, while also somewhat mysterious. It is rumored they use the nature magic so frequently, it's what keeps them immortal. Some elements in society have become a bit jealous over the Leader's access to the magic, and at times, there are skirmishes, conflicts and theft to acquire special devices rumored to remove diseases and extend life.

Our Protagonist is "sensitive" to this "magic", even though it should not be a possibility. After all, everyone knows the magic has a nourishing and beneficial influence, never a bad one. This complaint alerts the authorities that our Protagonist must go in for a "special treatment" to be cured of the bad feelings generated from the magic. Instead, our Protagonist escapes the society altogether with the help of another (probably a love interest) and finds the ancient passages that leads to strange antennas called "trees" by our love interest. Our Protagonist also discovers that many more people have escaped the Leaders; several hundred people that don't use the magic. And they have been living very abnormal lives in this hidden society: without any illness.

What will happen when the two societies collide?

The knowledge in this realm is more powerful than physical strength. How we act and respond to the knowledge we obtain is paramount for the rest of us to save the others around us that fail to realize they are being damaged. This scene from 2013 Superman movie describes the choice.

The hidden "physical" spaceship = the hidden "knowledge" of EMFs and how they are fundamental to life

"We were sure the government would show up at our doorstep." = Certain groups know what's been going on upon Humanity

Knowing what we do about EMF proliferation upon the Earth, "we are going to have to stand proud in front of the human race or not."

An adopted boy from another planet has humanity for his father and sees the good in them.

"There is more at stake here than just our lives and the lives of those around us."

Clark's father is talking about all of humanity and what is to come.

Notice that Clark understands there is good in everyone. After saving the children on the school bus, he realizes the bully that was just tormenting him 1 minute before is drowning in the water. Clark saves him as well. This perceived "enemy" is within all of us. Our greatest enemy is our personal paradigms. People live a life and base their believes upon what they have been taught. It is wise to get our "enemies" to see how we can save them as well.

### taiyang — 2018-03-04T15:50:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-236260>

i tend to look away from uncomfortable things, or have a short attention span for them. i don't think i really realize the danger of nnemf's in my core- i just know that i have to do certain things to get a good night's sleep and not have headaches. but another part of me is nervous and restless that nnemf's are hugely dangerous. that's when i start to think of my mom's breast cancer and how before it was detected, she had a hospital bed in her room to prop her up when she had a cold or the huge led screen tv she hung up on the wall a short distance from her so she could watch it before she went to sleep. or i think about my 20 year old daughter's addiction to her phone and her insomnia. or i think about how my heavy menstural bleeding starting in 2008 around the time of 4G...

then i think of my father's colon cancer that was detected in the winter when a mass had grown so big in his bowel that he couldn't have a bowel movement. he had to have large sections of his bowels cut out and because of his age (79) or the advanced nature of the cancer (not sure what stage), he was only give a pill to take-no radiation. he lost a lot of muscle and weight, but that spring he went right back to farming and worked hours in the fields in the sun. he has always said he wanted to die in the fields. the cancer has not come back. he's 83 now.

when my mom knew her time was near, she told a little lie to me because she knew the truth of the situation would overwhelm me and make me freeze up. she said that she needed her socks on to get to sleep, but later in the night they would make her too hot, so she wanted me to take them off her feet before i went to work (she lived with me). so even though i thought it strange, i did it. in hindsight i realized that she wanted me to check in on her because she knew she might be lose her balance and fall to the floor or need to be taken to the hospital and any moment. she didn't want to say that, she just told a white lie so that i would check on her without being too upset.

that's how i feel i have to act with the teachers. if i tell them how dangerous using the laptops are for the elementary school kids, they will reject that horrific thought and not change anything. but if i say that the screens are causing my students' eyes to water and hurt, so they need the screen at a distance with a mouse hooked up, they might accept that advice....it's not good enough but it's something.

### Scompy — 2018-03-05T08:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-236316>

When I was growing up as a kid in the 1970s, my parents would often play the radio or 8-track tape in the car. Having relatively fresh 8year-old ears in comparison to my parent's 40+ year-old ears, I would often sit just in front of a speaker mounted behind my brain in the back seat. Even though I complained to them the music was too loud quite frequently, they both thought I was being unreasonable. Back in those ye ole' times, many cars did not come standard with a "fade" option to push the volume to the front of the car. On long trips, I would have my fingers plugging my ears for hours on end.

You would think empathy would play a role. But when two people in the front seat of the car have worse hearing, are positioned several feet farther from the speaker and there are barriers in between (including my own head), their reality doesn't match my reality. It requires people to think about how all parts of the system are designed and work under specific conditions to not come make a wrong conclusion. In this case, the conclusion was, "Our son is being a little brat about the nice music. We have it set to a reasonable volume."

This thought I had this morning got me thinking a lot about the ES and EHS (myself included). How society treats us is very much like my childhood experience I described when it comes to the senses. For many years, my parents would see me plugging my ears in the backseat and realized over time that my experience was far different than their own.

Eventually when we got a new car and the fade-dial, my parents would put the volume more into the front, but only when I requested. They would often tell me, "your ears are really sensitive" to pad any wrong-doing on their part and actually told me, "your ears will eventually get used to the volume." That kind of freaked me out at first because they were implying, "I need to go more deaf in the future to acclimate to this problem and come down to their level of hearing." I actually thought they had a good point if it would make the pain diminish or stop with loud tunes.

If I were to apply the same logic around a cigarette smoker, I should be labeling myself as Cigarette Smoke Sensitive. Likewise with other toxins, I would also be Asbestos Sensitive, Mercury Sensitive, Lead Sensitive and Sulfuric Acid Sensitive as well. Getting bit by a rattlesnake makes me Poison Sensitive, and falling accidentally from a roof makes me Gravity Sensitive. This all makes perfect sense.

In thinking about this parent-created-problem of the past and what we see with the ES or EHS in our present time, we erroneously label and put blame upon those that suffer to satisfy another segment of the population's personal pleasures and/or profit margins. This lack of empathy is very astounding to those that suffer as canaries in the coal mine. But to those that don't feel or perceive the influences of being radio of electro-damaged, it doesn't mean they are immune. As many are now discovering, the rate of people coming down with ES and EHS symptoms is growing rapidly. In the past, those with ES or EHS were only a slice of the pie. Now the pie is at the tipping point in my opinion, about to fall off of the table altogether waking up many more people to the nnEMF issue.

Society will make a lot of excuses for the pleasures and/or advances deemed 'beneficial' to people, even though those new technologies were never tested properly upon humanity in the first place. It can be easily argued that society is providing a broad-stroke selfish act by ignoring the ES and EHS, as well as the problems society has created. But to be fair, those that do not feel the influences of nnEMFs are often deaf and blind to the influences, or even worse, they have developed paradigms for their illnesses putting the blame on something else entirely without any synergy to nnEMFs. Here we have the establishment of our modern medical system, looking for answers in the biochemical or the genetic. They will continue to bark up the wrong trees as long as society continues to refuse nnEMFs play little to no role in disease expression.

For these reasons, society will continue to go to great lengths to push down and omit this issue. And sad as it may sound, those that are being damaged the most are exactly the portion of the population that is bound to ruffle the feathers of industry, governments and society as a whole. We are sending a dire warning, just like the dying bee colonies and amphibian populations across the world. Ignoring the problem won't make this "invisible problem" disappear. It's gotta be dealt with before those in industry, government and all of society succumbs to the damage first-hand.

### Scompy — 2018-03-18T23:40:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-237460>

Ready for some real talk? Then prepare yourself for this 7.83-minute message. Science as a whole has a very long and bumpy road ahead of it in the coming decades if Smart technologies get interwoven into laboratories. I haven't heard any of our Deans, Presidents or University Officials talking about this real potential to skew experimental data results across the world. It's not good for business to admit conditional factors at work in research labs. Also, many universities have their funding-pie interwoven with Industry to promote the impression their science is "state of the art" and impeccable in how they have designed their experiments and their cutting-edge tools.

As a results of the political bed-sharing, it puts many research institutions and individual research laboratories for that matter between a meteor and a hard place for financial funding to accept or deny industry influence, as most labs are at the mercy of investors. Ironically, the slice of investors that are funding science "for science with as little bias as possible" need to be aware of the EMF issue right now so their hard-earned money being burned as a result of more tainting potential with wireless light and energy in the air.

Now think about a typical biology research lab on any university campus in 2018. I'm able speak of the standard practices because I lived it in the 1990s before RF/Microwave was present. It was very common to have walk-in warm and cold rooms, shaker systems, centrifuges, machines for pouring petri-dishes, fields from fans in hoods, plate spinners and autoclaves for reliance. These normal lower-frequency nnEMFs have already been in place as electric and magnetic fields, along with various dirty electricity and harmonics generated in the wiring and such in building walls. Also, we cannot forget all of the computer systems and specialized technology components that may be very high voltage or offering higher frequencies in places.

Powergrid nnEMF influences have been ongoing for a century in experiments. Let's look back in the 1930s. Neuroscientist Karl Lashley performed radical brains on rats at that time. His goal was to try and get them to forget how to run a maze by frying part of their brains. He did a lot of unconventional (really horrific) things, including using his wife’s curling iron to try and burn different parts of the brain and see if the rats would still know how to run around the maze. Here we have a curling iron that generates a stronger magnetic and electric field when plugged in, in addition to the direct heat produced.

Most laboratories have non-sunlight lighting conditions overhead pulsing down strange and omitted light frequencies upon their experiments. Ready for this? Two years ago, 23andme put out a Youtube video about their genetic-testing products, and showed the inside of their labs in the video; kind of a mistake they realized later after I commented and saw their laboratory workers spent all day long under fluorescent lighting and asked if they controlled for the fluorescent lighting wavelengths and missing wavelengths overhead upon their genetic assays.

Although many other people thought it was a brilliant observation I made about how this could influence results, I didn't expect 23and"me" to get back to "me." I was indirectly showing them that observations are everything, and that we live in a world where the most simple physics around us are not being controlled as good as they could be, and could have a potential to taint results.

I often think of the future about the plethora of "what if scenarios" as we "progress Science" with our modern tools and techniques. The first two questions that come to mind are, "What if nnEMFs do not get revealed to the public in just 5 years by 2023? How will research laboratories conduct their experiments in what type of environments and with what new tools?" I'd expect to see a flood of "Smart" RF/Microwave devices in labs in the propagandized names of "efficiency", "convenience" and "reliability" due to government and industry pressure to sell more products to labs to feed the whole financial chain from start to finish.

At this time, what is very concerning to me about how we conduct Science are the many "very close tools" to perform experiments, in addition to the normal skewing potential already mentioned above. Back in 1992, most tools were still mechanical to extract biological solutions or chemical reagents. In 1995, I was faced with a choice to order student-researchers "electronic versions" of "mechanics pipettes" they would normally use. At that time, I had learned how to take apart these "mechanical pipettes" and clean them properly to retain the most accurate measurement possible within a milliliter or two of variance. Over time, students wouldn't use these mechanical devices properly and they'd acquire a lot of wear and tear with various chemicals and biological materials that could get inside of them.

In 1995, my laboratory coordinator elected to forego the electronic versions because I knew how to repair the mechanical ones. Plus, the new versions were a little more costly, even though people above her were pressuring her to get "updated" electronic ones to make it a littler easier on the students to use. Because of my repair-skills, she knew I could maintain the mechanical pipettes to keep them just as accurate or even more accurate than the electronic ones; plus, if an electronic one broke, it would eliminate the need to send them back to the manufacturer to not be out of commission for weeks waiting to fix or replace it. This analogy is very much like mechanical cars from the 1960s vs. modern cars with all of the electronic junk built into them. My point here is that, "What If" we would have ditched the mechanical ones and bought the electronic ones... Would we have unknowingly introduced a new nnEMF variable condition into experiments?

Now I think about the sheer volume of research-monkeys all over the world that are over-focused in biochemistry and mechanics. They may not have much biophysics background, but their experimental results may have been influenced by nnEMFs. Many scientists won't consider energy fields, frequencies and light impacts in the very tools they are using for their experiments. Many may not control for the indoor environments they perform and store their experiments. Is it no wonder there are so many differences in results from one lab to the next when conducting similar scientific experiments?

The increasing nnEMF soup (including lighting differences) is no doubt adding conditional influences that could influence some types of experiments where fields and frequencies can play a larger role in what is being tested for. Graciously, we need to help educate our educators that may be thinking about designing and setting up experimental conditions. In many cases, this may require pointing them to the knowledge and science that already exists in the biophysics to help increase more wedges of their knowledge pies.

We have a great number of highly intelligent geniuses among us at educational institutions, research labs and facilities that may simply have not been exposed to many knowledge slices that are interwoven between physics and biology at this time. Much of the biophysics is not typically taught at universities at this time and people along a pre-med undergrad track may have little to no exposure to this field of science.

All people, including geniuses, will only make judgments about the knowledge slices they have obtained, unless they entertain enough creativity to formulate what they have not been previously taught and then dig to find if their creativity matches up to past scientific observations (that were omitted from their high school and college knowledge acquisition). This is why we can have a generation of respected people with high intelligence and great communication skills, but with missing pieces of knowledge that make judgments for the rest of society. Culling specific knowledge banks away of the public and out of our schools is arguably the greatest method to control the status-quo.

[https://psychology.fas.harvard.edu/people/karl-lashley](https://psychology.fas.harvard.edu/people/karl-lashley)

### Scompy — 2018-03-24T20:07:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-237896>

I believe we are entering a time when the reliability of "View Counts" and other statistics represented on the internet may become even more manipulated than they already are. nnEMFs at its core is a challenging issue for communication companies. Most companies are entrenched in technologies with full-saturation. By definition, most companies will have a natural bias that "their technologies are beneficial to them and their profit margins." As a result, they will naturally gravitate away from any messaging content or narrative that could undermine them and their profit margins. No virtue is immune to being trampled upon when an organization's survival is at stake.

So when Senator "Colbeck cites health as reason to slow 5G rush" goes out on the internet and we see it approaching 5,000 views today (currently at 4,646), it's basically an attack on companies as 5G, the IoT, wireless, etc. will be getting integrated into the pulse of most companies in the near future. As we know here at EMF Warriors, because both the higher and lower frequencies impact biology, we want to see designers and engineers product technologies that has less impact upon human health and all life for that matter.

Mainstream Industry won't admit there is a problem with the powergrid or with RF/Microwave use, so there is little progress or a company's willingness to adopt a "more safe" technology, or it would be admitting to a liability. Hence, this is why insurance companies fear the day when the public learns about nnEMFs upon their health and demand compensation. Industry will continue to ride their WAVE for an extended LENGTH of time to kick the can down the road (and into our faces) until enough people speak up outside of mainstream media. They have that "Media-Manipulation" checkbox checked off also, since the mainstream media has sold-out to them in many ways, especially over the nnEMF issue that could devastate their core tool-set.

The in coming year or two, it's time for those of us awake to break out the popcorn to watch what happens to those that are asleep at the wheel. It will be quite telling a year from now on social media when it becomes clear how companies manipulate people for their interests. Expect to see View Counts altered (higher or lower) and algorithms developed to bury video content as deep as possible that does not suit their financial interests. If we had a media that fought for The People, the media would be reporting this story left, right and center... This is an issue that influences people with all political views. We live in interesting times when the virtues of truth, love and liberty get so easily shredded. If enough of the voting majority are asleep, there won't be enough to defend timeless virtues in elections at ballot boxes or the story perceptions on social media.

"YouTube’s latest algorithm appears to weigh views paired with interactions — in the form of a like or comment — much more heavily. Buying likes doesn’t remedy this, as — even if both services are bought from the same panel — they won’t come from the same IP address as the views. As fool-proof of a system as this may seem, viewbotters appear to have already found a workaround. They’re called “non-drop views” or “real views” (though they’re not actually, you know, real), and panel owners claim they’re able to game the algorithm. It’s possible to compare engagement (i.e. likes and comments) to view totals, but that’s hardly a science. Since viewbotters have managed to stay ahead of the curve, and YouTube no longer gives access to video statistics, we are even more in the dark than ever about what is actually popular, and what is being forced into the cultural conversation through manipulation."

[https://theoutline.com/post/3804/inside-youtube-s-fake-views-economy?zd=1&zi=wlupfqiw](https://theoutline.com/post/3804/inside-youtube-s-fake-views-economy?zd=1&zi=wlupfqiw)

### Sheddie — 2018-04-10T04:45:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-239112>

Scompy -- I didn't know where to fit this in but your Journal seemed most lively on cross-relevance and multi-disciplinary info related to frequency effects and artificial/synthetic biology... (Sorry, there was no year-date on this.)

Viruses can be made to churn out high-tech nanomaterials

Viruses subvert their hosts to pump out masses of new viruses. In an unusual twist, an MIT researcher reports in the May 3 issue of Science that she used genetically engineered viruses that are noninfectious to humans to mass produce tiny materials for next-generation optical, electronic and magnetic devices.--"We've been looking at using genetic tools to grow semiconductor materials," said author Angela M. Belcher, associate professor of materials science and engineering and biological engineering. "In this case, we took advantage of the viruses' genetic makeup and physical shape to not only grow the material but also to help them assemble themselves into liquid crystal structures that are several centimeters long."-- Belcher and colleagues at the University of Texas at Austin are interested in using the processes by which nature makes materials to design new biological- electronic hybrid materials that could be used to assemble electronic materials at the nanoscale. Her research brings together inorganic chemistry, materials chemistry, biochemistry, molecular biology and electrical engineering. She will join the MIT Department of Material Science and Engineering and the Biological Engineering Division of the School of Engineering in September.-- Belcher's approach is to use systems such as viruses that evolved over millions of years to work perfectly at the nanoscale, but to convince the viruses to work on technologically important materials. Belcher's research team can evolve the viruses to work on the materials of interest over a period of months.--Building self-assembling and defect-free two- and three-dimensional materials on the nanometer scale is essential for the construction of new devices for optics and electronics. Researchers have been looking at ways to use organic materials to organize molecules of inorganic materials on the nanoscale. Fabricating viral films, Belcher said, may provide new pathways for organizing molecules to help create electronic, optical and magnetic materials.

"We showed that engineered viruses can recognize specific semiconductor surfaces, and these recognition properties can be used to organize molecules in inorganic nanocrystals, forming ordered arrays," she said. "In this system, we can easily modulate the length of the bacteriophage (the type of virus) and the type of inorganic materials through genetic modification and selection. One can easily modulate and align different types of inorganic nanocrystals in 3D layered structures."

This work is supported by the Army Research Office, the National Science Foundation and the Welch Foundation.

### Scompy — 2018-04-13T04:43:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-239325>

**Expect Expectation Failures (by Scompy)**

I think I have just discovered a way to put many relationship specialists and marriage counselors out of business. And out of all of my posts, I think this one might be the most shared. Last night after eating a lot of Scorpion Chili sauce when only one drop was supposed to be used, it got my brain heated up to think about things at 4am.

Some questions pondered in a dreamlike state: "Why do many older men and women end up stubborn and lonely?" and, "Why do some people have a hard time at their relationship years later, when just years before, they were enamored and deeply in-love with each other?" This is what the beloved scorpion chili will do.

The expectation typically happens when the person assume that "it's a given" the expectation "will be occurring" at some point in the future. Otherwise, a person will be crushed that it never happened. When these add up over time, it can create a seemingly inescapable "grudge-dungeon" where there is a list of past failures that have added up like a wall of negative chalk-marks that add up over time. And some people may tend to focus on the other person's failures and not have an equal wall of positive chalk-marks. At the same time, each person has been keeping tally of oneself, but the reflection of the self-created wall of good marks or wall of bad marks may each, independently, be near or distant at a given moment.

When an expectation is harder to fulfill due to the circumstances in another person's life, there is a greater chance for failure. If the importance of the expectation is not ever discussed, there will be a breakdown at some point. One example that comes to mind might be something like when a couple goes on a date night. Let's say the couple has 3 kids and each kid has individual needs or even special needs. Both parents may feel some guilt for leaving the children at home to spend some romantic time on their own away from them, and when on a date, may even get emotions about how the kids are doing, often spoiling any romantic moment to spice itself up like the scorpion chili. In this example, if date night was spoiled, a failure has happened in the minds of both participants--making it more unlikely a new, future date night will be planned again in the future.

Now we get to the meat of this post. Every person on the planet is changing constantly... their minds are growing, they are replicating their trillions of cells continually, new experiences are happening, new goals are being considered, new expectations are forming, etc, etc. It is not possible to live a static existence. For this reason, we have to consider our \*adaptive nature\* built into each of us. In a relationship, if both participants have the "expectation that the other will change and grow" over our experiences, it alleviates an underlying concern for the relationship and allows both to understand we are not static and live will be an adventure, even if we choose to buckle down and not take any chances in life. What I am driving at is there is a large misconception that "Oh, I know who that person is", when in reality, we are all changing and adapting each day we live.

Here is the magical question we need to be asking ourselves in all relationships, "Does the waterfall dry up in the summer?" Why is this question so significant?

When traveling to a beautiful place to see a waterfall, people \*expect\* to see nature as it is and we are cool with all of its faults. We don't question it. It is important to note that we don't get angry at its faults. If there is a partial rainbow rather than a full one, we don't complain to nature about it. We have a predisposition not to be disappointed by nature, even when fails us. For instance, if we see a tree that's leaning a little over the road like the Tower of Piza, we may consider, "Hmm, it might be a good idea to help the tree not fall, or maybe chop it down in case it falls on someone." We typically will not question \*why\* the tree was leaning in the first place, and we won't get angry at the tree for leaning.

Humans \*are\* nature. We can easily lose sight of this because we have a consciousness. When a human creates something, like a toy, a car or any object of any complexity, we develop expectations about its function and design, how well it works or if it is close to being broken. We look for failures in things as a process for fixing them, but because of this, we often apply lofty and harder-to-meet expectations upon other human beings.

To pound this point home even more about nature, when a baby or an infant is screaming for something, the parents will put down what they are doing, run quickly and do anything for the child. When the 5 year old starts screaming for something, parents may now \*expect\* better behavior and put the child in time-out. When the 15 year old starts screaming for something, the parents may start up a verbal battle to instill ?parental wisdumb? into the mix with biochemical surges of adrenaline and cortisol mixed up in all participants.

Expectations are the subtle, insidious blotch-mark on our relationships. We love to pigeonhole ourselves and believe people are static, but even as adults, we never stop learning and growing, even if society says we are done with those schooling years. Many people that have become stubborn and alone probably have a long grudge-dungeon account of past expectations where many people have failed them. But ironically, if a person had formed far less expectations of other people and had \*treated\* people more like a \*product of nature\* in the first place, there would had been far less negative tally marks placed within that person's mind.

Less tally marks translates into less negative sentiments about other people we interact with in our lives, and therefore, less negativity held within our own minds. For this reason, when people flee and desire to purge other people from their lives, it may not actually not resolve the underlying issues of "expectation formations" to question them. It may \*feel good\* to purge others and burn bridges to remove a perceived mental weight, but the problem originally started with the formation of the expectation that ended up as a failure due to our personal paradigms. We have to be dead-honest and truthful to look deeply within ourselves to understand how and why we form them.

For this reason, the Holy Grail of all relationships, at the very root, is to bring to the surface all "expectation failures" in a relationship, so these preconceptions can be brought out into the open and talked about for resolution. This is typically the hardest thing to accomplish and why relationships fail because typically one person will ashamed about listing out what a person expects of another. Likewise, the person that could not live up to the other person's expectations feels like a failure and won't want to bring it up either. It is the fear of exposing mental expectations that divides people, typically not the scorpion chili sauce.

These are the main questions that need to be asked:

1\. When did the expectation-failure occur in the past based on a person's expectation of another?

2\. How and why did the expectation fail occur due to the circumstances involved?

3\. The KEY: Was it okay for the person to be a failure and not meet the expectation?

This last question is a huge key in the equation. Why? It shows how we previously designed our expectations in our minds and to question why they are there in the first place. Just like the past example about about the baby, 5 year-old and the 15-year old, it helps the person that created the expectation to understand why it was formed and the underlying reasons for it. If both the expectation-creator and the person that failed in the expectation cannot resolve the problem, it ends up hurting both participants and no one wins.

This post end on some profound irony: we must \*expect\* that our "expectation formations" are one of the most powerful wedge drivers into all of our relationships. If we are mindful about the expectations we create and live by, and question them continually and throw them back as a comparison to the reality of nature, we may end up realizing all humans are filled with faults, we're all on the same path together and we're all growing continually in unique ways, even if this means growing scorpion chili in the garden.

And btw, I think I may be onto a new mitohack with the Scorpion...

It is important to note that we don't get angry at its faults. If there is a partial rainbow rather than a full one, we don't complain to nature about it. We have a predisposition not to be disappointed by nature, even when fails us. For instance, if we see a tree that's leaning a little over the road like the Tower of Piza, we may consider, "Hmm, it might be a good idea to help the tree not fall, or maybe chop it down in case it falls on someone." We typically will not question \*why\* the tree was leaning in the first place, and we won't get angry at the tree for leaning.
>
> Humans \*are\* nature. We can easily lose sight of this because we have a consciousness. When a human creates something, like a toy, a car or any object of any complexity, we develop expectations about its function and design, how well it works or if it is close to being broken. We look for failures in things as a process for fixing them, but because of this, we often apply lofty and harder-to-meet expectations upon other human beings.
>
> To pound this point home even more about nature, when a baby or an infant is screaming for something, the parents will put down what they are doing, run quickly and do anything for the child. When the 5 year old starts screaming for something, parents may now \*expect\* better behavior and put the child in time-out. When the 15 year old starts screaming for something, the parents may start up a verbal battle to instill ?parental wisdumb? into the mix with biochemical surges of adrenaline and cortisol mixed up in all participants.
>
> Expectations are the subtle, insidious blotch-mark on our relationships. We love to pigeonhole ourselves and believe people are static, but even as adults, we never stop learning and growing, even if society says we are done with those schooling years. Many people that have become stubborn and alone probably have a long grudge-dungeon account of past expectations where many people have failed them. But ironically, if a person had formed far less expectations of other people and had \*treated\* people more like a \*product of nature\* in the first place, there would had been far less negative tally marks placed within that person's mind.
>
> Less tally marks translates into less negative sentiments about other people we interact with in our lives, and therefore, less negativity held within our own minds. For this reason, when people flee and desire to purge other people from their lives, it may not actually not resolve the underlying issues of "expectation formations" to question them. It may \*feel good\* to purge others and burn bridges to remove a perceived mental weight, but the problem originally started with the formation of the expectation that ended up as a failure due to our personal paradigms. We have to be dead-honest and truthful to look deeply within ourselves to understand how and why we form them.
>
> For this reason, the Holy Grail of all relationships, at the very root, is to bring to the surface all "expectation failures" in a relationship, so these preconceptions can be brought out into the open and talked about for resolution. This is typically the hardest thing to accomplish and why relationships fail because typically one person will ashamed about listing out what a person expects of another. Likewise, the person that could not live up to the other person's expectations feels like a failure and won't want to bring it up either. It is the fear of exposing mental expectations that divides people, typically not the scorpion chili sauce.
>
> These are the main questions that need to be asked:
>
> 1\. When did the expectation-failure occur in the past based on a person's expectation of another?
>
> 2\. How and why did the expectation fail occur due to the circumstances involved?
>
> 3\. The KEY: Was it okay for the person to be a failure and not meet the expectation?
>
> This last question is a huge key in the equation. Why? It shows how we previously designed our expectations in our minds and to question why they are there in the first place. Just like the past example about about the baby, 5 year-old and the 15-year old, it helps the person that created the expectation to understand why it was formed and the underlying reasons for it. If both the expectation-creator and the person that failed in the expectation cannot resolve the problem, it ends up hurting both participants and no one wins.
>
> This post end on some profound irony: we must \*expect\* that our "expectation formations" are one of the most powerful wedge drivers into all of our relationships. If we are mindful about the expectations we create and live by, and question them continually and throw them back as a comparison to the reality of nature, we may end up realizing all humans are filled with faults, we're all on the same path together and we're all growing continually in unique ways, even if this means growing scorpion chili in the garden.
>
> [View attachment 7344](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/7344/)
>
> And btw, I think I may be onto a new mitohack with the Scorpion...

I know this has nothing to do with what you are saying, to my dumb question/thought.

### Scompy — 2018-04-28T01:53:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-240069>

Twinkle, twinkle little "star" within our cells...

"A biochemical analysis of purified recombinant human Twinkle protein (gene product of _c10orf2_) showed that the mitochondrial replicative helicase inefficiently unwinds well characterized intermolecular and intramolecular G-quadruplex DNA substrates, as well as a unimolecular G4 substrate derived from a mitochondrial sequence that nests a deletion breakpoint described in human renal cell carcinoma. Although G4 has been implicated in the initiation of mitochondrial DNA replication, our current findings suggest that mitochondrial G-quadruplexes are also likely to be a source of instability for the mitochondrial genome by perturbing the normal progression of the mitochondrial replication machinery, including DNA unwinding by Twinkle helicase."

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4208006/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4208006/)

Having played RPGs since 1979 using 4-sided, 6-sided, 8-sided, 10-sided, 12-sided and 20-sided dice, it's an amazing part of mathematical nature that Metaton's cube contains a great number of 3-dimensional shape constructions within a 2-dimensional drawing of a few circles!

In thinking about water biophysics and the hexagon lattice formed, 7 circles of equal diameters are fundamental to the formation of a hexagon. For that matter, in thinking about all semi-conductors. Then adding another 6, circles, we get the "Flower of Life" and formations we find in nature. I really could care less how woo-woo associations, or crop-circle or conspiracy-theories, or any of the things could be formed about me posting this. Anything that promotes a rigid mind to turn away from what is embedded into shapes and nature, and what they create could be considered a form of mind-control, no? Flat-out sentiment: anything that points us what could be closer to an observed truth is what motivates me to investigate.

We have to start thinking atomic and subatomic as to the ramifications of geometry. It's also a point of contention I've found that people will laugh at to dismiss, but I don't think it's something we should ignore. It's a wonderful thing to see how the most basic part of the Pi can turn into the most complex things.

### Scompy — 2018-04-30T06:38:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-240217>

**SCOMPY'S 5 MITOHACKS FOR A 5G WORLD!
This is \*in addition\* to the normal quantum goodness of sunlight, grounding, CT, good water sources, friends, etc.
**
1\. **DDW and DDF.** Deuterium Depleted Water and Food sources. I am just finishing my last batch of 25ppm DDW this coming week (undiluting up to around 75ppm) and not only do I feel great, lots of improvements to my fatloss. I purposely gained 10 pounds of extra bodyfat from Feb-March to prep my April DDW hack. The 10 pounds are back off, and my strength and lungs also feel way better compared to this time last year. My original metric was 142ppm in my breathy, and in a week or two, I'll see where my breathy is at now. I view this like an "oil change" I may do twice a year. I suspect I'm down below 130ppm now. The larger observations: I suspect my ES has gotten better because of my DDW now. I have no tinnitus and I've tested it more and more recently with specific places I know that has usually set it off in the past. My eye spots are basically gone also, and I spent 30mins on the phone earlier in the week with no eye spots...normally this would also form them after 15+ minutes.

2\. **Fasting**. A very long water fast at the beginning of each month (3 to 6 day fast based on various factors). During each month, I also have periodic IF daily. This varies from about 14 to 18 hours per day based on what's going on, when I exercise, etc.

3\. **Baking Soda**. Proton information boost, lowered inflammation and pH balance. In the morning right upon waking, I will do an 8 oz glass of water with 1 teaspoon of baking soda. About 2 hours before sleeping, another 6 oz. glass of water with 1 teaspoon mixed. Then I also brush my teeth with baking soda with some neem.

4\. **Overall Potassium Boosting.** I have been adding more K+ to my diet overall in the form of salts, but also some greens. I've been doing a bit of basil, kale or spinach for the K+ at lunch with typically fish/shrimp or seafood or dark meat chicken or egg (usually tumeric is here with pepper and Habenero or similar hot salsa for another benefit). Then I have a "NoSalt" salt alternative that is made with K+ Salts. I'll weave in 25% of this K+ salt in a mixture of 75% Celtic and/or French salts.

5\. **Dark Chocolate (in Great Moderation).** About 2 to 4 wedges of Dark Chocolate at max per day (often skipping a day between) for the Magnesium and Resveritrol. I'll typically do an 85 ot 88% Dark chocolate that was not wrapped in Aluminum foil and I'll have the chocolate right after breakfast, or, right after lunch. Never past 1pm for the day.

### Scompy — 2018-05-04T20:22:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-240505>

It's wise to kick back and focus attention on \*intention\* for a comment or post online. We are dealing with an issue that borders on disbelief, challenges personal paradigms and could invoke stress responses. Industry laughs at us if we squabble and spew among ourselves. People that are not able to pull back enough and see the bigger picture on this issue will play into those that knowingly advocate anti-health to side with EMF proliferation across the world.

It all comes back to intention. Here is the basis of the "mental chatter" that often consumes people that focus through a negative lens. Do I look at another person's cup half-empty and harp on the person's flaws? Do I like to point out flaws in most people because of a cause-effect lens? Do I get emotional at the drop of a pin? Do I knee-jerk react anytime I read something to spew? Do I believe if I solve problems by this approach, I will be receptive?

Wisdom arrives when we flip behaviors. In comparison to the above mental chatter, seeing any issue through the other lens can be a powerful tool to get others to be receptive to an issue. This is extremely true of the nnEMF issue where our mental faculties are being challenged, ironically, by the nnEMF itself for influences to our neurology, moods, oxidative stress generation, etc.

Now let's flip what I wrote above. Do I look at another person, that I dislike, through a positive lens and attempt to see that person's cup half-full? Do I like to point out the strengths of people in because we are each very complex thinkers, have our own experiences, could have bad days and see that each person has something to offer me? Do I first come from a place of uplifting another, even when I fully disagree or dislike that person? Was the person a kid long ago with hardships, trials and trauma? Can I be as logical in my approach as emotional? Do I believe my uplifting approach will be more receptive than a negative one?

Focusing on positive intentions and logic, and resisting the urge to spew venom is actually an acquired skill set woven with behaviors. Letting emotion trump us at times and "being in the moment" is also part of being human, but do we let it control us 24/7 at our core? Being a parent teaches us a lot about how our "amazing little humans" live moment to moment. They react to the world and our environments by consuming everything around us to grow like a flower, consuming language, consuming others' emotions, and, consuming our intentions. Have you ever noticed when you're having a bad day, the child does as well? But when you're having a wonderful time, the love spreads. At rare times, venom may actually be warranted due to the exchange and to learn something for it, but ultimately if no resolution occurs, it was all for nothing.

The nnEMF issue puts us all in a tight spot, but my \*intention\* and the vast many people here that can flip on their positive intentions will be a force of power unlike any seen before us. It involves word-choice, finding resolution, agreeing to disagree with each other and be cool with it, and pulling in what works for your personal life rather than getting hung up on ill intentions. People are complex, and it can be quite hard to dive into the \*intent\* behind the eyes of the person, if it came from a place of love or hate, even if it looks like love on the surface in one case, or hate on the surface in another.

This is why too much negativity will get people shredded from EMF Warriors, but often not immediately. I gather my data points over time as well. But keep in mind that I don't take sides. I do what is right for the community as a whole, living, breathing being and look out for those that are coming from the right place of heart to uplift us all.

### Scompy — 2018-05-18T21:43:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-241532>

I think Einstein nailed it: for me, some form of skepticism is built into intuition. I know it's one of my personal gifts because I can smell the BS, but I can smell it because I am open to connect to everything, don't like to dismiss things because often my instinct tells me to dismiss, while my intuition tells me to understand and empathize why someone has a sentiment I disagree with on the surface. Part of our rational thought puts us all into a "Cause:Effect or On:Off or Right:Wrong Mentality" to chain and control our thoughts, rather than a more pliable and bending viewpoint that allows some flexibility with our established paradigms and beliefs.

I think part of the challenge of life is to find a marriage of foundations that can be tested and observed, while also reading between the lines with what we have come to believe for a healthy skepticism. At the root of it all is actually love. When a person comes from a place of love and uplifting another, the Cause:Effect viewpoint fades. But at the same time, the positive _**intent**_ that was created by that sentiment can be felt via the natural EMFs from our hearts and brains. The intuition perception is in itself part EMF as well to give and receive if we are not blocked. Look around at how many people are now blocked by the nnEMFs encircling their bodies. It's more that just looking down at a cell phone and not-making eye to eye contact. It is real interference at work in our modern lives.

### Scompy — 2018-05-25T05:44:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-242016>

"**RESULTS:**
We show that resveratrol-induced apoptosis occurs through both the intrinsic and extrinsic apoptotic pathways. Mitochondrial membrane potential and apoptosis-related markers, such as an increased Bax/Bcl-2 ratio, and cleaved forms of caspase-8 and caspase-3, arise following resveratrol addition. Moreover, we find that resveratrol increases both the levels of microtubule-associated protein 1 light chain 3-II and the number of autophagosomes, and further demonstrate that resveratrol-induced autophagy depends on the LKB1-AMPK-mTOR pathway. We next reveal that some apoptosis-related markers induced by resveratrol are further attenuated by the inhibition of autophagy with 3-methyladenine or knockdown of autophagy-related (Atg) genes by siRNA."

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/29788929](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/29788929)

### Scompy — 2018-05-29T20:41:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-242311>

Start Valuing Nature More!
The **_value_** we place on nature, is arguably, the greatest decision we can make about our future health. When a person decides to text on a phone for 15 minutes vs. going outside for 15 minutes, the value-choice is clear: often people believe multitasking is the answer: why not do both as a compromise? Go ahead and compromise your health, but know what you're doing.

This greatest choice is to either start using nature and integrate it daily into our lives for the value of nature itself, or keep nature removed away and buried often because we won't move or sacrifice ourselves from our Modern Day, craptastic environments. If the choice is to stay the course and keep the crappy environment and not use sunlight and grounding, then people that aware aware of nature's vast benefits are left with little choice but to find something artificial that attempts to simulate nature to maintain health. These types of biohacks are just that... short-term tests to ward off disease for a period. Should this be a permanent solution: omit nature and embrace artificial nature?!

Seriously, if we don't want to change our lives toward nature, then expect the artificial to change our lives. Don't let the Paleo and Biohacker marketers make you think you can have your artificial cakes and eat them with artificial nature, unless you'd like to participate in the longer-term experiment. Making that choice to remove nature and embrace artificial nature is like drinking fluoridated toilet water daily when you could be drinking glacial runoff DDW instead.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) started telling us this basic sentiment about nature for over 7 years now. It is all good and well to know what's best for our health, but if we are not living it enough, how can we heal properly? We are all guilty of knowing the right thing to do, but unable to act upon it because we're all being forced, at times, to live with the artificial in this Modern World due to the people in our lives. But that still doesn't stop any of us from planning future moments to get back into nature away from the artificial, especially when we understand circadian timing, seasonal disease expression and any extended length we spend away from nature. If we value nature enough, we **_naturally_** keep track of how long we have been away from her instinctively and find ways to find her sooner than later. In other words, if we are not romancing nature like our significant others, expect all of the normal feelings of hurt, depression and fear to appear when the artificial is romanced instead.

Appeasement is one of the hardest lines to walk because there needs to be some level of appeasement to form relatedness, but just a too much appeasement can destroy credibility and over-emphasize weakness. In my book, this is where matter-of-factness and truth are the best remedies for those that need a wake-up call, but done in a way to not invoke an emotional response is the greatest game of them all.

Since phosphorus is a key atom in life's post translation modifications of proteins, I knew it had to have specific advantages solar effects using its semiconductive properties to generate light emission. That key finding for me, was realizing that red phosphorous can act as an atomic semiconductor. This means this atom has a property most biologic researchers still have not recognized. The conventional wisdom in material sciences was that red phosphorus was really only useful as an insulator."

### Scompy — 2018-06-07T00:35:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-journal.16641/post-242867>

After examining my behaviors and ES over the past 25 years, I will admit to probably my largest

facepalm

I had in the year 2003. Scompy is about to get more personal for all of you. After experiencing many benefits from getting outside into morning sunlight running around the Pasadena Rose Bowl the previous year to train for a marathon, I stopped getting up at 5:45am for convenience.

I initially started my gym membership because I had a strain in my right hip from the marathon, so I wanted to focus more on anaerobic weight lifting to let the hip heal over time. However, in this timeframe, I started sleeping in until 9am+ and then went straight to work (that happened to be 3 blocks away). I wouldn't feel like walking to work... I'd drive my 2001 VW Beetle 3 blocks because I realized I was pretty tired in the mornings.

This was also the era when people would start taking in their cell phones to the gym at 1 to 2 bars and hold their phones to their brains talking to friends on nnEMF-generating treadmills and ellipticals. Over at the weight-lifting stations, mirrors from floor to ceiling reflected the fluorescent lighting to show off muscle quality. At this time, I went to the gym at 9pm with a Russian programmer that once helped with the MIR space station. He was about 6'10 and could easily bench-press over 350 pounds as I spotted him. I'd usually leave the gym by about 10 to 10:30pm.

But most nights after getting back home, I never felt that great. I would typically re-feed because I would want that "protein" to help build my muscles after the workout. By 11pm, I felt something was "off", but I chalked it up to my harder, anaerobic workouts. I remember going through a half-jar of soy or peanut-butter spread on bread to appease the conventional wisdumb for that "protein goodness" while getting online and then watching the Tonight Show or David Letterman 'til about 12:40am (many will know why this was typically the magical time turn off the TV).

But at 12:40am, that's when I'd then go online and typically meetup with a few friends in an MMORPG (Massive Multiplayer Online Role-Playing Game) in the 12am to 3am window. Now it should be clear why I was waking up in the 9 to 10am window, huh!? I've lived the mountains and valleys of circadian rhythm. But when a person is in their mid-30s or younger, there's no stopping us. Risk-assessment is pretty-well skewed, and we're also told by the older generation that we're immortal when we are younger.

After absorbing another hour or two of a monitor's pulsed blue-light, I'd head off to sleep and it would often take me another half-hour to fall to sleep. In that time-frame, I remember listening to Coast to Coast with Art Bell on the radio because I'd find falling to sleep was easier with long monologues as a loose form of hypnotism. My day-job had me 10 to 12 hours a day on average in front of 3+ monitors and various nnEMF equipment. In other words, I was eating more technology than food, and destroying my circadian rhythm.

Now fast-forward 3 months later and I had gained a good 40 pounds... probably 10 pounds was muscle and the 30 pounds was fat. I started "itching" more all day and realized the inside of my arms and legs had strange and new stretch-marks all over them. The day of my marathon when I weighed myself, I was 214 pounds and about 5 months later at the gym, I was 255 to 260 pounds. I remember convincing myself for a few weeks, "Well, I'm more like an American Football player, all is good", but finally woke up when my father visited me and hadn't seen me since the marathon. He said something profound that I remember to this day, "In my whole life, I've never seen you so tired-looking, Scott" after watching X-men 2 with him in theaters.

He mentioned my red-eyes, the dark-circles I had and also had pointed out, gently, how much weight I had gained back. It woke me up because I knew I was bashing my sleep, but also I had a comparison just 6 months prior to feeling quite good, getting good sleep, waking up earlier and feeling refreshed. Also in that same time-frame, my eye ES started reappearing and that combination drove me to change back to the patterns I had just a year before: I cancelled my gym membership and the Russian tank had to find a new spotter. But I started walking and jogging in the mornings under real sunlight and going to sleep by 10 to 11pm. Yep, I had to sacrifice watching The Tonight Show, and found other ways to absorb comedy.

Just 3 months later, I was back down to 220 pounds, my mind was much more clear and my ES had subsided once again. I realized back then, if I missed going outside in the mornings, my health would be affected. I didn't know why for the most part, downplayed sunlight as a major influence, instead attributing most of the benefits to "fresh morning air" and just feeling better because I was getting better sleep. I didn't discover the details and nuances \*why\* my pattern was working until a decade+ later. I just knew it worked because I had a direct and powerful contrasting experience: what not to do and what to do.
