### Inger — 2015-08-08T16:55:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parasites-in-seafood.16679/post-174244>

Well not really once. Except one time I had eaten high meat that I forgot to air... anmd got really sick for 1 day but after that I felt even better than before so I think it was a good thing

once in a while I might get a day of loose stools.... nothing I worry about because I think it is all fine and my body knows what it does  My digestion is awesome, and I really cant complain

Have not seen parasites in stool not once. I have eaten raw fish with live parasites and nothing happened.
Well, not that I have tested for parasites either but I do not feel the need to. IMO it is impossible to avoid every parasite anyways. We have pets... they lick us and we cuddle with them... kids eat dirt... and let dogs lick their mouth.... and we kiss the kids... and we touch lots of stuff that could have parasites..... so what
we cant wash and sterilize our hands every minute, no?
Being afraid of parasites is like being afraid of bacteria. When we are healthy we can cope with them, I think we even need them for immune system.

I have been eating oysters even that were not really good anymore... to test.. and I still did not get sick! That was a cool biohack..lol My boss was there with me (he is a great chef that have been around the world on cruises for work, even in New Orleans, and he have opened too many oysters in his life) and he thought I might get sick, he said that oyster is not good anymore as he smelled it but I said let me eat it and see what happens.. i kinda wanted to feel how it feels like to get sick from an oyster... but it did not work..ha

We need to worry about all the artificial stuff we have invented that hurt us instead!
That is how I think and how I live.......

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-08-09T18:40:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parasites-in-seafood.16679/post-174292>

I wonder if many of the 'bad reactions' to raw animal foods, especially raw seafood, aren't just another example of the progressive neuro-endocrine-immunological de-evolution we've been facing as a result of poor food choices, less magnetism, nnEMF, etc.

Our microbiome would likely work in harmony with many (but maybe not all) 'parasites' in the past, when it was filled with copious amounts of many different strains of bacteria. This is why many parasites/worms are known as symbiots.

Also, there is the whole issue of excessive ubiquitination that Dr. Kruse has been teaching us about. I could be wrong, but wouldn't excessive blue light and exposure to nnEMF, _especially in the face of a lack of seafood+clean water, poor sleep, etc_, drive up ubiquitination so much in the wrong places at the wrong time that it could lead to increased mitosis and spread of parasitic and viral infections? Hence why we see such an explosion of mold related illnesses, lyme (especially neuro-lyme symptoms), herpes simplex virus related induction of neuro-cognitive illnesses, etc.

Without all these things, we likely experienced a positive feedback loop of increased consumption of raw seafood, frogs, eggs, meats, etc. in many different forms - raw, fermented, rotted, cooked, etc - which increased the diversity of our gut microbiome, interaction b/w symbiots, release and production of various neurosteroids and neural growth factors, etc. which coupled with an increased magnetic field (say, during a cold period) to lead to explosive brain growth and immune development, which allowed us to further adapt to new foods, environmental and social stressors, etc. At one point we were evolving and, perhaps flourishing. Now humans must do everything right to even stay "healthy" (or, at least maintain our current notion of health).

# Thread: Low carb diet constipation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-diet-constipation.16685/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### msclman — 2015-08-06T16:19:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-diet-constipation.16685/post-174103>

Any time i go into ketosis I get constipated. Does this happen to you guys? any cures?

### Frank Peters — 2015-08-06T17:18:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-diet-constipation.16685/post-174104>

I did Paleo for years and was plugged up a lot of the time. But I started the leptin reset 3-weeks ago and haven't been constipated. There are several differences between then and now. Two are from food and two are from my environment.

I am eating more fermented foods and drinking much more water now than I did when I was doing Paleo. Other than that, the diets are too similar to suggest anything more about food. I am in the sun more often now than I was when I was doing Paleo. Also, I am more connected when I am in the sun because I am purposefully walking barefoot on the grass.

Of these four things, fermented foods, lots more water, sunshine and walking barefoot, I don't know whether anyone of them is the difference that is making the difference.

### nonchalant — 2015-08-06T17:30:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-diet-constipation.16685/post-174106>

I think seafood will make the difference. Ketosis without much seafood tends to make your breath smell, dehydrate and also constipate you.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-06T18:20:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-diet-constipation.16685/post-174109>

This time of year in Nrothern hemisphere carbs are not real low so I am not getting why this is being asked now?

# Thread: Sara's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saras-journal.16687/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SVandy — 2015-08-06T22:39:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saras-journal.16687/post-174136>

Hello! I'm a 40 yr old mom to 8 (ages 5-15). I've battled various auto immune issues for most of my life, but didn't realize what was going on until about 3 years ago and I was in such chronic pain, I could no longer walk. From celiac disease, to MS, uveitis, hypothyroidism and more fun. I have chosen the natural path after a year of testing in western medicine. I have eaten an auto immune protocol diet for 3 years, and my entire family eats Paleo, or as epi-paleo as we can so far.

I have learned a lot through Jack's book, and recommendations and have applied a lot as well. My circadian clock is getting switched around, which is a huge piece to my puzzle, but I'm needing to grow some good bacteria in my gut and the light that was suggested, is out of stock (Ideas appreciated).

I have so many food sensitivities - and I completely lost all of my good bacteria 3 years ago after an ablation let to a severe UTI, which my OB prescribed cirpo for. The ablation was to fix the 3 out of 4 week bleeding each month.

Now I'm battling what we think is invasive carcinoma in my left breast. I have another MRI this month (following my MRI 3 months ago), to see if things have improved. I had shrunk my tumors a lot (it was 7.5 cm) but after applying Epi-Paleo, it seems that the tumors have grown back. I'm wanting to avoid surgery and any other conventional treatments.

I know my light cycles have improved, and I "feel" better, but I'm a little torn on what to do to have the best of both worlds.

We got a magnetico, and that has been awesome. We have figured out EMFs were making me super sick, and we are revamping that area of our lives (cut out WiFi, and turned off what we could in the car). We have lived in a "smart home" for 12 years, and are building a "dumb home" to into at the end of the year. I know I'm surrounded by loads of dirty electricity and it's making me sick.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-06T22:41:00+0300

### SVandy — 2015-09-24T00:52:25+0300

# Thread: Family
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/family.16688/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2015-08-07T00:19:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/family.16688/post-174141>

I love my family very much and appreciate my siblings more as time goes on, despite being triplets with a little brother and fighting a lot.

However, I am deeply concerned about my family and feel powerless and could really use some encouragement or advice.

First off, my mom. She has had allergies, vitiligo, hashimotos, then thyroid cancer, as well as chronic uncontrollable stress. She clearly suffers a lot and began to fall into the deep hole of the medical system (surgeries, drugs, etc.). She, however, eats nearly epi-paleo and drink a lot of good water- a start because of me.

My dad is overweight, definitely very, very stressed, but I don't believe he has any other chronic conditions. I believe he takes a statin.

My little brother spends all of his hours staring into his ipad, phone, and tv screen with emf from all those things and video games, etc., and beats headphones on.

My sister is a vegetarian (as I was until 2 years ago). She "doesn't sleep"- that's how she says it. Also, she has chronic leg pains and diminished function in her leg attributed by a doctor to some foot disease (probably dehydration and inflammation). Her body comp. is going south. She has always has had dark rings under her eyes. She is definitely very, very anxiety oriented.

My other sister seems very healthy but sleeps 15 ft from the wifi router, which causes me great concern. She works out hard running and rowing all year. I fear the long term consequences.

All in all, I feel a profound obligation to shout in their faces how they are ignorantly killing themselves with the help of special interests in the US, because I care about them and want them to live happy and disease free. However, doing that is not culturally acceptable and I dont want my whole family to hate me.

Any advice appreciated.

\- Matt

P.S. I will be leaving for Bosnia for 10 months in 6 days, so I want to leave them with what knowledge I can.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-07T03:05:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/family.16688/post-174159>

Focus on you........you cannot help people not ready to change. [http://menwithpens.ca/dont-help-people/](http://menwithpens.ca/dont-help-people/)

### nicld — 2015-08-07T16:36:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/family.16688/post-174183>

Leave a copy of Jacks book as a parting gift. It's up to them to read it.

# Thread: LBK's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 48
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LBK — 2015-08-08T01:22:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-174216>

What the heck, might as well give it a try. Never been successful at journaling, but need a way to organize my thoughts. Been around here on and off for a few years. Follow Jack's posts on facebook a little. I do check in on the forum occasionally. I rarely interact, but enjoy reading and appreciate all the helpful people. Generally, I get overwhelmed which stresses me. I give up, but I am always drawn back. Stumbled onto EMF 2 this time. It connected some dots for me (still reading) and I am taking notes for myself and my husband as I go along. My husband says, "Just tell me what to do". Ok, but only because I want someone else to live a long life with. I am still incredibly overwhelmed, but will try to come up with a plan on how best to conquer.

About me: Born in Kansas (lived there till age 8), raised in south Texas, lived in central Texas for past 30 years. I am 51 yrs. young ("half full"). Married 27 years. Husband age 55. 4 daughters aged 27, 26, 23, and 17. One granddaughter, age 4. Basically I've been tall and thin most of my life. 5' 8.5" Usually about 120-125 lbs, been as low as 115, but recently up to about 134. Up until about 3-4 years ago, chronic exerciser. Hooked on cardio. ( That was my excuse for not doing the leptin reset.) Gradually exercised less over the past 3-4 years. Currently doing nothing except walking the dogs. Lost my desire to work out. Try to eat epi-paleo or at least paleo. Never tried leptin Rx more than 2 days, though. Fall off the wagon often. Most of my life I was a chronic carb eater. All kinds: veggies, sugars, grains, whatever. Addicted to my daily coke from Sonic. Had a hard time wrapping my head around "fats don't make you fat". I was on the high carb, low fat train. (Gave up daily coke and replaced with daily Monster energy drink- lol, no better, I know.) I enjoy my family, but really have no friends. I am an extreme introvert. I tend to get hobbies that consume all of my energy until I just all of a sudden lose interest. I've been through a home building/decorating stage, a gardening stage (I can not literally fit another plant in my yard), a totally consumed with my kids stage, a chronic exercising stage, and am now into genealogy (I have almost 14,000 people in my tree) and crocheting (I have 4 large tubs full of crocheted items and have given away as much). I look healthy and don't have anything debilitating, but I know I am far from Optimal and only a matter of time before something comes up that I won't be able to ignore.
MY HEALTH HISTORY:
age 2 overdosed on my mother's iron pills (was told I had my stomach pumped)
Similar age, fell and hit the front of my head (near outer edge of eyebrow) on concrete (my mom said I probably needed stitches, but she didn't like hospitals so didn't take me)
Myopia since at least kindergarten. Stable vision since around high school/college. (-6.5 prescription for contacts both eyes although I have one under-corrected to -5.0 as my near vision was worsening as well) Told at last eye exam I have thinned tissue at the back of my left eye and to go to emergency ASAP if I see a bright flash of light-risk of retinal detachment. No signs of macular degeneration, yet. (Or ever, I hope!) Chronic dry eyes.
Chicken Pox, mumps and measles in elementary school. I was vaccinated for measles, but contracted them anyway (my mother had them more than once as a child and blind in one eye as result)
Probably undiagnosed ADD- Very forgetful, zone out a lot, foggy head, easily overwhelmed, hyper-focused (considering bio-feedback)
Herpes simplex- since childhood, almost always same spot on lower lip, more often in college, rarely now \[treat outbreaks with Zovirax (Acyclovir)\]
Mononucleosis in high school (energy levels have never been the same since)
Chronic sinus allergies since childhood, brow ridge close to nose always sensitive to touch, Sinuses most of the time feel very hollow and dry
canker sores since childhood-rarely now
Many cavities in childhood none in past several years (switched to non-fluoride coconut oil based toothpaste)
One root canal in my late 30's to early 40's (considering removal of tooth) Tooth is filled with gutta-percha \[_Gutta-percha endodontic filling points were found to contain approximately 20% gutta-percha (matrix),_ 66% _zinc oxide (filler), 11% heavy metal sulfates (radiopacifier), and 3% waxes and/or resins (plasticizer) source: [http://jdr.sagepub.com/content/54/5/921\]](http://jdr.sagepub.com/content/54/5/921])_, and has a porcelain crown fused with a high noble metal band. NTS: research removal of tooth
Some issues with receding gum line below bottom front teeth.
Racing heart occasionally- haven't been able to link to any one specific event, doc says could be unrecognized anxiety
Breast implants (Saline) at age 35 ( I am 51 now, so I need to address removal before they start leaking, if they aren't already)
Skin cancer started in mid 30's on face, chest, ear \[I forget which kind, but NOT melanoma, I now treat with Imiquimod (Aldara) or have frozen off whenever a spot shows up and that usually takes care of it\].
Chronic diarrhea for a few years now (only mornings usually)
Early Menopause- perimenopause started in late 30's to early 40's Menopause by mid 40's
Abt age 49 got a rash in a few spots on my face- dermatologist said was candida. Gave a script for something, I don't remember what, but didn't work. I think I used coconut oil to get rid of it.
Late 30's early 40's I was anti-depressants for awhile
Mid 40's Epstein Barr Virus (took acyclovir until levels dropped-no longer taking)
Scoliosis- worst at upper back/neck area
Skin hair and nails dry, deep vertical ridges in nails and soft flexible
Thyroid issues (border line hypo)- was on Cytomel for a few years, but took myself off it a couple years ago after improving my diet
Within the past year noticed my upper arms have a mottled coloring similar to a peeling tan
Last labs showed high levels of aluminum (high aluminum- alzheimer's connection?)
Area of thickened skin on the outside edge of left foot
Occasional Migraine headaches

What I am doing so far to help myself:
Supplements
Magnetico sleep pad and dark at night
Look at sun in the morning + ground
3 meals a day, no snacks (most of the time) Eat seafood (Wild caught salmon, canned sardines, clams) Now eating 2 meals/day.
Sun almost every day, at least 15-20 min. Increased time to 1-2 hours AM sun.
Recently gave up the daily energy drink, replaced with one 100mg caffeine pill ( I hate coffee)
Added a grounding cord to my laptop
Drinking Pellegrino or Topo Chico mineral water (Have Nestle Pure Life for drinking and cooking delivered to house as well)

Planning to:
Wear blue blockers at night and limit nighttime exposure to computer, phone, television
Start using a sleep mask
Gradually go to sleep earlier and wake up earlier
Find a good source for fresh Oysters and add to diet

Next post I will list the supplements and prescriptions I am taking, my biggest concerns health wise, and general questions I have so far (at least to remind myself what to research). I would post my last labs, but they were over 6 months ago, so will post next ones I get.

Haven't had the house measured or anything. Currently have 3 children living with (2 adults and one high school senior). With that said, counting me and the hubs there can be up to 5 cell phones on at the same time plus wi-fi. We get poor cell reception here, so I guess that's worse- puts out more EMF? Hubs says Smart meter is 200 feet from house. We are on one + acre lots here, but still pick up neighbors wi-fi. I turn off wi-fi at night here. Have a grounded wire outside that I try to go out and hold onto a couple times a day. I plan to get the hubs to help me with reducing EMF. He is an electrical contractor. Would like to get rid of wi-fi and hardwire everything. Just checked my Kindle and I DO have the book.

### LBK — 2015-08-09T20:52:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-174303>

Prescriptions:
1/8 tsp Hormone Replacement Therapy-Compounded Cream 2mg Estradiol, 45 mg Progesterone, 0.6mg/ml Testosterone
Imiquimod cream (substituted for Aldara)-As needed for for pre-cancerous areas on face, and chest
Zovirax Cream 5%-as needed for herpes simplex breakouts on lower lip

Supplements I am taking:
AM before breakfast:
1/3 tsp HLC High Potency Powder probiotic (pharmax)
4.2 g Inositol powder (Pure Encapsulations)
900 mg Colostrum (Pure Encapsulations)
.25 oz Detox Clay Powder (TheLiving Clay Co.)

AM with breakfast:
81 mg Aspirin
25 mg 7 Keto DHEA (Integrative Therapeutics)
D and K with Sea Iodine- 5,000 IU D3, 1,000 mcg K2 as menaquinone-4, 100 mcg K2 as menaquinone-7, 1,000 mcg Iodine from organic kelp and bladderwrack extracts, potassium iodide (Life Extension)
Adreset-400 mg Cordyceps Mycelium, 200 mg Asian Ginseng Root Extract, 50 mg Rhodiola Root Extract (Metagenics)
100 mg Ubiquinol QH (Pure Encapsulations)
800 mcg Folate as calcium L-5 methyltetrahydrofolate (Metagenics)
10 mg PQQ with BioPQQ Pyrrroloqquinaline Quinone (Life Extension)
600 mg Alpha Lipoic Acid (SuperiorLabs)
Corticare B- 75mg Vit C, 75 mg vit B6, 400 mcg folate, 60 mg Calcium, 750 mg Pantothenic Acid, 150 mg L-Carnitine , 3 mg Black Pepper Extract (Xymogen)
600 mg N-Acetyl Cysteine (Source Naturals)
Motivation Herbal Caffeine and Energy-200mg Caffeine (Natural Energy Labs)

PM:
360 mg Magnesium Malate (Designs for Health)

Things of concern to me are macular degeneration, glaucoma, and dementia/alzheimer's. I tested at 23andMe and macular degeneration and glaucoma among other things showed as genetically higher risks for me. Alzheimer's wasn't included in my testing, it's just something I worry about. (NOW I'M REALLY WORRIED ABOUT SINCE I NOTICED MY LAST LABS: ALUMINUM LEVELS WERE 58) 9-9-15 Started drinking 1 liter/day of Fili water to detox aluminum.
Family history of AMD: Grandmother, b. 1926, brown eyes, no corrective lenses needed until cataracts and AMD in 70's. Mother, b. 1947, brown eyes, blind in one eye from childhood measles, but no corrective lenses needed until cataracts and AMD in her 60's. Me: b. 1963, blue eyes, probably Myopia since birth or early childhood. Corrective lenses, glasses since age 7, contacts since age 15. (My contacts are -6.5 both eyes so glasses would be even stronger).
I want to concentrate on reducing non native EMF and blue light exposure. Given my concerns about eye diseases, I want start with addressing blue light, so I will be searching the forum, and Jack's blogs and webinars for info on this. (Also, it seems the easier path for me to start with as dealing with the EMF thing is gonna be hard for me to understand.)

Some questions that come to mind about UV Light and Blue Light:
I wear contact lenses that have some UV protection, so I go outside and look at the sun in the morning before I put my contacts in. ( I think I remember Jack saying to look at the sun with the naked eye?) So, is it not good to have this protection at other times while outdoors? Are sunglasses bad, or only wear them at certain times like mid-day? Should I wear blue blockers anytime I'm on the computer, cell phone, watching t.v. or just after sunset? Basically wanting to know when, and how to protect my eyes from what things?

Maybe optometry is catching on, Jack:

[http://www.healio.com/optometry/retina-vitreous/news/blogs/{339e8c98-1a84-4bc1-addc-6fca2ba005b9}/gary-l-morgan-od/blog-blue-light-awareness--finally](http://www.healio.com/optometry/retina-vitreous/news/blogs/%7B339e8c98-1a84-4bc1-addc-6fca2ba005b9%7D/gary-l-morgan-od/blog-blue-light-awareness--finally)

"Numerous studies confirm that cumulative lifetime exposure to blue light causes photo-oxidation of retinal cells that leads to AMD." here: [http://www.healio.com/optometry/news/blogs/{6d723bbc-f5bb-45a8-8995-2a70cdbf39aa}/gary-l-morgan-od/uv-vs-blue-light-which-is-more-dangerous](http://www.healio.com/optometry/news/blogs/%7B6d723bbc-f5bb-45a8-8995-2a70cdbf39aa%7D/gary-l-morgan-od/uv-vs-blue-light-which-is-more-dangerous)

[http://www.healio.com/optometry/news/blogs/{9110024b-d930-476b-ba74-f79c92202f4c}/gary-l-morgan-od/blog-blue-light-symposium-clarifies-opportunities-for-optometry](http://www.healio.com/optometry/news/blogs/%7B9110024b-d930-476b-ba74-f79c92202f4c%7D/gary-l-morgan-od/blog-blue-light-symposium-clarifies-opportunities-for-optometry)

[http://www.healio.com/optometry/retina-vitreous/news/print/primary-care-optometry-news/{5f61a476-55b5-401c-afc7-60ffd7f7793d}/customize-blue-light-protection-for-each-patient](http://www.healio.com/optometry/retina-vitreous/news/print/primary-care-optometry-news/%7B5f61a476-55b5-401c-afc7-60ffd7f7793d%7D/customize-blue-light-protection-for-each-patient)

[http://www.healio.com/optometry/news/blogs/{9b6a51b1-4e56-4b5e-82a2-d22e91dd7e46}/gary-l-morgan-od/blog-become-your-communitys-blue-light-expert?page=1](http://www.healio.com/optometry/news/blogs/%7B9b6a51b1-4e56-4b5e-82a2-d22e91dd7e46%7D/gary-l-morgan-od/blog-become-your-communitys-blue-light-expert?page=1)

buy directly------->>>

Micronutrient Test

Above test is easier to arrange all by your self.

There is another test that I think is super important,
specially that members of this board likely emphasize seafood.
I do not know how to get that test without a scrip,
unless this changes, to get the test done (LabCorp or Mayo Clinic), you need script.

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

\----
There is a load of similar tests offered, mostly worthless or outright salesman's pitch to get you buy fish or krill oils.
No to fish/krill oils and this type of tests.

### JanSz — 2015-08-11T03:15:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-174383>

> [LBK said:](/goto/post?id=174356)
>
> Thanks, JanSz! I have a pretty good doctor that would probably give me a script for any tests needed. He uses Quest Diagnostics for labs. Should I take tests while continuing with supplements, or discontinue taking supplements for awhile and then test to see what I actually need?

The Micronutrient Test is evaluating status of Lymphocytes.
More details here:
[http://www.spectracell.com/media/up...itamin-mineral-and-antioxidant-assessment.pdf](http://www.spectracell.com/media/uploaded/s/0e2059361_slides-functional-vitamin-mineral-and-antioxidant-assessment.pdf)

I would say keep taking all supplements without making any changes.
After you receive test results, re-evaluate.

You should not do this test more often than once every about 6 months.

If the test shows that you are not missing anything, then retest once a year or less often.

....

### LBK — 2015-08-16T21:13:42+0300

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=174321)
>
> Blue light is a big one and easy to cut out quickly.

Went to check out lenses at the optometrist and they carry Crizal brand specifically the Prevencia lenses. [http://www.crizalusa.com/scratch-smudge-dust-water-uv-glare-protection/lenses.html](http://www.crizalusa.com/scratch-smudge-dust-water-uv-glare-protection/lenses.html)
Looked through a pair and couldn't see any difference in colors with or without the lenses. (Awesome if they work!) They aren't amber coated, so maybe not true " blue blockers"? Says they protect against UV and Blue-Violet light. What I am thinking is get these for computer work and t.v. during the day and get the amber lenses for after sunset. I currently wear contact lenses with UV protection, so I'm thinking of switching to some without, so I don't block UV when I'm outside in the sun. My main concern is protecting against macular degeneration as I have tested at 23and me as higher risk, my maternal grandmother had it and my mom has the beginning stages at age 68. I am using my laptop a lot (grounded), as I am reading a lot here in the forums and blog. My head is spinning with questions. Time to jump in the pool!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-174904>

The Crizal Prevencia lenses block a certain range of frequencies in the blue light spectrum (415-455). I have a pair for work as our workspace is lit with fluorescents and I work on a computer frequently. Like them very much.

My nightime blue-blockers are the fitover style and they block far more - like 90+%.

### LBK — 2015-08-23T00:17:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-175400>

About 2 weeks on stricter epi-paleo. I have not counted carbs per say, but am keeping below 50g for sure. Probably under 30g. I have limited nuts to reduce omega 6 as ice causes headaches, so I'm sure I have high omega 6. Feel pretty good in regards to food. No cravings unless I'm bored, and even then not bad. I like crunchy things, so I've been eating Pork rinds (only ingredients are pork rinds and salt) with pastured butter with some of my meals. Don't have a source for oysters yet, but eating Wild sockeye salmon, Wild Planet brand sardines with olive oil and lemon, and Bumble Bee brand premium select wild baby clams, gulf shrimp when available. Some grass feed beef and some raw cheese. Getting in pool every morning and laying in sun. Glasses for computer, phone, Tv for daytime ordered (Crizal Prevencia). Also ordered some blue blockers at lowbluelights.com. Using the Uvex one right now, but they slide on my nose. Next on list is to get the EMF Bootcamp.

nice update......keep us informed.

### LBK — 2015-08-31T22:02:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-176124>

UPDATE: Ok, yesterday was crap, so I had to start thinking, "why?". I could barely muster up enough energy to move. Last night I drug myself to a restaurant with my husband because we found out they have oysters flown in from Alabama, I believe. So we each ate a half dozen plus grilled shrimp. I even ate a little of the exoskeleton on the shrimp. I think I needed that boost. When I started thinking about what went wrong, I knew I had not eaten enough breakfast (which by the way happened to be a good thing because that prompted me to actually count the grams of protein I normally eat for breakfast and haven't been eating enough. I was watching carbs, but didn't actually keep track of protein.) I also noticed a pattern of Sundays feeling like crap. I tend to sleep in a little later on Sundays, and there usually isn't a "plan" for the day. So, thinking about N=1, I realized I need at least a loose schedule. I think best for me is pool or river and yard work or other outdoor time after breakfast and no computer or phone in the morning. I am very foggy headed in the morning so I need something physical to jump start my brain. I think adhering more closely to schedule even on Sundays will help. I also think since I upped my protein for breakfast (added a can of sardines for 25g more protein), I will be able to do 2 meals a day. I am hoping I will sleep better on 2 meals since I will eat my 2nd meal earlier than I had been eating dinner. Shooting for ten to ten-thirty bedtime and seven to seven-thirty wakeup. Something else I thought about was I used to be on Cytomel for hypothyroidism, but after awhile of better eating, I took myself off it. Haven't been on Cytomel for a couple years at least now, but considering my thyroid function is most likely not close to optimal, I should maybe eliminate dairy. (I have been eating some raw cheese, eggs, pastured butter). I can use more Ghee in place of butter, and eliminate cheese, but wondering about eggs. I did show a slight reaction to eggs when an allergy panel was done awhile back. My Dr. just said that was common for someone who eats eggs every day. I haven't seen any change in my chronic diarrhea, so maybe I should eliminate all the foods I listed to see if there's a change. Eggs and butter is a daily thing for me, cheese is a little bit several times a week. Revisiting my thoughts about supplements I am taking (a lot of them), and I still wonder if I should quit them long enough to get a baseline of where I am and then only supplement what I actually need, or if I continue the supplements, how will I know if or when I can eliminate any of them? I plan to get my labs drawn after I complete the leptin reset, which might be 6-8 weeks from now, as the past 3 weeks I have not been eating enough protein for breakfast. By the way, I feel much better today! I had 50g of protein for breakfast, got in the pool for a little bit and worked in the yard about an hour and a half. It's almost 2:00 now, and not even the slightest bit hungry.

### LBK — 2015-09-02T22:10:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-176267>

Currently wearing glasses in the morning after looking at sun. Considered trying to do yard work without them, but my contact prescript is -6.5 (both eyes), I'm afraid I might get bit by a snake. I literally wouldn't see it unless it was right in front of my face. I try to remember to look above the frames several times while I'm outside. I generally put in my contacts after this until bedtime and wear the Crizal Prevencia as well if I'm on the computer and the blue blockers at night. My concern is maybe I should ditch the contacts all together? (Eyes deprived of oxygen?) Tried a little nude sunbathing, hopefully where the neighbors can't see me, actually-who cares? Why haven't I done this before? Editing my initial post again. Thought of a few more things to add. (I seem to leave out really important things like my last eye exam was told I have thinning in the tissue of the back of my left eye and to go to the emergency room ASAP if I see bright flash of light, and a few other things).

### LBK — 2015-09-15T00:54:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-177126>

Update: Still doing epi-paleo, 2 meals/day, 50 grams protein at breakfast, about half my protein from seafood. Carbs 30g or less. Sun and pool at least 1- 2 hrs every morning. Drinking a liter of Fiji water daily hoping it will help detox aluminum. Trying to go without glasses till about 11 or 12 in the morning. Day 2 of not wearing contacts, but hard to wear blue blockers over glasses (the weight hurts my ears), so I ordered a clip on pair till I can order some prescription ones. Also going to have to replace the lenses in my computer glasses with prescription ones. For those of you that get glasses with a weaker prescription than needed, how much weaker did you go? Today I was thinking cool would feel good on my eyes, so decided to take a baggie of ice water out with me. Well I discovered the first couple minutes with the bag over my face was very painful and felt similar to a migraine headache coming on. Pain and nausea. Got better after a few minutes, but still that weird awful pre-migraine feeling. Tested some other areas; on lower belly caused back pain and on chest caused trapezius pain. High omega 6? Anyway, my eyes felt great and actually watered. I have dry eyes that hurt in bright sun, but don't usually water. Also, I put Imiquimod (Aldara) on face and chest every once in awhile to detect any pre-cancerous spots. then I treat the spots once a week for several weeks. This time there were a lot more spots. Maybe it will take awhile to level out with the increased sun exposure.

### LBK — 2015-10-12T01:07:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-179146>

Still keeping up with the AM sun and seafood everyday. Getting used to not wearing contacts. Started mixing sea veggies seasonings, sea salt, tumeric, and black pepper with organic mustard and eating that with a can of sardines every morning. I have had a lot of trouble with post nasal drip (sore throat), sinus headaches and foggy brain in the mornings. It helps when I put a bag of ice water over my face about 10 min. while I'm outside in the mornings. One day last week I put the ice water on my face then on my abdomen and stayed in the sun a bit longer than usual and noticed I got a little sunburned, but only on my face and abdomen.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-12T01:23:04+0300

keep going. Watch the burns.........by watching the time. You have to build up a hello callus on your skin.

### LBK — 2015-11-04T18:47:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-181152>

Keeping up with epi-paleo, (not Leptin Rx now -did it strictly for about 6-8 weeks). I had previously gained a little weight (not overweight, just about 10 lbs more than I like to be), no change with the Leptin RX, but dropped the extra weight when added morning sun. My husband is on-board as well, so helps that we both eat similar diets. Reading another journal, someone said that eating oysters helped with focus more than just any seafood. I think I need to try that, as my biggest obstacle is focus due to "foggy brain". More days than not, I feel like I'm wandering around with no purpose. Some days I feel very focused. I haven't yet figured out why this is. Still having problems with chronic diarrhea, it is getting a little better, though. Allergies have been tough to deal with lately. Bad headaches, head feels like like a brick, especially in the morning. (We have had a lot of rain and flooding in the area, so molds have been super high.)
Goals for this week: Go get a corded phone for the house so I can turn off my cell when I'm home. I currently keep it in shielded case. Go in and get labs done. Pay more attention to drinking enough water.
I would really like to help some of my relatives, but at this point, I think they would just dismiss me as crazy. I have told my mom about getting morning sun, eating seafood and putting a blue light filter on her phone, which she is on constantly. I have an uncle who took the zoster vaccine and couldn't see two weeks later. He had a couple of eye surgeries, but vision hasn't improved much. He has diabetes and was recently diagnosed with pre-leukemia. I did tell his wife about getting am sun. I feel like I don't want to push too much too fast when it comes to helping family. Mainly concentrating on me and my hubby now. My kids are ages 17-27, and can't tell them anything, so trying to "tell" them by example.
Sun's out now, so gonna go get some.

### LBK — 2015-11-06T20:56:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-181285>

Not sure if I mentioned this before, but I have random episodes where my heart races for a few minutes up to about 30 minutes. I haven't had one in awhile (maybe a few months), but today while I was standing in the kitchen, my heart began to race. I counted 120 beats per minute. About 20 minutes later, it was still racing so I think, "go outside." I went outside in the front yard where the ground is wet and sat on a wrought iron bench with my feet on the wet ground. After about 2 minutes, my heart stopped racing. When I came back inside my heart rate was down to 67 beats per minute. Not sure if this was coincidence, but if it happens again, I'm going to go outside and ground myself and see what happens. I think I will start tracking episodes as well.

### nonchalant — 2015-11-07T00:47:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-181296>

> [LBK said:](/goto/post?id=181285)
>
> Not sure if I mentioned this before, but I have random episodes where my heart races for a few minutes up to about 30 minutes. I haven't had one in awhile (maybe a few months), but today while I was standing in the kitchen, my heart began to race. I counted 120 beats per minute. About 20 minutes later, it was still racing so I think, "go outside." I went outside in the front yard where the ground is wet and sat on a wrought iron bench with my feet on the wet ground. After about 2 minutes, my heart stopped racing. When I came back inside my heart rate was down to 67 beats per minute. Not sure if this was coincidence, but if it happens again, I'm going to go outside and ground myself and see what happens. I think I will start tracking episodes as well.

No coincidence.
Can you think of what happened to trigger the episode? Were you inside for a long time? Perhaps often standing near the refrigerator?

### LBK — 2015-11-07T04:20:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-181309>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=181296)
>
> No coincidence.
> Can you think of what happened to trigger the episode? Were you inside for a long time? Perhaps often standing near the refrigerator?

I had been inside. Wi-fi off, but in the kitchen cooking quite awhile. Most of the time more than 3 ft from fridge, but not much more. I am going to pay attention to where I am, etc. if it happens again. I have had these episodes ever since I can remember. At least by my teens, maybe childhood, too. I was told my dad had a heart murmur, but he died (unrelated to heart) when I was young, so I don't know for sure.

### nonchalant — 2015-11-07T19:30:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-181348>

The kitchen is often the worst room of the house, as far as electrical circuits and devices. Hope you can pin it down soon.

### LBK — 2016-01-26T02:54:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-187374>

Wow! Didn't realize I haven't posted since Nov. Still hanging in there. Fell off the wagon over the holidays as far as diet goes. Have been able to get sun a few days days a week even this winter and have been getting in the pool about once a week. This past week, however, got hit hard with allergies. Cedar and mold were high so I'm guessing those were the culprits. Always been sensitive to mold, but never had much of a problem with cedar till now. Worst allergy symptoms I've had in about 20 yrs. Sticking with sardines in the morning mixed with mustard and lots of spices. Getting fresh oysters once a week. Need to up my water intake, though. So easy to fall back into bad habits!

### fitness@home — 2016-01-26T12:44:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-187384>

^^ Cedar is starting activate allergies around here too. Have heard people at work complaining about it and my DH has been sneezing like crazy.

It's the start of spring allergy season...time to break out my Bionase

### LBK — 2016-06-13T23:02:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-196523>

Just some random things:
Wearing metal (jewelry): good or bad? Does it depend on when and where? i.e. good if outdoors, bad if in an office around wi-fi, etc? What about at night sleeping on a magnetico? Will search the forum later. Didn't want to forget.
Going to listen to June 2016 webinar when I get a chance. Sparked my curiosity as I'm into genealogy.
Been having trouble with carb cravings lately. Ate a can of sardines, 3 eggs, and 4 slices salami for breakfast this morning. Forgot to eat lunch, lol. Usually I'm starving by lunch time. Guess I had slowly reduced the amount of protein I was getting for breakfast, so I added an extra egg and a whole can of sardines vs. half. So easy to slide backwards without even realizing it.

### LBK — 2016-09-30T02:52:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-202103>

So, I posted on Facebook that I am going to opt out of being scanned next time I fly. This is the response I got: "The radiation dose from the airport scanner is less than the radiation you get from the sun in an airplane at 35000 feet in a few minutes; it is essentially negligible. As a radiologist I have EXTENSIVE training in radiation physics and have zero worry about cell phone, airport scanner and wifi radiation." I might just be at this guy's funeral.

### LBK — 2017-07-18T19:50:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-219281>

Update. Still here. Have been feeling pretty good lately. I still have problems with "foggy" brain and allergies. When I wake up in the morning my head feels heavy and my sinuses feel swollen and dry. Hoping this will improve when we move. I am beginning to understand what routine works for me. I do best when I start physical activity soon after waking. I have stared walking about 45 min. 3 x week with a neighbor and most other days I do yard work. I get naked sun in the morning several times a week. After morning routine I eat breakfast or if not hungry I wait till lunch. I've noticed in the summer I don't get hungry in the morning if I've had physical activity and sun before. I am 15-20 lbs heavier than when I was a workout fanatic, but I try not to let that bother me. It is a process of unlearning everything I thought was right. I am happier. I am not naturally outdoorsy, so I am learning to tune in to nature, to listen, to see, to feel. I actually crave the sun on my skin in the morning. Seeing things everyday that affirm some of Jack's wisdom is actually sinking in. I'm way behind, but I'll get there.
My husband and I purchased 10 acres in the country. ( It's about 15 minutes to the nearest city, so not really country.) Now to figure out how to build our house. My plan is to not not have internet. I figure I can go to the library a couple times a week to do what I need to.

### LBK — 2017-09-28T18:11:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lbks-journal.16693/post-224429>

First day to use Quantlet. P30 protocol, manual. Don't feel any different yet. Curious as to how my sleep will be tonight.

# Thread: Biohacking CERN?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-cern.16702/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-08-10T02:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-cern.16702/post-174317>

So here's crazy, weird post. I was thinking about the energy being released by CERN ([https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CERN](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CERN)) and the potential influences on Earth's electomagnetic field.

Recently, CERN has been running around 13 Tev.

[http://home.web.cern.ch/about/updates/2015/05/first-images-collisions-13-tev](http://home.web.cern.ch/about/updates/2015/05/first-images-collisions-13-tev)

There's lots of sketchy people all over 'the youtubes' talking about when CERN turns on, Earth's magnetic shields weaken when looking at the +RMC Magnetopause data, such as bending of Earth's shield. People are attempting to link a causation with CERN in this respect, but I'm very skeptical as any good scientist would be.

I'm not educated enough to know what this may mean with influence on humans, proximity to CERN, etc. But it makes me think, do we need to biohack ourselves when CERN turns on if there is an energy influence on us?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-10T03:28:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-cern.16702/post-174318>

when you turn your toaster on you affect the a butterflies flapping wings. CERN does have an effect but no one knows what it is because no one really wants to know............ignorance is bliss in particle physics.

### Scompy — 2015-08-10T04:45:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-cern.16702/post-174327>

Great analogy! The way I see it, the compartmentalization and overfocus on a scientific discipline often leads to conclusions based in "knowledge-depth" without considering a wider breadth of knowledge outside of one's discipline. I've witnessed a strong trend of biochemists and biologists living in a world not considering the importance of the underlying physics. Likewise, the physicists are not considering the biochemical ramifications because they are geeking out with their new toys. There are few people out there in the wild linking and bridging the connections among the sciences.

### Bill1 — 2015-08-10T05:17:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-cern.16702/post-174331>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=174318)
>
> when you turn your toaster on you affect the a butterflies flapping wings. CERN does have an effect but no one knows what it is because no one really wants to know............ignorance is bliss in particle physics.

Bigger question would be: what's the effect of HAARP ?? yikes

.

# Thread: Cause and Prevention of Heart Attacks
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cause-and-prevention-of-heart-attacks.16705/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-08-10T20:08:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cause-and-prevention-of-heart-attacks.16705/post-174350>

I had been aware of this video by Dr. Thomas Cowan but only just got round to watching it.

What made me do it was partly @[Gracelind](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/329/) 's posting the list of speakers at the WAPF conference here recently. I only wish I could hear Dr. Cowan, and the others, speak live.

Spoiler: he doesn't believe in the diet-heart hypothesis, and what's really at issue, he says - as a number of others do now - is inflammation. (The story he has to tell is, however, far more complex than this.)

I don't know whether everyone would get along with everything in this - for example, the thoughts from Rudolf Steiner, intriguing and suggestive as they can be, are certainly a bit "out there" - but I thought it interesting enough to post. Two things out of many that really struck me:

1\. He says that it's generally acknowledged that smoking causes heart disease. However, on the coronary artery theory of heart disease this would be strange. Smoking does not affect the coronary arteries. I wonder why people do not notice this kind of thing, and think about it. Sometimes I wonder if we're half asleep

2\. He says that there have always been two theories of heart disease. Furthermore, he says that back in the sixties the coronary artery theory, while it was around, was generally disbelieved by doctors. Again, this raises thoughts - namely, why have doctors become less thoughtful and more dogmatic? And what has caused that?

I was fascinated by the digoxin story - where its found in nature, how it's been used, and finally that it seems the body will make it to help the heart. Moreover, that it makes it out of … wait for it … cholesterol. Yet the medical profession has been over the last few decades, largely speaking, trying to lower cholesterol. They thought they knew it all, and the harm they must have been doing. The damage hubris does. _Kyrie eleison_.

Dr. Cowan also mention how when he was working in ER he had it in his mind that he should take people who had heart attacks off digitalis, theorising on the basis of thinking current at the time - which I guess shows that even the best of us can be led astray by false patterns of ideas.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-10T20:59:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cause-and-prevention-of-heart-attacks.16705/post-174352>

You said he says, "He says that it's generally acknowledged that smoking causes heart disease. However, on the coronary artery theory of heart disease this would be strange. Smoking does not affect the coronary arteries. I wonder why people do not notice this kind of thing, and think about it. Sometimes I wonder if we're half asleep" Smoking certainly does cause arterial disease because of its linked to pseudohypoxia.............when the mitochondria in the arteries are affected they contain more protons (inflammation) and less electrons. So its clear to me this "expert" is not an expert. He needs to read David Sinclair's work on pseudohypoxia. He seems to be a linear thinker and not a systems thinker.

### Gagnrad — 2015-08-10T22:05:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cause-and-prevention-of-heart-attacks.16705/post-174358>

Thanks.

Did you think I gave an accurate account? I've got no qualifications in the area, and I don't want to mislead people on your forum about what one of your professional colleagues has said. I was, of course, only picking out a couple of things that had happened to strike me - though perhaps I had, in any case, misunderstood what was said - and I'm not sure that much of the rest that was said wasn't actually more important. So even if I was accurate, I'm not sure I wouldn't have been misleading.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-10T22:46:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cause-and-prevention-of-heart-attacks.16705/post-174360>

Anthroposophic medicine claims that the heart does not pump blood but that blood propels itself along. Pollack's experiments showed us this because of EZ water. What Cowan believes is the source behind heart disease has little to do with biophysics. Digitalis helps preserve systolic heart function. Do you know that most cases of heart failure occur as a failure of autophagy and diastolic arrest? What causes that? Calcium ionic disturbance based upon the amount of protons and relative lack of electrons within the heart muscle. Most allopathic docs know it is inflammation, but Cowan is telling a bunch of lawyers we believe it is something else. FOS. In medical school over 20 years ago we knew 55% of fatal sudden MI has no atherosclerosis. How do you make EZ? It is entirely biophysical. Proteins loaded with electrons that become more hydrophilic. _Digitalis_ species thrive in acidic [soils](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Soil), in partial sunlight to deep shade. This "environment' is the same environment one would find in a person with bad mitochondria Gagnrad. Digitalis blocks the ATPase. Just like REM sleep does to us.......shocking huh? Where does the energy come from? If you've been reading the Ubi series.......you'd know. Water and light. It is a failure of half of the mammalian battery that supports photosynthesis and not what Cowan is telling people allopaths believe. The most credible thing he said is when he mentioned Viktor Schauberger. He did understand fluid flow and dynamics but not on the level we have today from Chaplin and Pollack. Cowan seems completely unaware of this long published data and defaulted to Steiner medical woo instead. This data was published decades before this talk in 2009. How do I know? Because I been reading it for ten years before his talk. His talk was all confirmation bias and I am glad it was too a bunch of lawyers. This is why I am wary of WAPF doctors.........NOT DETAILED ORIENTED AT ALL, in my opinion. They are interested in dietary dogma support WAPF. He should stick to acute care medicine as he is trained. When he stepped into the chronic disease management........he gave us more woo. He talked about diabetes and never once mentioned mitochondria. He never mentioned anything about blue light and its link to calcium efflux. More over, he did nothing to link that to how digitalis really works. pH rises and this increases the EZ of WATER!!!!! Pollack 101. He mentioned beta blockers in MI treatment: They are out of favor too in MI today!!! We have stopped using beta blockers for ten years already!!! He did not mention that either to his audience. We have also known we make digoxin endogenously since the 1980's and he says that he and 3 people know that!!! W Schoner - ‎1986 published cited data to that effect. We also know that people with stress (calcium efflux) all increase their LDL cholesterol levels to respond to the excess of protons which correlates with low pH and low EZ formation. These are some of the things off the top of my head after listening to this.

So where does digitalis really fit in biophysically? What does blocking the ATPase fundamentally do? It is a reversal of calcium efflux. Where have you heard that before? This MD Cowan lives in San Francisco.........who creates more calcium efflux in human hearts than anywhere around. Do you think he might look around his own environment to see what was happening? When efflux occurs calcium decreases inside the cell from its normal stores. This results in an increased intracellular concentration of [sodium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sodium) ions and thus a decreased concentration gradient across the cell membrane. This increase in intracellular sodium causes the Na/Ca2+ exchanger to reverse potential, i.e., transition from pumping sodium into the cell in exchange for pumping calcium out of the cell, to pumping sodium out of the cell in exchange for pumping calcium into the cell. This leads to an increase in cytoplasmic calcium concentration, which improves cardiac contractility. Again, it is a biophysical story not one based upon anthroposophic medicinal dogma. Some of what they do advocate is good, like exercise, massage, That dogma, by the way, also proposes that a patient's past lives may influence their illness. If you want to understand alternative medicine and its history read a bit about Edzard Ernest. Edzard Ernest reported that Rudolph Steiner used "imagination and insight" as a basis for his ideas, drawing mystical knowledge from the occult [Akashic Records](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akashic_Records), a work which is supposedly situated on the [astral plane](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Astral_plane), and which he said was accessible to him via his "intuitive powers". I can't support that kind of woo here.

He did not mention that either to his audience. We have also known we make digoxin endogenously since the 1980's and he says that he and 3 people know that!!! W Schoner - ‎1986 published cited data to that effect. We also know that people with stress (calcium efflux) all increase their LDL cholesterol levels to respond to the excess of protons which correlates with low pH and low EZ formation. These are some of the things off the top of my head after listening to this.
>
> So where does digitalis really fit in biophysically? What does blocking the ATPase fundamentally do? It is a reversal of calcium efflux. Where have you heard that before? This MD Cowan lives in San Francisco.........who creates more calcium efflux in human hearts than anywhere around. Do you think he might look around his own environment to see what was happening? When efflux occurs calcium decreases inside the cell from its normal stores. This results in an increased intracellular concentration of [sodium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sodium) ions and thus a decreased concentration gradient across the cell membrane. This increase in intracellular sodium causes the Na/Ca2+ exchanger to reverse potential, i.e., transition from pumping sodium into the cell in exchange for pumping calcium out of the cell, to pumping sodium out of the cell in exchange for pumping calcium into the cell. This leads to an increase in cytoplasmic calcium concentration, which improves cardiac contractility. Again, it is a biophysical story not one based upon anthroposophic medicinal dogma. Some of what they do advocate is good, like exercise, massage, That dogma, by the way, also proposes that a patient's past lives may influence their illness. If you want to understand alternative medicine and its history read a bit about Edzard Ernest. Edzard Ernest reported that Rudolph Steiner used "imagination and insight" as a basis for his ideas, drawing mystical knowledge from the occult [Akashic Records](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akashic_Records), a work which is supposedly situated on the [astral plane](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Astral_plane), and which he said was accessible to him via his "intuitive powers". I can't support that kind of woo here.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-11T04:19:51+0300

Why does the heart function like this^^^^^^^ to basal loops have more mitochondria because they move. The apex rarely moves therefore. This develops spiral or helical flow.Blood flow is normally described as laminar in that the blood travels smoothly or in regular paths. The
velocity, pressure, and other flow properties at each point in the fluid remain constant, all parallel to each other. Our understanding
has revolved around a two-dimensional representation of flow within three-dimensional (3-D) blood vessels. However, MRI and color
Doppler flow imaging techniques have demonstrated that there is a spiral/helical/rotational property to laminar blood flow. Why does this occur? Biophysics. Blood flow needs to touch the surface vessels to make connection with the electron density in arterial walls to develop the EZ in blood plasma. 93% of blood is water. The column of blood expelled from the helical heart turns on a central axis as it passes along the major arteries to allow maximum content to generate the EZ. As the EZ develops proton conduction cables forms in the center of arterial systems to augment flow without the expenditure of energy. This brings us to the work or Schauberger and helical or vortex flow. Feynman showed us in the 1960's that all vortices flow is quantized. I mentioned this a long time ago in the quantum brain and sleep blogs.
Before the twentieth century, _hydrodynamics_ was synonymous with fluid dynamics. Realize that blood is a magnetohydrodynamic fluid. This means it is capable of carrying more energy than water by itself. Why does the heart and circulatory system do this? To dance on the edge of the second law of thermodynamics. Blood itself is a Maxwell demon. This is why parabiosis experiments show the results they do.

The 2nd law says things get more random by the second, life says I have that covered because of my inherit design. Life will remain far from equilibrium to remain alive. Spiral design and vortices are purely quantum because they lower free energy costs. They lower ATP demand. That is why digitalis works! Tell me where any of this is in Cowan's talk? It is not.

1.  Houston JG, Gandy SJ, Sheppard DG, Dick JB, Belch
    JJ, Stonebridge PA. Two-dimensional flow quantitative MRI of aortic arch blood flow patterns: Effect of age, sex, and presence of carotid atheromatous disease on preva- lence of spiral blood flow. J Magn Reson Imaging. 2003 Aug;18(2):169-74.

    2\. Houston JG, Gandy SJ, Milne W, Dick JB, Belch JJ, Stone- bridge PA. Spiral laminar flow in the abdominal aorta:a predictor of renal impairment deterioration in patients with renal artery stenosis? Nephrol Dial Transplant. 2004 Jul;19(7):1786-91. Epub 2004 May 25.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-11T04:31:27+0300

What has the surgery data told us that Cowan does not seem to know? That endothelial cells are exquisitely sensitive to juxtamural turbulence, areas of stagnation, stress gradients, and/ or high oscillatory shear stress. As a neurosurgeon this is what makes aneurysms so deadly in the cerebral vascular circulation. It then follows that Schauberger described the macroscopic advantages that Feynman laid out in quantum vortices to explain why the stabilization of laminar flow is likely to be a deep evolutionary advantage in keeping flow brisk while blood pressure low. The heart is not a pump..........but not because of the reason Cowan asserts.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-11T04:39:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cause-and-prevention-of-heart-attacks.16705/post-174392>

So where does Schauberger come in? He was a an expert forester from a family of experts. Using his knowledge of the forrest, Schauberger learned to waited for the time when the water current would be at its strongest to move trees from the forrest in water ways. When was that? It was during the **_early hours of the morning_** when the moon was full. This is precisely when we build the largest EZ in water. This means it would augment EZ and proton flows in water. Schauberger directed his workers when the right moment would be for the timber to enter the water and in one night 1600 cubic meters of timber were brought down the mountain to a temporally constructed pond in the valley where his family was hired to harvest wood. Schauberger was the first to visualize the macroscopic power of the EZ in water. This occurred 100 years before Pollack's experiments.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-11T04:54:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cause-and-prevention-of-heart-attacks.16705/post-174393>

Schauberger used his position to investigate the forests and its streams his family worked and one of the first anomalies he encountered was a previously opulent mountain stream had unexpectedly dried up after the _old stone hut which stood over its source had been dismantled_, exposing the spring to the _light and sun_. Why would this matter? Simple.......the stones acted as the hydrophilic barrier to build the EZ in the ground water to allow its proper forward flow. Various explanations were considered and finally the suggestion to rebuild the stone hut was followed up and after a while the spring returned. Schauberger never tied the hydrophilic stones and sunlight together but his observations were keen.

So let us talk about how blood and vessels share some optical abilities. Did you know that both hemoglobin and arterial walls have the exact same absorbance of light? The aorta and hemoglobin’s peak light absorption peaks at 280 mm 420nm 540nm and 580nm and has sharp cutoff at 600 nm since green and yellow wavelengths of krypton ion lasers peak at 531 mm and 568 mm respectively they almost perfectly match the absorption peaks of hemoglobin so these laser colors are used for coagulation of blood and blood vessels. We use this in surgery all the time. Those wavelengths of light correspond to AM sunlight frequencies. They are also present in full moons. Schauberger was a great observer. He just never linked it all to light.

Viktor Schauberger, with time and observation, grew quite sure that water responded to the shade of the forests from which it sprang. So he knew light was a big deal. He begun to perceive water as the life blood of the earth and surmised that it must be allowed to follow its own course if it is to remain unspoiled. He concluded that watercourses are shaped by winding curves and shaded banks to protect itself from direct sunlight and that its **_low temperature_** and **_natural flow_** was the condition necessary for water to preserve its supportive and carrying strength. Low temperature and hydrophilic environments create the largest EZ in water..............

### Gagnrad — 2015-08-12T20:26:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cause-and-prevention-of-heart-attacks.16705/post-174506>

Thanks, Jack.

A very much longer and more detailed post than your previous ones. And, golly! followed up by a flurry of other posts. I'm not sure you answered my question, which, if you recall, was "Did you think I gave an accurate account" of what he had said? But never mind.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=174360)
>
> The most credible thing he said is when he mentioned Viktor Schauberger. He did understand fluid flow and dynamics …

Ah, you noticed that. One supposes Schauberger would have. Interesting to me that what medics generally believe doesn't square with this.

> This is why I am wary of WAPF doctors.........NOT DETAILED ORIENTED AT ALL, in my opinion. They are interested in dietary dogma support WAPF.

Well, that's very sweeping, Jack. You've based that one one video by one man. Still, I suppose you've already burnt your boats with the Paleo Folks and the LCHF crowd, so why leave any turn in Ancestral Health unstoned?

You really need to calm down, Jack.

> Edzard Ernest reported that Rudolph Steiner used "imagination and insight" as a basis for his ideas, drawing mystical knowledge from the occult [Akashic Records](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akashic_Records), a work which is supposedly situated on the [astral plane](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Astral_plane), and which he said was accessible to him via his "intuitive powers". I can't support that kind of woo here.

So there is, then, one thing you would agree that I had accurately summarised in that post, since I said (using more moderate language that you, of course, are wont to use) "the thoughts from Rudolf Steiner, intriguing and suggestive as they can be, are certainly a bit 'out there'. "

What I would say to you, however, is that you should go back to original sources, preferably in the language in which they are written, rather than relying on what someone has said someone has said about someone.

But, of course, Steiner is beside the point here, anyway. The point is what Cowan thought about heart disease. And he has, you will recall, a crate of papers in three different languages, correspondence with several cardiologists, and some twenty years reflecting on the matter. None of this means he is right, of course. Understanding things is hard, and we all makes mistakes. But it would seem to add up to something.

If I were you I wouldn't hold out for an invitation to speak at the next WAPF conference. I'm sure the likes of Gerald Pollack would always be welcome, but you can't hope to keep spitting venom at people and still find a warm welcome.

Here are the details:

# Thread: Geopathic Stress & Cancer

### SpiritGuide — 2015-08-11T17:25:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/geopathic-stress-cancer.16708/post-174413>

Hi All,

I wasn't sure where to post this, but I wanted to share it since it's got some great information, especially considering the backbone of much of Dr. Kruse's work is based on magnetic fields and EMF's, this article goes into detail on some very fascinating information about how the magnetic fields of the Earth interact with each other, and offers some suggestions on how to combat this issue.

These intersecting lines of geo-magnetic force can cause disruptions in your body's natural defenses against diseases such as cancer, so something as simple as where you place your bed in your home can make a difference on whether or not you'll get sick.

The good news is a simple compass needle can be used to determine where these danger zones are located so you don't wind up hurting yourself by sleeping in the wrong spot.

The bad news is these magnetic lines are everywhere, in fact they are in grids which are only about 10 feet apart, so every home has some spots in them which are unhealthy to stay in for any length of time... so it could be worth a read at the very least, and perhaps some simple in-home tests to make sure your couch and your bed are placed in areas which won't negatively affect your health.

Link: [http://www.healingcancernaturally.com/geopathic-stress-and-cancer.html](http://www.healingcancernaturally.com/geopathic-stress-and-cancer.html)

### SpiritGuide — 2015-08-11T18:28:15+0300

As a brain tumor survivor I can tell you Dr. Kruse your my hero!

I'll never fall victim to my own ignorance again when it comes to unseen forces in the environment, not to mention I'll be eating oysters and getting a magnetico in the very near future, as well as moving to one of two locations:

1.) Jacksonville, FL (North of I-10)
2.) Denver, CO

If you had to pick one of these locations to live which would it be? Thanks!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-11T18:58:37+0300

# Thread: Nuk - DNA Spreader (psoriasis)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuk-dna-spreader-psoriasis.16710/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kcehealth — 2015-08-12T16:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuk-dna-spreader-psoriasis.16710/post-174487>

Well here we go.
Introduction... Male 56... Psoriasis reduced by 90% after 40yrs of acceptance and lost 50lbs with more to loose... 25-30.Paleo knocked out brain issues (depression - panic attacks - brain chatter) and my journey led me to ketosis and Wahls Paleo Plus. Just can't eat the volume of carbs she suggests but do the balance.
Just introduced earthing and yellow lens glasses to my life... plus my very supportive wife. Backpacking SE Asia and finding things, like proper blue blockers hard, to find. Had to buy a volt meter to test the grounds (some aren't connected) and made my own grounding strap. Also and most important morning earthing outside.
Following circadian seasonal light cycles and plan to eat only locally bio available foods based on North American native history. They're being very supportive via email. Right now we're eating more carbs in anticipation of our return to Vancouver this fall. Light cycles almost matching with Asia. Eat lots of seafood and always have but it appears tubers we're saved and they'll get back to me if they stored any fermented items. Pre European.
I'll be very interested to see how we finish our eating by light cycles and bioavailability as we move into fall and thru winter.

First question... Started reading about altitude changes when done rapidly (beyond foot) via car. I've notice my skin does react. Could be skin tightening or some other mechanism. Question is. Living at an altitude above sea level means no seafood (using the by foot thinking). What about eating foods that were never bioavailable, example seafood, at communities that are often around 1500feet?

Sorry it's going to take a few months to get thru the blogs (enjoying our travels so much) so need some direction as I want to make sure this seasonal change is done right.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-12T16:50:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuk-dna-spreader-psoriasis.16710/post-174493>

Sept 2015 webinar all about your condition.........FYI

### kcehealth — 2015-08-13T06:33:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuk-dna-spreader-psoriasis.16710/post-174543>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=174493)
>
> Sept 2015 webinar all about your condition.........FYI

A month away... Not to long to wait.
Noted on my calendar. Thanks

### kcehealth — 2015-08-16T09:19:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuk-dna-spreader-psoriasis.16710/post-174817>

Question on the blue blocking glasses. Anyone else noticed a shift in the type of lense (day use verses night use) you're using.

Night driving... When I'm driving an amber/yellow lense at night keeps me on the road... Orange just blocks to much (driving).

Blue screen electronics... Orange seems to be to much if used during the day and but good in the evening. Yellow during the day. Wee testing of my sleep quality.

During the day while out and about... Just sunglasses in direct sun but if under a tree outside I do my best not to wear them.

This is based on my continued earthing and morning light triggers. And as much outside light during the day as I can muster though in peak sun hours there's a hat and lightweight clothing involved. Skin exposure before and after peak sun.

Anyone else found lense type and time of day correlations?

### kcehealth — 2015-08-23T10:19:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuk-dna-spreader-psoriasis.16710/post-175428>

Week 2 ish. Been doing very well this week. Notices under two nails copper colours forming. Need to revisit my diary cuz it's usually a deficiency in something. Weird cuz diet pretty steady. And my loose stool (not panic diarrhea) turned in 2 1/2 days of constipation which followed by 3 discharges within 16hrs. Nails started just before the constipation. I've reviewed my eating, earthing and blue light and nothing of note. Had some very good news type stress but happy stress hasn't shown negative effects before.

Try to keep up on a regular schedule with my journal here... Personal one done everyday.

# Thread: Cortisol, ROS, & so Leptin Rx questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-ros-so-leptin-rx-questions.16711/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 66
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Frank Kaplan — 2015-08-13T01:03:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-ros-so-leptin-rx-questions.16711/post-174520>

I just read The Epi-Paleo Rx. I have a few questions - sorry this is longer than I thought it would be.

From the book:

“D. Do not work out before or after breakfast. In obesity your cortisol is already high, so we don’t want to drive it higher to cause a pregnenelone steal syndrome. This will cause adrenal fatigue, cause your sex steroid hormones to crater, and worsen your body composition.”

Excerpt From: Dr. Jack Kruse. “Epi-Paleo Rx.” iBooks. [https://itun.es/us/IHTwK.l](https://itun.es/us/IHTwK.l)

**Cortisol:** My cortisol levels are not high. ONLY high in the evening (not high as suggested in the book, but I don't know what his general statement of being obese and "high" really mean exactly as there's not enough info to back it up.

I received results from the Pharmasan 4 x day Salivary Test. It shows I start out at 5.3 at 7:55 am (it should be between 8 to 10 on a 0 to 10 scale). I tested at 4.2 at 12:55 pm, down to a low of 3.0 at 7:55 pm, rising to a high at 6.2 at 11:18 pm when it should the lowest of the day. I've tested like this for the last 15 years or so.

I wake up feeling just okay and don't have really good energy. I can and do take a nap either mid-morning if I am really tired, or after lunch, if and why I can depending upon my schedule. This energy drop off doesn't change too much whether I eat carbs or avoid them completely. I am going to stop eating at 7:30 pm and eating the BAB within 30 minutes of rising. I've done this for a while now with no change in how I feel. I walk on the grass barefoot and stare as the morning son. I also put f.lux on my computer as well for evening reading or work I have to do.

**Two questions:**

**Napping:** Is it okay to nap during the day if my energy levels are really low or should I tough it out?

**Working Out Idea:**

Given cortisol starts off for lower than idea in the morning, and working out causes a rise in cortisol per the book, wouldn't it make sense for me to workout in the am instead of between 9 am and 4 pm (but at least an hour after I have my BAB)? Working out can mean weights or walking. I don't do HIIT or walk/run at a hard pace.

**Walking:** Another question regarding walking and cortisol 0r **ROS:** Can I walk at a relatively slow pace of 220 - age x 70% for 30 to 45 mins really hurt? It helps me think clearly and gets my blood flowing even though I'm not killing myself and breathing too hard. I feel good for many hours after I do this.

**Heavy Cream vs Raw Heavy Cream:** I've seen a few times now heavy cream being mentioned and being okay in coffee. Some say raw heavy cream, others saying heavy cream. Since I don't have access to raw heavy cream, is heavy cream okay when my BMI is 30 to 35 or for my friend who is 40%?

**Water:** Is there a recommendation on how much water I should be drinking per day? I know it has to be spring water or RO water.

**Supplements:** Aside from taking a pure form of Fish Oil, B-Complex (needed while I take Metformin), CoQ 10, and Vitamin D, are there any other supplements like PPP I should be taking? I realize the book doesn't have a recommendation for supplements before I'm Leptin sensitive. Full Disclosure: I'm currently on HRT - .60 cc's of Cypionate a week / divided by 3 and .05 Arimidex 3 days per week. I've also been taking 500 mg of Metformin 2 x's a day. I'm under an endocrinologist's care for these.

**Morning Protein Shake:** Since there are times I need to get out of bed and leave immediately, I do the following quick smoothie in my bullet: 1.5 scoops of Jay Robb's Egg Protein - 24 grams of protein per scoop, 4 small organic and pastured eggs, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil (just eliminated Udo's Oil 3-6-9 oil based on what I read in the book), 1 cup of Bragg's Apple Cider Vinegar / Stevia drink (no sugar, no carbs, no calories, etc...) and 1/4 cup of Hemp Milk (not always). I just ditched Tera's Grass Fed Whey Protein or even Bulletproof Whey due to it potentially spiking insulin (which I just read in the book or the blog). If there's any other good tasting, healthy protein power out there anyone recommends? (this knowing full well eating real food is the best choice)

Thank you.

### nonchalant — 2015-08-14T19:48:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-ros-so-leptin-rx-questions.16711/post-174683>

I believe your cortisol rates will be high with obesity. However, with time, you use up enough resources, so that your cortisol levels drop. I was in that position a few years ago. Energy levels tanked.

As far as your mild cortisol spike in the evening, you need to avoid light after dark on your eyes or skin, including blue light emitted by microbes in your gut. Don't eat after dark.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-15T23:13:54+0300

> [Frank Kaplan said:](/goto/post?id=174520)
