> About games: For me, as a professional game designer for the past 20+ years, I can 110% tell you I've had xerophthalmia off and on. Not only do I boot up games about 50+ times a day to make them, I also play a ton outside of work for 'research.' But after many of the biohacks, I don't get eye issues anymore. I see my son getting issues. I'm very knowledgeable with game-related or tech-anything because of my skillset... I put together my own computer systems, I hardwired everything in my home, etc. When I recently discovered that many of my consoles emitted Rf, I curbed my son playing with the Wii anymore. It registers 5 to 20 mW/Sq.m on average along the right-side of the Wii remote controller. Shutting down the Wii causes all Rf to go away. But, the Xbox360 outputs all of the time as I mentioned, even when plugged in. A person must physically unplug the X360 unit from the wall or from the power-supply to stop all Rf-EMF. So I enviro-hacked the X360 a bit now... we only use hard-wired controllers, but also, the Rf coming off comes out of the front near where the power-button is... if you turn the X360 180-degrees, the Rf will emit away from the gamers that are playing.

My 14 year old son is addicted to his xbox and has had similar eye issues. We use hard wired controllers and unplug the modem and take it to bed with me every night. He refuses to eat fish. He refuses to swallow a fish oil supplement. He refuses to drink the RO water I have...He refuses to do a lot of things. All efforts to monitor and limit his time on the xbox were futile and so about 3 months I took it away (and the iPad) completely. Once he hit puberty his attitude and behaviour really became intolerable at home and at school and I attribute this to the xbox that I bought xmas 2013...before that he had very limited time on gaming consoles. All he wanted to do was be on the xbox - no homework, no sport, no friends...just sitting for hours in front of the television. I spent so much time arguing with him about turning it off and doing something else. He stole money from my credit card to fund his xbox playing and refused to do anything else. I really wish I had never ever bought it. After I took it away he rampaged around the house trying to find it - this went on for hours and hours each time....breaking things in the house and violent behaviour towards me. His behaviour and attitude slowly improved but just this weekend he had a friend come for a sleep over and he bought his xbox with him and Nathan played it for about 6-8hrs in a 24hr period and his behaviour and attitude went right back to what it was before. This xbox has seriously limited my sons development and I made a massive error in judgement buying it for him. I will never give it back to him. But he is 15 next year and he has already told me the first thing he will do when he turns 15 is get a job and buy xbox.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-23T01:57:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visual-sensory-detection-of-bad-redox-and-or-nn-emf-a-possiblity.16762/post-175409>

Puberty and artificial light is huge issue............(big for great CA and infertility too)
Melatonin induces protective autophagy enhancement via an AMPK-dependent pathway. I've been telling you this for 5 years. Now the science is catching up. This autophagy enhancement regulates cardiac muscle cell apoptosis and protects against heart damage caused by chronic intermittent hypoxia (CIH). This is important for athletes and people with chronic diseases who want to begin to increase their redox potential. When we are pseudo-hypoxic in our mitochondria NAD+ drops and disease manifests or an athletes redox drops. [http://www.sciencedirect.com/…/article/pii/S0006291X15302023](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0006291X15302023)

Fake light causes your mitochondria ECT to slow because the respiratory proteins enlarge to slow electron tunneling….…..when it slows you up regulate carbohydrate metabolism by way of AMPK pathways. All this from the change in frequency of light. The brain can not tell sunlight from blue light.......and ancestral health is unaware of this. You no longer can afford to be.

**What does pseudohypoxia (low NAD+) mean to our hormone panel? The cost of a high aerobic capacity is low fertility. This is why the pregnenolone steal syndrome exists and why young males now suffer pandemic low testosterone's and females have an upside down PG/E2 ratio's WHEN docs finally look. Pediatrician's rarely do because they have no way to fathom how surface fake light can do this. Ignorance of physics is why. Not their fault. They were not educated in this fashion. You usually have to go to a endocrinologist and beg. It is not what the anti-aging docs think. Cortisol over sex steroid production is due to the use of oxygen as the terminal electron acceptor in human mitochondria in a really bad light environment. Lack of catalase (H2O2) and AM UV light fuel this low fertility. Artificial light makes fertility doctors rich and busy when children become reproducers..........this is how it begins in adolescence.**

**With time it will be proven surface chemistry of the skin and gut is more important than biochemistry for humans. You can hold me to it.**

**Medicine applies technical solutions to adaptive challenges today and this is why it is failing.**

### Scompy — 2015-08-23T07:12:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visual-sensory-detection-of-bad-redox-and-or-nn-emf-a-possiblity.16762/post-175419>

> [Richelle Jones said:](/goto/post?id=175408)
>
> My 14 year old son is addicted to his xbox and has had similar eye issues. We use hard wired controllers and unplug the modem and take it to bed with me every night. He refuses to eat fish. He refuses to swallow a fish oil supplement. He refuses to drink the RO water I have...He refuses to do a lot of things. All efforts to monitor and limit his time on the xbox were futile and so about 3 months I took it away (and the iPad) completely. Once he hit puberty his attitude and behaviour really became intolerable at home and at school and I attribute this to the xbox that I bought xmas 2013...before that he had very limited time on gaming consoles. All he wanted to do was be on the xbox - no homework, no sport, no friends...just sitting for hours in front of the television. I spent so much time arguing with him about turning it off and doing something else. He stole money from my credit card to fund his xbox playing and refused to do anything else. I really wish I had never ever bought it. After I took it away he rampaged around the house trying to find it - this went on for hours and hours each time....breaking things in the house and violent behaviour towards me. His behaviour and attitude slowly improved but just this weekend he had a friend come for a sleep over and he bought his xbox with him and Nathan played it for about 6-8hrs in a 24hr period and his behaviour and attitude went right back to what it was before. This xbox has seriously limited my sons development and I made a massive error in judgement buying it for him. I will never give it back to him. But he is 15 next year and he has already told me the first thing he will do when he turns 15 is get a job and buy xbox.

I can see how behaviors can shift with some kids in the realms of video games, as they can be addictive and it's often hard to keep the timing of some games to a window... the critical ages I'm finding has been in the 5-7 year old to really make a stand and implement specific times to do specific activities. We cannot 'be the friend' with our children before 'being the parent' I've found out, or they'll always take advantage of us. Instead, we need to be the parent first, and sadly that means, being the jerk in their eyes at times. But if we keep our cool and show strength and be stern about it, they'll respect us even more in the long run. It's 'tough-love' but they have to learn it.

They test every parameter possible with a parent and we cannot give in because it ultimately means they control us instead of us guiding them and giving them the wisdom they require. As a video game designer, I joke with myself about all of this on the 'tactical front.' I've found it's literally about finding new tactics to figure out ways to get what 'we the parents' want. It's why reward systems are necessary I've found, so they have to learn how to work and earn things, as well as earn the time to do things. I've implemented in my household a window of time for my kid to play games, a time for stories, a time for 'lego-adventures and other table-games', a time for doing school work, a time for eating, a time for bathing, etc. Without us setting patterns earlier in their lives, kids will make up their own pattern and that behavior can be set strongly and may be really hard to break in the later years.

I view this the problem at its root as addiction and idolization. It's something we all face in certain facets of our lives? Chocolate anyone? If it an Xbox now, it may be something else later. Think about entertainment in the past... some people would record mega-hours on their VCRs and watch them all night long after returning home from work. In College, I was a fencing master and also had joined the SCA (Society for Creative Anachronisms) that had fencing aspects in it. (SCA is a medieval type group that re-enacted fantasy-like stuff, women would dress up in medieval gear, men would wear armor, weekend camping trips were about staying in-character as if people were back in the 12th century, etc.). I saw immediately a lot of SCA people were extremely addicted to their hobby and it became unhealthy to the point of making some of them want to live it 24/7 rather than looking at it more like a temporary moment of fun. To many, it became their life. It's something they looked forward to every weekend as a way to escape the normal world, but it was an addiction nonetheless for some (not all as everyone has their reasons for doing this type of stuff). The modern version of SCA is morphing into video-game related stuff, like Comicon where people will go to events and dress up re-enacting and escaping as their favorite gaming characters. Role-playing games in the past were similar in this regard because many would get so engrossed with other players around the table and with their Character that it becomes far more addicting than reading a great book. The combat is designed to weave in and out of the storytelling. There are many levels of addiction too, from moment ot moment combat, to expecting one combat to another, and the freedom a player can interact in these types of games to overcome challenges and manipulate outcomes. This is why there is a crossover between table-top games at the root of many video games, as they carry the same type of mechanics that pouncing on one's addictive natures.

For these reasons, everyone needs the basic skills of time-delegation and when something becomes an idol in their lives over the virtues. It's the same reason why some carboholics justify their high-carb eating simply due to the biochemical addiction and responses to the sugar-highs. Once a person has the skills to master addiction by seeing it for what it is and how it can negatively impact their lives, and all of the ramifications that go along with it, only then will a person understand. In my household, I've also created 'family day' in which every Sunday, my son has to do something with the family whether he likes it or not. Now with his blue-blockers, the only way he gets to use the computer past sunset is to have them on, and typically we now limit that window and replace it with other activities--reading a book under amber light or telling creative stories.

### Bill1 — 2015-08-23T07:41:37+0300

### Scompy — 2015-08-23T19:28:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visual-sensory-detection-of-bad-redox-and-or-nn-emf-a-possiblity.16762/post-175438>

Thanks, it's more than a full-time job to be a parent, and when the children are resentful, willful, don't listen, can easily go enraged, etc., it can be exceptionally hard for any parent to parent. We often resort to knee-jerk reactions rather than try to give thought-out guidance. I really believe each parent needs to discover and acquire what unique methods of behavioral-ammunition are possible with each given child; it is required if you want to one-up them and since we're all unique, we need unique to employ unique tactics. When parents have done some homework to figure out what strategies that can be used to address many conflicts, it's like pulling an ace or king from a deck of cards at one's disposal for handling a situation. When the deck of cards do not exist in the first place, and the rules of the game have not been established, the children will make up their own games as the creative beasts they are, and decide what they want to do to override the parent.

I've found tactics of distraction and limited choices can be very powerful. Asking a younger child to make adult-decisions is not the right approach--it undermines the parent and also leaves too many options open in the child's mind. It's better to offer Choice A or Choice B, or sometimes Choice A, B or C. Never leave it up to the child to answer blanket questions. Trying to 'be nice' is translates into weakness at some point and they'll walk all over the parent when they want to get their way. A wrong example would be to ask a child, "What do you want for dinner?" Instead, if the question is to be asked at all in my house, it would be, "Would you rather have soup or a hamburger tonight for dinner?" The child's mind will start to make choices around what is offered rather than thinking about desserts. Really the better choice would be what we were all brought up on... mom or dad says, "Okay, we're having spagetti and meatballs tonight!"

Someone else made the decision rather than the child. If the kid doesn't like the outcome, it forces the kid to respond. Specific strategies come in when the parents \*expects\* the child to complain and attempt to control the meal in this case... when a parent is ready with the right answer, it works wonders especially when that answer is fun, playful and the child interprets the response as a benefit. If I were to say, "No, I've already got the stuff out and I'm making it!" in a harsh tone, it's a guarentee that my child would rebel and probably do something else to distract me so I couldn't make it. But if my response is semi-distracting, smiling and cheerful like, "well let me see, if you help me with dinner, I'll add an extra marble for your earning box! Sounds good?" The kid's mind usually switches away from eating the food to "helping = more rewards" (while also getting a parent's smile and encouragement). When the child insists on not-eating, it's sometimes better to say nothing or distract in other ways by changing the subject altogether, and then when it comes time to eat, everyone eats at the same time so the child feels obligated to eat with everyone and at least try something out. With food, we have to remember that everything tastes bad until we try it 7+ times.

It took a long time for my 6-year old to eat certain things, but using tactics like, "I'm making it different this time, adding more stuff you like--it won't be like that past stuff you had that tasted horrible..." That has gone a long way to get him to eat a great deal more. My best concoction is a garlic, chicken broth, broccoli, asparagus, carrot, chicken and noodle soup with having everything in it finely shredded. This way, he only sees an off-greenish broth with carrot and noodles, but is still sucking down all of it. That's right, I also have to finely shred the chicken because he's so picky that he won't eat it normally, but will eat the 'carrot coins'. Now over time, he likes the flavor and at some point, I can tell him, "oh, ya want some of this chicken? It's just like you have in your soup. I'm thinking you'll like it." If he refuses, fine, but use these types of tactics as a way to warm them up over longer periods of time rather than wanting instant gratification myself. I have to plot out a long timeline with most things I know my kid won't like, and with food, that often means throwing a lot of food away, so I have learned to make smaller portions to not be as wasteful when trying stuff I know he might not like. It's all a big game to me... it really is. And it should always be: Parents 1, Kids 0.

### ColinGorham — 2015-08-24T21:47:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visual-sensory-detection-of-bad-redox-and-or-nn-emf-a-possiblity.16762/post-175568>

**[Richelle Jones](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/richelle-jones.17133/).** I have a similar problem with my elderly parents. I learned a long time ago that dictating to them would never work. Instead, I point out information to them with minor editorialization on my part and let them reach their own conclusions. Once the data pool is rich enough in their mind my parents will make a better choice and believe it was their own idea.

### Scompy — 2015-08-24T23:03:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visual-sensory-detection-of-bad-redox-and-or-nn-emf-a-possiblity.16762/post-175576>

Stubborness usually leads to a dismissal of ideas. Twenty years ago, my mind would not have been open to Dr. K's ideas for example.

# Thread: Sunny's Journal - It starts with me.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunnys-journal-it-starts-with-me.16764/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sunny Cowgirl — 2015-08-22T07:04:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunnys-journal-it-starts-with-me.16764/post-175362>

Hi everyone!

I'm a long time lurker, (procrastinator) but am sick of mediocrity and life is too damn short for that! I want to record things here to keep myself accountable and also describe my context to any of you who are reading this.

I'm 48 years old, no children, perimenopausal (I think..was very regular up until last couple of cycles)
Height 168cm, weight 132 kg (lost 20kg over the last year). Not married but living with partner of about 8 months.

Live and run a beef cattle farm in Australia. Located a few hours drive from East Coast at 1140m altitude. Coolish climate with occasional snow in winter.

Health issues:
\- Weight
\- Some mild arthritis in my joints. Lumpy finger joints. A scan approx 5 years ago showed some degradation in my lower spine. Hip also aches a bit where I hurt it in a fall from a horse 18 years ago.

\- About 10 years ago I became very anaemic and was diagnosed with multiple uterine fibroids. I had an embolisation done in preference to a hysterectomy and that has controlled the heavy bleeding. Never taken hormonal birth control.

Previously, over 5 years ago, was on two medications to control blood pressure. No longer required as BP normal now.

Was also on Zoloft for about a year? 8 years plus since I have used. I don't think it helped.

Lucky to live in a area where mobile phone coverage is patchy but it still exists and I use it. Carry my phone with me on the farm in case of accidents but do try to have it completely switched off most of the time. Turn it on periodically to check messages and often fall into the distractions of Facebook and the like.

I've been quite good over the last few weeks with wearing blue light blockers after sunset. Switched to amber globes a few years ago. My partner is putting up with me switching off circuit boards and wifi at night but that has been only a recent change so haven't noticed any improvements.

Now go to bed between 9 and 10 and get up with the sun. Usually sleep ok but have the occasional bad night.
Mostly get AM sun on my skin and stand barefoot on the grass as soon as the frost has melted.

I know I have cortisol issues as I am waaay too reliant on coffee. Thank you Gretchen for highlighting this issue for me.

That will do for now. Need to be vigilant with my environment and brutal on my excuses and will see what unfolds.

Hi Caroline!
I'm north of Armidale.
Eating a ketogenic diet, not nearly enough seafood with it though. Baked salmon a couple of nights a week has been the extent of it lately. The fish truck usually comes to town every Friday and my partner tried to get fresh oysters yesterday but there were none!
I'm thinking that sewage spill near Sydney has meant some of northern oysters have been snaffled by Sydney Siders.

I've read the CT threads a while ago and actually enjoyed the ice baths. Needed to let my parents borrow my house for a bit and to accomodate Dad's needs (walking frame etc) we remodelled the bathroom and I no longer have a tub! Must remedy that.

### Sunny Cowgirl — 2015-08-22T09:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunnys-journal-it-starts-with-me.16764/post-175368>

Drink rainwater, it's stored in concrete tanks and a galvanised iron tank. Collected from galvanised iron roof. I've been wondering whether I should be filtering that.
My main vice is coffee and my reading on the blog and Facebook is telling me what I feared

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-22T17:06:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunnys-journal-it-starts-with-me.16764/post-175381>

The DJD of spine and fingers and fibroids are a big tells that your surfaces have been altered by a blue light environment.......fibroids is a huge omega 6 and lack of DHA.

Let me give you an example from neurosurgery of a peripheral nerve injury: I look at a blue light injury to your eye and skin a lot like a peripheral nerve injury

-   How should patients and physicians change their perception on underlying internal relationships when the surface changes?
    Consider the question: water, light, and magnetism... Atmospheric barometric pressure changes greatly effect / influence DJD pain in arthritis, neuropathic pain in injury or T2D. Hydration is key for minimizing pain activation. This is why T2D get it bad because they are chronically dehydrated. A PNS (surface) damaged at CNS (deeper tissue) say from a brachial plexus avulsions, with arm paralysis or skin alterations to cause alterations in your joints or uterus. The anemia in your case was the key.......when RBC's drop it is a sign of anemia of chronic disease and this is always associated with low RBC DHA. These people often report they have always felt post injury there had to another way to minimize pain through some other modality. Something is true for those with DDD AND DJD in my clinic. We have horrible options for these patients today in NS. There is another interesting environmental fact that is a teaching point: The fact that outer body air pressure (atmospheric H2O) increase or decrease universally affects arthritic pain and many neuropathic pain suffers, suggests there must be some treatable cause & effect recipe. So what might that be from a quantum perspective?
    -   Water flows in cells are all controlled in the CNS by AQA 4 channels. They all contain 4 nitrogens suggesting that they would respond to pressure and light much like a leaf or RBC would. RBC's are also loaded with porphyrins which also have 4 nitrogens which you are missing. This tells me your likely have a bad skin from fake light and lack of UV and IR light in AM. This chronic change caused the joints and uterus to eventually be depleted of omega 3's because of your specific environmental exposures at the eye skin and gut. These changes can alter their surfaces to environmental changes they find themselves in. Free diving is a way to bring a hydraulic pressure to alter bariatric pressures sensation in the skin and eye that is missing and DHA in the remaining cell membranes of the bad wing will limit your pain because its breakdown products are all anti-inflammatory in contrast to omega 6's. This surface change will eventually be felt in the deeper tissues like the uterus when they fill back up with DHA. This may take a long time because it takes decades to show up. If the water is colder to the surface I would expect the reversal to be even better and maybe faster. DHA works better and longer in cold. One could design a harness device that simulates these environmental changes at the damaged surface and see if the bad effect reverses of goes away. Surface bring sensory stimulation to the thalamic relay station in the brain. You need constant sensory thalamic inflows to populate the somatosensory cortex in the CNS where a lot of DHA should be.......if DHA or the sensory stimuli are absent other fibers from other modalities (pain) from the thalamus will own the real estate instead. Since movement is the efferent pathway it will stimulate and affect the modality that took over the real estate. In the eye saccades are the motor component of the visual apparatus. Sex or moving the cervix in this way could actually be a therapeutic maneuver........I spoke about this in the sex webinar a long time ago........just left out all the DHA omega six details.

I have a question about total daytime blue light from sunlight. I spend a reasonable amount of time outside as a farmer. Is there any need to limit outside natural light exposure? I woke up early as usual, after a great sleep, feeling fine but soon after my big salmon breakfast I folded! Could not stay awake! Actually went back to bed about 9.30 and slept until about 1.30 pm. ### Sunny Cowgirl — 2015-11-05T07:42:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunnys-journal-it-starts-with-me.16764/post-181190>

Several weeks since I've posted here but going well. Spending the first 3-4 hours of the day outside in the sun, sans clothing. Mostly listening to replays of webinars and Q&A's.
decision to trade pay tv subscription for gold membership was worth it. Think I'm getting a fairly good handle on most concepts but need to go back and read blogs that I have missed or only skimmed in the past.

Standard breakfast for 4 or 5 days of the week is a jar of raw oysters. Buy them once a week when the fish truck visits town. I'd like to have oysters every day but I'm a bit wary of how well they will keep. Still seem ok at 5 days after purchase. Then balance of the week breakfast is salmon, baked in the oven from frozen with eggs.

I think my eye sight is improving. I got my first pair of reading glasses back in January and although I didn't need them all the time, I'm noticing now I need them less. It could just be that I've stopped the stupid habit of reading on my phone during the night...duh! Phone is outside of the bedroom now and wifi off at night.

### Sunny Cowgirl — 2015-11-05T07:43:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunnys-journal-it-starts-with-me.16764/post-181191>

Finding this Kate Miller Heidke song inspiring.

**"Bliss"
Sometimes things are closed
Like windows on an empty street
Not this time though
First drink, the glow, the heat

A piece of morning sun
Swallowed with a grin
Shooting sparks within
Shower everyone

Bliss

Up to the skylight
These walls are warm again
Out of my right mind
This feeling
Old absent friend

A piece of morning sun
Swallowed with a grin
Shooting sparks within
Shower everyone

Bliss

My violet sea receding like an empty threat
Could it be I’m free? I’ve paid the debt?

Bliss
**

# Thread: Luis Arauz - Be The Change You Want To See In the Universe
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luis-arauz-be-the-change-you-want-to-see-in-the-universe.16766/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### optimizedprimate — 2015-08-23T17:58:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luis-arauz-be-the-change-you-want-to-see-in-the-universe.16766/post-175433>

This is my journal, there are many like it, but this one is mine. My journal is my best friend. It is my life. I must master it as I must master my life. My journal, without me, is useless. Without my journal, I am useless. I must write my journal true. I must be selfish and focus on me first, because if I am not optimum, I'm of no use to anyone else.

I don't have any diagnosed health issues, although I aim to keep it that way, goals are way beyond that.

Basic Stats:
**Name:** Luis Arauz (I pronounce it Louis Arouse, in case you are wondering)
**Birthday: Feb 3, 1976
Sex:** Yes Please!
**Location:** Chicago, IL USA
I'm of Cuban & Chinese descent
Height: 5'9
Weight: 174 (been as high as 240lbs)
Exercise: Minimal I used to do martial arts (15 years ago) crossfit (2 year ago)
My interests are in Science, health & performance, Technology, survival skills, cooking, martial arts, business, marketing, Comedy to name a few.

My Current list of famous historical personalities that influence me: My Dad, Bruce Lee, Jay Abraham, Jack Kruse (obviously), Louie CK, George Carlin, Richard Feynman, Neil Degrasse Tyson, Dan Pena,

Goals for my biological assets.

1.  Optimal performance in brain function (higher IQ, processing power and memory)

2.  Longevity(at least 160 years) so far so good.
3.  Full function and optimal performance of all my other biological assets.

My current summer routine:

1.  Wake up at 4am
2.  Breakfast (within 30-1hour of waking) 50gm mixture of seafood based protein and eggs (lamb/bacon on occasion) with seaweed or some kind of salad and fat (avocado, bacon fat or coconut oil)

3.  approx 4 liters of water a day
4.  Yellow blue blockers when im in front of a computer. orange glasses when the sun goes down.

5.  on days that I am not working morning shift I head to the park to ground and get at least 40min of grounding and AM light.

6.  On days I am working morning shift I get to work by 5am at around 6am I go out to the parking lot and find a small patch of grass to ground on and catch some AM light for 15 minutes.

7.  Generally I can stay satiated till 2pm (with proper AM routine, but days that I can't I get hungrier faster.)
8.  I will eat dinner around 7-8pm (but im working on getting that done earlier)

9.  In bed by 10pm (when Im not working the night shift)
10.  I sleep in a DARK ROOM, Ear plugs, blindfold, minimal clothing, AC on.

My challenges

1.  Home: I currently live in a basement "apartment" with my wife and daughter(5)
2.  Work: I currently work at a laundry service with a business model similar to UBER. I work different shifts depending on what is needed.

3.  Income: my income is not high enough to do all that I need to do, so for now I am doing the best that I can with what I got, while I work to improve my situation.
4.  I dont have many easily accessible natural areas around me other than parks.
5.  I have the CT FatBurner Vest, I want to get a feed tub to do Water CT though.

Lab Results.
I will be posting my lab results, which are relatively useless because I took them last year while basically following the BP diet and my 23 & me data for some context.

If there is anything else anyone would like to know please feel free to ask.

Thank you for reading.

Desk 3.5 (but the circuit breaker is in this room)
Front yard 3
Back yard 3-5 Power lines in back yard and smart meter as well.
Bathroom 4.5

Living room couch 3.5

Bedroom 4

I can pick up 15 wifi SSIDs from inside.

Thanks Caroline,

Yep my post code is horrible. I'm currently starting my own business as an executive/entrepreneur performance coach to help them improve performance of their biological assets, once I establish the business, it can be run from anywhere, and then I'm out.

my goal is to be able to move into one of the houses from earthship.com ideally by a large body of water filled with fish.

Family is getting on board, the hare moves faster than the tortoise, but as long as the hare sees the tortoise sticking its neck out, the hare will keep cheering the tortoise along.

My daughter loves salmon heads (especially fish cheeks), sardines, avocados, seaweed, sushi, its difficult to keep clothes on her, she loves to swim and we got her red lensed blue blockers. so far so good.

### optimizedprimate — 2015-08-24T14:11:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luis-arauz-be-the-change-you-want-to-see-in-the-universe.16766/post-175527>

Made a 2 sardine smoothie this morning, descaled it, but i left the sardines whole. with the gills. tried asian flavors this time, didnt work out as well as the DHA guacamole i made the other day. Too many variables. I need to go back to the dha guac and control the variables.

But what ever I downed it any way, my head is buzzing I always hope its just DHA and not a brain parasite ;-)

Incase anyone wants to try the DHA guacamole here is what was in it.
It tastes just like guacamole. Here is what was in mine.
1\. Whole sardine (chopped off the gills, tail fin and squeezed out the intestines)
2\. Red onion
3\. Avocado
4\. Cilantro
5\. Salt, pepper, tumeric
6\. 1 clove of garlic
7\. Lime juice
8\. MCT oil

No fish taste at all. Next time I will titrate up to two sardines and see what it tastes like.

### optimizedprimate — 2015-08-31T16:17:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luis-arauz-be-the-change-you-want-to-see-in-the-universe.16766/post-176109>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=175660)
>
> Great job with the daughter Luis. Sardines are not my favorite either but gagging them down.

Nicid, here are some recipes to try if you are having problems with sardines
add them to fish broth with spiralized cabbage noodles, a little bit of sesame oil, and fish sauce (fermented sardines, salt and water), coconut aminos, tumeric,salt pepper, garlic, a poached egg.... and you have a pretty banging asian "noodle" dish

Po' Boy
Sardines Hard-boil two eggs, caramelized onion, sardines, tomato sauce and some capers and hot sauce mayo cilantro sprigs, parsley, tomato slices, cucumbers, lettuce—anything you like. eat as a salad or lettuce wrap

Stuffed Potato
Combine canned sardines in cauliflower cheese sauce with a steamed potato, some red onion relish: diced red onion, lemon juice, olive oil, dill and salt and pepper to taste. I use a sweet potato though

let me know if you want more

### optimizedprimate — 2015-09-01T15:41:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luis-arauz-be-the-change-you-want-to-see-in-the-universe.16766/post-176160>

Challenges:
Yesterday saw my daughter eating an ice cream cone that her grandfather bought her after we have told them that she is not allowed to have them because we dont know/like what is in them.

I talk about people being able to make a lot of mistakes/expenses for a long time in business when they have a large enough cash reserve to pay for those mistakes. But if their "burn rate is too high" they will use up that cash reserve sooner than expected.
That is what investors call your "Cash to Zero" date. In our biological assets the same holds true. we have a mistakes/expenses (biological processes, inflammation) and we have a cash reserve (stem cells, telomeres) and the length of our telomeres compared to our chronological age is our burn rate.

Im always thinking about my daughters burn rate, and I just hope that the environment my wife and I were in before she was born, (and still in) gave her enough biological cash reserve to hold on till we get out.
man Ive never really posted this anywhere.

### optimizedprimate — 2015-09-06T23:54:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luis-arauz-be-the-change-you-want-to-see-in-the-universe.16766/post-176634>

Arrgh soo much of the learning in this group is about self discovery and re-evaluating who and what you have been up until this point and are you going to continue down that path or course correct to the direction you are supposed to be going. But its not just a physical change this is a mental change (still physical quantum etc but you get what I mean).

I love discussions and even heated debates. I openly welcome differing opinions especially when they are backed by solid information. I actually enjoy when someone can move me from my position,

But its no fun when someone starts something with you but then deletes those statements and you have no opportunity to complete the conversation, I dont care if someone doesn't like me, but at least make sure your reasoning is correct.

That being said I'm still trying to learn this new skill of just walking away from a good retort.
I rather be right than liked, but I don't have to prove people wrong.

I just had a good one too, but the person who confronted me then deleted their message thread. That's no fun! why would you delete a thread gone wrong? there is so much you can learn from an exploded land mine. and there I was left with a great comeback with no were to put it.... i felt like I wanted to rant about it, and post my comment anyway....but that would have served no purpose....then then i noticed something...

...... that it felt different than all the other times, I am....more... OK with just letting it go than I would have in the past. I have so much further to go......

Today I took my family to the forest preserve at 7am to collect well water, electrons and photons. we had a great time running barefoot and playing frisbee... I even went sans shirt felt weird to do that when not at the beach.

Ive been looking into TAN THROUGH clothing items. I think this would be a great items for those times that going native might be the best idea socially.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-09-07T05:17:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luis-arauz-be-the-change-you-want-to-see-in-the-universe.16766/post-176648>

Pretty amazing weekend in NOLA during decadence fest. What i realized tonight is fate never finds us. We create it. When there is no enemy within, the enemies outside cannot hurt you. Modern life and technology creates mass discord, making folks believe there is great prestige for being the first to create a precept, when there is wisdom built in nature's concept. The future of humanity lies in unity and respect, not division and stereotypes created by distraction. You have to find what sparks a flame in you so that you in your own way can illuminate the world. The sunrise is my spark. I am awaiting her signal are you? Night

### optimizedprimate — 2015-10-19T03:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luis-arauz-be-the-change-you-want-to-see-in-the-universe.16766/post-179834>

Today was the first day of my CT training.
The basic plan is 2 full weeks of CT everyday, I want to see if I can achieve increase of strength, shred fat, and be able to radiate heat (Ive always had cold hands and feet).

My daughter and wife at least gave it a try but as soon as they touched the water they were out, They need more Fish and protection of their DHA (plus Ive been through the face dunking, shiver walks, AC on 4 in the car, and Icepacks on my back and chest. )

Breakfast
Asian inspired Fish Soup
Bone broth
6 sardine filets
Fish sauce (fermented anchovies and salt)
2 pastured eggs
tumeric, pepper
Wakkame

Drink
16 oz Water well water

CT Tub 100gallon feed tub
Water 50 degrees
total time in tub 25 minutes

for the last 5 minutes or so I kept my feet and hands out of the water, the pins and needles got to be too much and I got worried about blood flow restriction to the outer extremities.
But the rest of my core felt fine. the shivering was minimal, but it was interesting feeling the waves of shivering, calmness back to shivering again.
I felt tingling all over my body, I didn't submerge my head though I will try that tomorrow.
I also was wearing swimming trunks I will try to ditch those while inside the tank.

Once I was done I got out to air & UV dried with grounding for 15 minutes.
My mobility was definitely restricted as my hands and feet barely worked, but it felt amazing to be with people walking by covered head to toe, while I was in shorts soaking up whatever UV&IR light I could.

When I got inside the shivering really started to happen. My entire body was convulsing like I was strapped to an e-stim device.

I made myself a hot chocolate and just relaxed.
Water,
butter
MCT oil
sea salt
cayenne peper
100% dark chocolate
xylitol
1 egg
BLEND

### caroline — 2015-10-20T06:44:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luis-arauz-be-the-change-you-want-to-see-in-the-universe.16766/post-179941>

### optimizedprimate — 2015-10-27T00:19:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luis-arauz-be-the-change-you-want-to-see-in-the-universe.16766/post-180521>

yeah thanks, other people have mentioned that Im thinner, great side effect. My hands and feet are now able to stay inside the water for the full CT session Im up to 1 hour in 53 degree water. and Ive started putting in 7 lbs of ice. to drop the temperature further. It is really interesting feeling the physics of water when you throw a block of ice into the tub.

The Ice floats, and starts to crack and bubble as it melts. I can feel the cold water sink to the bottom of the tub as the "warmer" water rises.
also Ive been playing with DEEP breathing exercises and amusing myself with buoyancy. as I breath in deep I float and then sink hard when I breath out. Im getting more oxygen for the mitochondria but the up and down movements are also circulating the cold water around me to keep me colder.

I love when the sun comes up and hits my skin while in the pool but air drying in the sun on the front lawn is quite an experience especially with people walking around in winter coats and Im on the grass soaking wet in swim trunks.

Ive also started doing 35lb kettlebell swings before going into the tub.

### Douglas Freeman — 2015-08-23T19:52:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dougies-journal.16768/post-175442>

I have been struggling with acne since puberty, I am now 37 and still struggle.

I met Josh Ferber almost 10 years ago in Sedona, AZ. Six months ago he started treating me with functional medicine and referred me to the Epi-Paleo 6 for a protocol to follow and have been doing that in baby steps to the best of my ability.

I have been doing the same with controlling my environment as far as blue light goes in as far as my occupation will allow.

I have been implementing CT as well and had to back pedal having jumped into it to quickly. I am back to head dunks and cold packs on me face back and torso as it was too much for my system to handle.

Prior to this life style the only thing that has cleared my skin has been "vision questing' (fasting with large amounts of desert sun basking) and intense intestinal cleaning coupled with raw vegan diet.

I am seeing progress followed by some set backs here and there and hope to continue to learn grow and get closer to my optimal with help from this community and Dr. Kruse.

Peace

Doug

# Thread: The biophysics of the microbiome:  state of affairs 2015
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biophysics-of-the-microbiome-state-of-affairs-2015.16769/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 58
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-23T21:53:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biophysics-of-the-microbiome-state-of-affairs-2015.16769/post-175457>

Fecal transplants are really a story about replenishing light to your gut. Today I have a lot of schedule posts on this science. Antibiotics kill the microbiome lowering their number and diversity of species. Using Roeland van Wijk, Fritz Popp, and Emilio Del Giudice's work this means there is less light released in our colon. Bacteria release 5000 times more light than our cells do. Did you know that? If not stay tuned to my website and forum to learn a lot more about this surface interaction as time evolves.

-   This is an exciting time for gastrointestinal microbiology. The recognition of Helicobacter pylori and its role in gastrointestinal disease recently earned a Nobel prize; the discovery of bacterial secretion systems and translocated effectors has unlocked numerous mechanisms of pathogenesis; the role of the commensal flora in many disease states, including inflammatory bowel disease, irritable bowel syndrome, and even obesity, is now accepted and will undoubtedly lead to new therapeutic strategies; the finding that eukaryotic cells perceive and respond to bacteria through the expression of Toll-like receptors and Nods is a major step toward understanding interactions between the host and gut microbiota or microbial pathogens; the discovery of quorum sensing as a means of communication between bacterial populations and even with host cells has provided insight into pathogenic mechanisms; and the recently NIH-initiated Human Microbiome Project will eventually allow better understanding of the role of this complex intestinal community in human health and disease. The key missing clinical questions are how quorum sensing work on a fundamental level. I believe biology won’t find that answer because they do not focus in on light frequencies. Bacteria make up the microbiome exclusively and their cell membranes are radically different than our own. They are able to release large amounts of light frequencies in the form of biophotons. The only part of biology that studies light is the biophysics of plants and marine bioluminescence with respect to photosynthesis. Because of this, I expect that the gastroenterology literature will not look or discover truly how the gut microbiome is capable of sculpting our epigenome with light through its interaction with our eukaryotic cell membranes of enterocytes.[http://www.thedailybeast.com/](http://www.thedailybeast.com/)…/are-microbes-stealing-your-mind
-   For those of you who are not lazy, I would suggest you read the bibliography of Van Wijk book that has a lot of the data on light signaling. In December 2013 David Sinclair published the land mark paper linking low oxygen environments to low NAD+ levels at cytochrome 1 in humans. This paper has been talked about in ten of the Ubiquitination blogs already. All chronic disease are linked to low NAD+ levels. When NAD+ levels drop ETC slows. When ETC slows the amount and frequency of bio-photons are also altered hence making the bio chemical changes linked to optical signals that affect chromophores and fluorophores in all cells. Artificial or alter light frequencies are fully capable of directly changing ETC. Cytochrome C reacts to blue and red light for an example. Blue light causes your mitochondrial ECT to slow because the respiratory proteins enlarge to slow electron tunneling….…..when it slows you up regulate carbohydrate metabolism by way of AMPK pathways. This has massive impacts on the microbiome. It alters its ability to make beta hydroxybutyrate, oligosaccharides, Vitamin K2, and something called FIAF. FIAF is what cause human obesity. All this can occur from the change in frequency of light release from the microbiome and from your own cells. This work has been published for quite sometime in biophysics and is chronicled in the book I mentioned above.......People who are lazy, medicine, and ancestral health is unaware of this published data. You no longer can afford to be.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-23T21:53:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biophysics-of-the-microbiome-state-of-affairs-2015.16769/post-175458>

Bacteria that use quorum sensing constitutively produce and secrete certain signaling molecules. All these molecules are either chromophores of fluorophores. Chromophores absorb certain frequency of light and fluorophores release certain frequencies of light. In biology today these same chemicals are also called autoinducers or pheromones. Many people in biology know these names better than the biophysical names. These bacteria have a receptor that can specifically detect the signaling molecule or light frequency(inducer). All proteins have an absorption spectrum for light. This is also something many in biology do not know but has been proven in bio-physics. When the inducer binds the receptor, it activates transcription of certain genes, including those for inducer synthesis. This is an important concept because it is foundational to the processes that govern epigenetics. "Epigenetics" as term is now casually used for all transcriptional regulators, except, curiously, TFs. There is a low likelihood of a bacterium detecting its own secreted inducer because of how protein emission and absorption is quantized. Thus, in order for gene transcription to be activated, the cell must encounter signaling molecules secreted by other cells in its local environment. This becomes easy in the gut. When only a few other bacteria of the same kind are in the vicinity, diffusion reduces the concentration of the inducer in the surrounding medium to almost zero, so the bacteria produce little inducer. Diffusion is also speed up when proton tunneling occurs. This has been experimentally shown to occur infrared ranges of light at 1538.5 nm. Too bad Dave could not have stuck around for this all this good stuff. Emilio Del Giudice work showed this effect of IR light and proton tunneling in the last decade. As the populations of bacteria within the microbiome grows and becomes more diverse, the concentration of the inducer/fluorophore passes a signaling threshold, causing more inducer to be synthesized. This is very similar to how the photoelectric effect works. If the light signal is not strong enough it can not be used to excite electrons to higher orbital levels. This is why this effect has been seen in the recent microbiome papers. In concert these actions form a positive feedback loop, and the receptor becomes fully activated. When the microbiome diversity is lost of numbers drop we lose negative feedback control and FIAF cause more light release and humans become obese. Proper activation of the receptor in the microbiome induces the up-regulation of other specific genes, causing all of the cells to begin transcription at approximately the same time. Only light is capable of this type of coherence because of the speeds it operates at. This coordinated behavior of bacterial cells can be useful in a variety of situations to make other signaling chemicals that the body can use. Vitamin K2 is one. Vitamin K2 is a quinone and all quinone absorb all frequencies of UV light. This matches the frequency of light eukaryotes naturally release from their cells. For instance, the bioluminescent luciferase produced by Vibrio fischeri would not be visible if it were produced by a single cell. By using quorum sensing to limit the production of luciferase to situations when cell populations are large, V. fischeri cells are able to avoid wasting energy on the production of useless product. This is how quorum sensing works. This explanation is from reading thoussands of papers in many disciplines of science. Many of these articles begin in Roeland van Wijk's work.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-23T22:01:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biophysics-of-the-microbiome-state-of-affairs-2015.16769/post-175459>

Obesity is an inflammatory brain condition. Inflammation is a defect in proton tunneling. When they are not coherent protons accumulate and they pH drops. When pH drops the EZ of water drops and energy levels in the gut drop dramatically. Where does this inflammation originate from? The gut flora actually is what causes humans to become fat. It has to due with the reduction in shear numbers and the species of bacteria in our gut. There is a particular flora that produces adiposity and obesity in humans. The human microbiome has already shown these relationships. Clinicians still dont know what these things mean. One area that they are seeing the results is in how fecal transplants have tremendously changed the way we treat c difficile infections of the gut. My bet is fecal transplants will usher in the error of quantum biology in medicine. These bacteria make something called FIAF ([Fasting induced adipose factor](http://gordonlab.wustl.edu/PublicationPDFs/344_BackhedPNAS04.pdf)) that control this process. This factor blocks lipoprotein lipase (LPL) in fat cells. LPL allows us to convert dietary Free Fatty Acids carried in lipoproteins into neutral fats that are stored in adipocytes.

**The take away:** Our gut bacteria makes us fat. The bigger shocker? The environment our microbiome faces.......is what is behind it. The key to the non linear optical signaling is what? What the terminal electron acceptors are in the microbiome determine the frequencies of light released by bacteria to determine quantize signaling between fluorophores and chromophore proteins in living things.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-23T22:03:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biophysics-of-the-microbiome-state-of-affairs-2015.16769/post-175460>

The FIAF is made by our liver, muscles, and our small bowel wall when food sources are in short supply. This is the signal to stop storing fat in our fat cells when food is scarce. What is not well appreciated by many is that the FIAF in our intestinal wall is controlled 100% by our gut flora. When we have a simplified gut flora it favors fat cell creation. When our gut flora is [complex and healthy](http://www.sciencefriday.com/segment/06/15/2012/mapping-the-microbial-make-up-of-healthy-humans.html), it has 100 trillion cells with 250 species. We tend not to make fat in this instance either! Moreover, when the bacteria are active metabolically due to the presence of simple sugars, production of FIAF ceases, and fat creation is signaled. **This implies that our gut flora is directly tied to fat creation in humans**. The gut flora’s action is directly signaled to the brain via the afferent nerve fibers in the vagus nerve. In those newly created fat cells, a protein called **leptin** is also produced, and acts as a score keeper for the brain of how much fat is stored in the body. This messenger is sent to the brain around midnight when we are sleeping allowing the brain to assess total energy balance in the body. At night, when it is dark, melatonin should be high. If it is not this process of signaling will not work!!!!

Genes are vehicles for duplication of environmental signals powering mass for consequence.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-23T22:20:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biophysics-of-the-microbiome-state-of-affairs-2015.16769/post-175468>

Our colon is the **anaerobic reactor** that reproduces our gut flora. When someone has IBD, GERD, or SIBO they have too much oxygen in their gut. This allows the microbiome to use oxygen as a terminal electron acceptor in our gut. We dont want that. Why? because it alters the frequency of light the microbiome releases and alters non linear optical signaling. The appendix is the “sperm bank” that keeps a nice sample of our current gut flora in case we get a nasty bought of gastritis that cleans our microfilms and our flora out into the toilet. We eat things under normal conditions with NO oxygen present in our gut. This is why the LES between the stomach and duodenum is key. That sphincter is under the hierarchal control of the SCN!!!! When you have a circadian mismatch to blue light in your eye clock it causes the LES to allow oxygen into your anaerobic reactor.

This type of environment stimulates the gut flora to extract a lot of energy from food because of scarcity. The chemical reaction basically is to extract as much oxygen from the food and leave behind carbon and hydrogen. This is essential what a ‘turd’ is in its basic form. In an anaerobic environment bacteria have an amazing capability to extract energy from food for its host, namely us. Bacteria have little ability to save any of this energy for them. The human body usurps their ability to be great energy extractors and uses it for itself. Your gut flora is what makes you fat and your gut flora is under control of hormones!

Since the human colon is anaerobic when we feed these bacteria a diet high in simple carbohydrates they extract the oxygen and and leave us with bowel gas and **lower levels of FIAF**. This actually allows us then to store fat that we can use later on for our own needs. This fat is loaded with long chain fatty acids, but to gain the energy in its bonds we have to have oxygen to access it. The bacteria don’t have that luxury, so they are inept at using the fat they created! We, however, can use it at our inner mitochondrial membrane to create energy via ATP using electron chain transport. In fact, long chain fatty acids have huge energy potential for the host. One molecule of glucose has only six carbons. Glucose can make 28-36 ATP. One molecule of an 18 carbon stearic acid, a FFA, has three times as many carbons as glucose but makes **five times** the amount of ATP (147 ATP) while only having two times the caloric density of glucose. This shows you precisely why a calorie is not a calorie and why CICO makes little sense in a bio-physics model. In the distal colon we make large amounts of long chain fatty acids under normal healthy conditions.

So when the human host turns around and eats a **fat laden ketogenic diet** it creates a huge hormetic issue for our gut flora. Why? seasonal ketosis raises NAD+ levels in cytochrome 1. They can not access the energy in these fats because there is no molecular oxygen present and the human host is not hungry at all because the presence of the fat in the gut is signaled to the brain via the vagus nerve. This is the perfect storm for the human and really bad news for the gut bacteria. This is why one has to question the validity of any safe starch theory in my view. The molecular biology of the gut just does not support it.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-23T22:20:33+0300

The vagus nerve connects the digital and analogue systems together in the brain. It also is the protector of the blood brain barrier, the gut barrier and the pneumo-environmental barrier in humans. The vagus nerve connects the entire gut down to the transverse meso-colon of the hind gut to the fourth ventricle of the brainstem. This ventricle is filled with CSF and the vagus nerve is covered with myelin in connection with this CSF. This is how the “central digital” circadian system of the SCN and the “analogue circadian system” of the gut are linked to light and the timing of food growth in the environment, so that the signals can be yoked via the area postrema and the median eminence in the brain. These are two areas in the brain do not have a blood brain barrier so the electron density in the CSF is accurately tied to the local environment. The area postrema and median eminence are called the circumventricular organs. Both of them are bathed in CSF that surrounds the brain. The digital circadian signals project visible light photons from the retina to the median eminence to affect hormone release in the pituitary. The analogue system from the gut’s photoelectric code for food projects directly to the area postrema. This unifies how the analogue and digital circadian signals in the brain work in unison to tell an organism what their energy balance is at anytime of the year by counting electron density in the CSF. With in CSF layers there also is a stratification of water by density as well. This is another example of fractal design of how water stratification occurs in the oceans to create oxygen. This is also how the central clock and peripheral clocks yoke the photoelectric effect from the different sources in our environment. It should be intuitive now why environmental mismatches in the brain are perceived as a mixed message by our leptin receptor in the hypothalamus now. Your leptin receptor’s job is to count the electron density in CSF that surrounds it to send this information to different areas of the body. When we have any mismatch in the photoelectric signaling in the hypothalamus, the result in a cell with the leptin receptor is molecular crowding. Molecular crowding is a synonym for inflammation. Inflammation is a synonym for leptin resistance. Fat provides more electrons than protein, but protein is more thermogenic and it is also energy efficient because it lowers the cost of protein ubiquination when autophagy is broken or inefficient.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-23T22:41:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biophysics-of-the-microbiome-state-of-affairs-2015.16769/post-175475>

So I have harped on surfaces........let us talk a bit more about the gut surface.........it is loaded with water which is at an interface with the microbiome and our enterocytes.

More than 50 years ago, Nobel Laureate and father of modern biochemistry, Albert Szent-Györgyi, had already suggested that water at interfaces is the key to life and proposed that water at interfaces, such as membranes, is in the excited state, requiring considerably less energy to split than water in the ground state. A sign of the excited water is that a voltage should appear at the boundary between interfacial water and bulk water, which was indeed observed not long after Szent-Györgyi made his prediction. Most, if not all, water in living organisms is interfacial water (EZ), as it is almost never more than a small fraction of a micron away from surfaces, such as membranes or macromolecules. Water at the interface is a coherent domain generated (Chaplin, Preparrta, Del Giudice work on CD in water) by the interaction of the electromagnetic field and water and stabilized by the interface, as Del Giudice and colleagues suggested.

Unexpected support for this theory came from a field study on aerosols generated at five water falls in the Austrian Alps carried out by Pierre Madl at Salzburg University in Austria with Del Giudice and other collaborators around the world. This work is no longer theory since it has been reproduced in many labs by 2015. It is just not well known in biologic or medical circles. I do know about it and so do you. The implications of it are tremendous for the human microbiome, skin, and eye. The aerosols showed a bimodal size distribution with small clusters a few nanometers in diameter consisting of a few hundred water molecules and larger aggregates about 100–200 nm in diameter with millions of water molecules. This should begin to explain to you why dehydration within the lumen of the gut or the tissues of the gut drastically alters the biochemistry of cells in deeper tissues.

In water falls in nature it has been found the small clusters of coherent domains disappear very rapidly with distance from the falls, the larger aggregates are able to propagate for hundreds of meters within water. The aggregates have now been detected, both large and small. They key part of this science is they have both been found to be electrically charged with negative charge predominating for 85% of aggregates. This experiment confirms the work of Pollack in total and points out why Ling's idea of polarized water is not accurate. Polarized water has dipoles that are neutrally charged. EZ water carries a net negative charge and is in agreement with this biophysical data of Chaplin and Del Giudice. The existence of the surface electrical charge and unusual size distribution of the aggregates are both predictions of the quantum electrodynamics theory of water; although, the precise details have yet to be worked out. These details will have massive implications in my opinion in microtubules of neurons and fundamentally explain how consciousness works in animals. This water bridge also forms the base of the brain gut axis in humans. It should then be clear how changes in the microbiome can lead to madness or build a properly functioning human brain. If there is scattering of light in the gut it will alter the non linear optics of this systems and manifest in altered neurology functioning, My prediction is all this will be proven in the next 5-ten years because of the fundamental work already published in the literature. My job is to coherent make sense of all the data.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-24T00:41:34+0300

All DNA/RNA absorbs light but scatters most of the energy in it. This means it dissipates it. The reason why? It signals 100% by light emission. This is why surface skin loses its nucleus.......and DNA/RNA in our skin surface become our built in version of natural sunscreen. Ketinocytes lose there nucleic acids to sunlight on purpose. The basal levels of the skin do something different. They interact with the arteriole system to transfer sunlight to porphyrins. This is why the basal skin increases its blood flow when sunlight shines upon it.
Life uses proteins coded or uncoded for by nucleic acids actually are what **_direct the arrow of time_** in biology. Those proteins all absorb or reflect light. In quantum physics equations (QED), time is invariant, therefore time is an illusion of nature. Time is created because of things created to be metastable of dissipative in nature. Such systems, rely totally upon nonlinear protein/quanta interactions, that can form their own mass, spatial, and temporal structures. This is akin to creating a painting by collecting colors and frequencies. The canvas is cells. Cells are collections of proteins and this is why DNA/RNA only code for proteins. They absorb or emit specific light frequencies to build the canvas of life. These aspects of proteins are synonyms for the "dissipative structure" of a cell.

These cells can exist as long as the system is continuously held far from equilibrium due to a continual flow of energy or matter through the system. Nightly autophagy (sleep) and seasonal mitophagy, keeps us in this state. This means the organization of the cell is far more important than the things that feed our metabolism. Anything having these qualities has to be kept far from equilibrium to maintain a healthy life. Only at death is equilibrium obtained. I view the arrow of time and the inability of reforming the shell of a cracked egg as playing a constructive role in nature. The arrow of time is essential to the existence of biological systems, because they contain highly organized irreversible structures. In this way life is symmetric. In other words, **_the cells organization is the critical part of life, not the fuel we put into it_**. The environment the cell is in determines the organization of the cell.

In this way, Nature, or the world can be thought of as a piece of art, produced according to a very specific peculiar style. That style is based upon light, specifically the non linear optics of light in changing environments at surfaces. What I find particularly curious is the outstanding role of symmetry in the process. Water breaks nature's symmetry in light and this is why non linear optics forms the alphabet of the brain gut functioning. The principle of symmetry as it is used in mathematics and physics can best be described as **"change without change."

This sounds nuts until you understand it. So let me give you an example.**
What makes a circle such a symmetrical object? It's that you can rotate it around its center and it will remain a circle. In the case of an equilateral triangle, small rotations will change it, but if you rotate it by 120 degrees it comes back to itself. It thus has some symmetry, though less than a circle. This concept of symmetry as "change without change" can easily be generalized to laws of physics, or to the equations that express them. This is why math can describe nature well but it cannot explain it fully. It describes the geometry of cells within space but it cannot tell us the story of the interaction of light on the cell.
And this can help us understand the world better because we can describe how life might happen.
Consider that a key assumption of the theory of relativity, for instance, is that if you view the world from a moving train, things may look different but the same laws of physics apply. In quantum electrodynamics, you can make a number of transformations on electric and magnetic fields, but the equations remain valid. Now you can look for the perfect equations whose consequences remain unchanged under a whole lot of transformations. These are, so to speak, the circles among the equations of electromagnetism. And it turns out that those are the ones that govern how we construct (describe) reality.

The miracle of our brain's physiology isn’t that you can see the world as it is. It’s that you can see the world as it isn’t. My job is to explain what you can't see but physics has observed to exist in their experiments. Absence of evidence in a biology book is not absence of effect in reality and yet we still have people calling for RCT's when we are dealing with fundamental universal laws. Life is not subject to RCT's it is subject to universal physical laws. DO NOT FORGET IT, because biologists and physicians have.
Nature builds enormous complexity via 6 leptons & quarks by evolving into a chemical evolution via the construction of atoms.

It is the arrangement of these atoms that dictate the life we live, biology we see and understand and write textbooks about. But how they undergo elastic deformation to give us longevity is what OSF 3-5 is all about at a fundamental level. You must read between the lines to see the true message. This thread has done just that in extreme detail using the brain gut axis.

### PaulG — 2015-08-24T03:47:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biophysics-of-the-microbiome-state-of-affairs-2015.16769/post-175504>

and Mae Wan Ho [http://www.mdpi.com/1099-4300/16/9/4874/pdf](http://www.mdpi.com/1099-4300/16/9/4874/pdf)

> In 2002, researchers at the FOM (Foundation for Fundamental Research on Matter) Institute of Atomic and Molecular Physics in the Netherlands used ultrafast femto-second pulses of infrared light to excite and probe the O-H covalent bond in liquid water at room temperature \[36\]. Again, the results support a quantum mechanical model in which the excited proton could be found simultaneously at a distance of the equilibrium O-H bond from both its neighbour oxygen on either side and at a much reduced energy for the excited (v = 2) state than if the hydrogen bond did not exist.
>
> The energy of excitation to the v = 2 delocalized state, estimated at 0.806 eV (6500 cm−1 or 1538.5 nm) is less than 20% of the O-H bond energy of 4.8 eV (38,750 cm−1, 258.1 nm), estimated from the O-H stretch vibration frequency. The energy required for excitation to the delocalized state is very much smaller than the 12.06 eV for excitation to coherent domains \[6\], and the O-H bond energy at 4.8 eV is also much smaller than the value of 12.56 eV required for ionization. I believe these results are telling us something very significant: it is much easier for H+ to dissociate from the water molecule than for the water molecule to lose an electron. The energy of 4.8 eV is equivalent to light at 258.1 nm, which is close to the absorption peak of EZ water and consistent with the loss of H+ from EZ, the H+ accumulating at the interface of EZ with bulk water. Consequently, the activity of protons derived from water may be much more important for living systems than electrons and in the form of proton currents. **Delocalization of protons, by absorption of ambient photons at 1538.5 nm, increases the probability of proton transfer, i.e., proton conduction could take place much more readily and rapidly. It would be easy to check if the predicted 1538.5 nm does promote proton conduction; if so, it could have important clinical applications.**

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-25T05:26:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biophysics-of-the-microbiome-state-of-affairs-2015.16769/post-175617>

have a look see to where our collision course is: Where electrons and protons in mitochondria meet: [https://www.cell.com/abstract/S0092-8674(13)01521-3](https://www.cell.com/abstract/S0092-8674\(13\)01521-3)

When nuclear levels of NAD+ are low, a sequence of events occurs in the cell nucleus that ultimately results in a lack of adequate expression of mitochondrial DNA genes that are required for Complex I, III, and IV in mitochondrial electron transport.

In this scenario, the nuclear-encoded proteins required for mitochondrial electron transport chain are still expressed, but the proteins whose genes are encoded only by mitochondrial DNA are not expressed.

Thus, mitochondrial electron transport cannot occur and cells develop a metabolic picture which is “typical” in cancer, which is called the Warburg effect.

In this scenario, cells do not generate ATP from the mitochondria and instead, become dependent on cytoplasmic generation of ATP via aerobic glycolysis.

The primary driver of this “metabolic reprogramming” is the transcription factor, Hypoxia Inducing Factor 1 alpha (HIF-1a), which normally only activates Warburg-type metabolism when oxygen levels are low.

When nuclear NAD+ levels are low, HIF-1a is stabilized and is not degraded by VHL as would normally be the case. Here is the link to VHL and the ubiquination pathways. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1299287/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1299287/)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-25T05:26:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biophysics-of-the-microbiome-state-of-affairs-2015.16769/post-175618>

When all these things happen simultaneously, our cells are programmed to use aerobic glycolysis in the presence of normal oxygen.

HIF-1a inhibits a chain of events that ultimately results in a reduced expression of the mitochondrial transcription factor, TFAM, which normally migrates from the cell nucleus to the mitochondria to stimulate mitochondrial DNA replication. As a result, mitochondrial encoded genes are expressed. When HIF-1a inhibits TFAM, this does not occur.

When TFAM is suppressed by HIF-1a, no mitochondrial-encoded protein components of the electron transport chain are expressed.

ie: low NAD+
\=> HIF-1a present in higher amounts
\=> TFAM suppressed
\=> mitochondria cannot make ATP at all
\=> but these mitochondria still have the ECT mechanisms in place. Electrons start to flow backward
\=> lots of ROS is generated from this reversed electron flow
This enhanced charge destroys the pH gradient of the proton motive force in mitochondria to destroy proper redox signaling.

The charge on the inner mitochondrial membrane drops quickly. This then creates the ‘Primordial molecular signal of aging and disease’ we see in experiments

All of these events can be traced back to inadequate NAD+ in the nucleus.

Without adequate NAD+ in the nucleus, SIRT1 cannot function.

It turns out NAD+ is a mandatory co-factor for all of the proper functioning of sirtuin signaling
